heading: 1.1 Chapter 1: The values and principles of the UK
en: In this chapter you will learn about the fundamental rights and responsibilities which apply to everyone who lives in the UK, citizen or not. There is also some information about the format of the Life in the UK Test and the requirements for becoming a permanent resident.
zh: 在本章中，您将了解适用于所有在英国生活的人（无论是否为公民）的基本权利和责任。此外，本章还包含关于“英国生活”考试格式以及成为永久居留者要求的信息。

heading: 1.1 Chapter 1: The values and principles of the UK
en: The questions you get in the real test will be based on the whole book, including this introductory chapter so make sure that you are familiar with the details of the application process for permanent residence as well as the rights and responsibilities of UK residents.
zh: 正式考试中的问题将基于整本手册的内容，包括这一介绍性章节。因此，请务必熟悉永久居留申请流程的细节，以及英国居民的权利和责任。

heading: 1.1 Chapter 1: The values and principles of the UK
en: IN THIS CHAPTER THERE IS INFORMATION ABOUT :
zh: 本章包含以下信息：

heading: 1.1 Chapter 1: The values and principles of the UK
en:
• The fundamental principles of British life
• Responsibilities and freedoms of all UK residents
• Becoming a permanent resident
• Taking the Life in the UK test
• The testable sections of this book
zh:
• 英国生活的基本原则
• 所有英国居民的责任与自由
• 成为永久居留者
• 参加“英国生活”考试
• 本手册中可考核的部分

heading: 1.1 Chapter 1: The values and principles of the UK
en: Britain is a fantastic place to live: a modern, thriving society with a long and illustrious history. Our people have been at the heart of the world’s political, scientific, industrial and cultural development. We are proud of our record of welcoming new migrants who will add to the diversity and dynamism of our national life.
zh: 英国是一个绝佳的生活之地：一个拥有悠久辉煌历史的现代繁荣社会。我们的人民始终处于世界政治、科学、工业和文化发展的核心地位。我们自豪于欢迎新移民的传统，他们将为我国生活的多样性和活力增添色彩。

heading: 1.1 Chapter 1: The values and principles of the UK
en: Applying to become a permanent resident or citizen of the UK is an important decision and commitment. You will be agreeing to accept the responsibilities which go with permanent residence and to respect the laws, values and traditions of the UK. Good citizens are an asset to the UK. We welcome those seeking to make a positive contribution to our society.
zh: 申请成为英国永久居留者或公民是一项重要的决定和承诺。您将同意承担与永久居留相关的责任，并尊重英国的法律、价值观和传统。良好的公民是英国的宝贵财富。我们欢迎那些希望为社会做出积极贡献的人。

heading: 1.1 Chapter 1: The values and principles of the UK
en: Passing the Life in the UK test is part of demonstrating that you are ready to become a permanent migrant to the UK. This handbook is designed to support you in your preparation. It will help you to integrate into society and play a full role in your local community. It will also help ensure that you have a broad general knowledge of the culture, laws and history of the UK.
zh: 通过“英国生活”考试是证明您已准备好成为英国永久移民的一部分。本手册旨在支持您的备考。它将帮助您融入社会，并在当地社区中发挥积极作用。它还将确保您对英国的文化、法律和历史有广泛的一般性了解。

heading: 1.2 Becoming a permanent resident
en: To apply to become a permanent resident or citizen of the UK, you will need to:
zh: 要申请成为英国永久居留者或公民，您需要：

heading: 1.2 Becoming a permanent resident
en:
• speak and read English
• have a good understanding of life in the UK.
zh:
• 能够说和读英语
• 对英国生活有充分了解。

heading: 1.2 Becoming a permanent resident
en: There are currently (as of January 2013) two ways you can be tested on these requirements:
zh: 目前（截至2013年1月），您可以通过以下两种方式接受这些要求的考核：

heading: 1.2 Becoming a permanent resident
en:
• Take the Life in the UK test. The questions are written in a way that requires an understanding of the English language at English for Speakers of Other Languages (ESOL) Entry Level 3, so there is no need to take a separate English language test. People here on work visas, including those on Tier 1 and Tier 2 of the points based system, normally must pass the Life in the UK test to become permanent residents.
• Pass an ESOL course in English with Citizenship. You will need to take this course if your standard of English is below ESOL Entry Level 3. The course will help you to improve your English and learn more about life in the UK. At the end of the course you will take a test.
zh:
• 参加英国生活（Life in the UK）考试。试题的编写方式要求考生具备英语为其他语言（ESOL）入门级3级（Entry Level 3）的语言理解能力，因此无需单独参加英语语言测试。持工作签证来英的人员，包括基于积分制体系中第1类和第2类签证持有者，通常必须通过英国生活考试才能获得永久居留权。
• 通过带有公民身份内容的ESOL英语课程。如果您的英语水平低于ESOL入门级3级，则需要修读此课程。该课程将帮助您提高英语水平并了解更多关于英国生活的知识。课程结束后您将参加一项测试。

heading: 1.2 Becoming a permanent resident
en: Once you have passed one of these tests, you can make an application for permanent residence or British citizenship. The form that you have to complete and the evidence that you need to provide will depend on your personal circumstances. There is a fee for submitting an application, which is different for the various types of application. All of the forms and a list of fees can be found on the UK Border Agency website, www.ukba.homeoffice.gov.uk. From October 2013, the requirements will change. From that date, for settlement or permanent residence, you will need to:
zh: 一旦通过上述任一考试，您即可申请永久居留或英国公民身份。您需要填写的表格以及需要提供的证据将取决于您的个人情况。提交申请需缴纳费用，不同类型的申请费用各不相同。所有表格及费用清单均可在英国边境管理局（UK Border Agency）网站 www.ukba.homeoffice.gov.uk 上找到。从2013年10月起，相关要求将发生变化。自该日期起，申请定居或永久居留，您需要：

heading: 1.2 Becoming a permanent resident
en: • Pass the Life in the UK test
zh: • 通过英国生活（Life in the UK）考试

heading: 1.2 Becoming a permanent resident
en: AND
zh: 并且

heading: 1.2 Becoming a permanent resident
en: • Produce acceptable evidence of speaking and listening skills in English at B1 of the Common European Framework of Reference. This is equivalent to ESOL Entry Level 3.
zh: • 提供符合要求的英语听说能力证据，达到欧洲语言共同参考框架（Common European Framework of Reference）的B1级别。这相当于ESOL入门级3级。

heading: 1.2 Becoming a permanent resident
en: The requirements for citizenship applications may also change in the future. Further details will be published on the UK Border Agency website and you should check the information on that website for current requirements before applying for settlement or citizenship.
zh: 公民身份申请的要求未来可能还会发生变化。更多详情将在英国边境管理局网站上公布，您在申请定居或公民身份前，应查阅该网站以获取最新要求信息。

heading: 1.3 The values and principles of the UK
en: British society is founded on fundamental values and principles which all those living in the UK should respect and support. These values are reflected in the responsibilities, rights and privileges of being a British citizen or permanent resident of the UK. They are based on history and traditions and are protected by law, customs and expectations. There is no place in British society for extremism or intolerance.
zh: 英国社会建立在所有在英国生活的人都应尊重和支持的基本价值观和原则之上。这些价值观体现在作为英国公民或英国永久居民的责任、权利和特权中。它们基于历史和传统，并受到法律、习俗和期望的保护。在英国社会中，极端主义和不宽容没有立足之地。

heading: 1.3 The values and principles of the UK
en: The fundamental principles of British life include:
zh: 英国生活的基本原则包括：

heading: 1.3 The values and principles of the UK
en:
• Democracy
• The rule of law
• Individual liberty
• Tolerance of those with different faiths and beliefs
• Participation in community life.
zh:
• 民主
• 法治
• 个人自由
• 对不同信仰和信念者的宽容
• 参与社区生活。

heading: 1.3 The values and principles of the UK
en: As part of the citizenship ceremony, new citizens pledge to uphold these values. The pledge is:
zh: 作为入籍仪式的一部分，新公民承诺维护这些价值观。宣誓内容为：

heading: 1.3 The values and principles of the UK
en: ‘I will give my loyalty to the United Kingdom and respect its rights and freedoms. I will uphold its democratic values. I will observe its laws faithfully and fulfil my duties and obligations as a British citizen.’
zh: “我将效忠联合王国（英国）并尊重其权利与自由。我将维护其民主价值观。我将忠实遵守其法律，并履行作为英国公民的职责和义务。”

heading: 1.3 The values and principles of the UK
en: Folowing from the fundamental principles are responsibilities and freedoms which are shared by all those living in the UK and which we expect all residents to respect.
zh: 基于这些基本原则，产生了所有居住在英国的人共同享有的责任与自由，我们期望所有居民都能予以尊重。

heading: 1.3 The values and principles of the UK
en:
• If you wish to be a permanent resident or citizen of the UK, you should:
• respect and obey the law
• respect the rights of others, including their right to their own opinions
• treat others with fairness
• look after yourself and your family
• look after the area in which you live and the environment.
zh:
• 如果您希望成为联合王国（英国）的永久居民或公民，您应该：
• 尊重并遵守法律
• 尊重他人的权利，包括他们持有自己观点的权利
• 公平对待他人
• 照顾好自己和您的家人
• 爱护您居住的地区和环境。

heading: 1.3 The values and principles of the UK
en: In return, the UK offers:
zh: 作为回报，联合王国（英国）提供：

heading: 1.3 The values and principles of the UK
en:
• freedom of belief and religion
• freedom of speech
• freedom from unfair discrimination
• a right to a fair trial
• a right to join in the election of a government.
zh:
• 信仰和宗教自由
• 言论自由
• 免受不公平歧视的权利
• 获得公平审判的权利
• 参与选举政府的权利。

heading: 1.4 Taking the Life in the UK test
en: This handbook will help prepare you for taking the Life in the UK test. The test consists of 24 questions about important aspects of life in the UK. Questions are based on ALL parts of the handbook. The 24 questions will be different for each person taking the test at that test session.
zh: 本手册将帮助您准备参加“英国生活”（Life in the UK）考试。该考试包含24道关于英国生活重要方面的题目。题目基于本手册的所有部分。在每次考试场次中，每位考生的24道题目都会有所不同。

heading: 1.4 Taking the Life in the UK test
en: The Life in the UK test is usually taken in English, although special arrangements can be made if you wish to take it in Welsh or Scottish Gaelic.
zh: “英国生活”（Life in the UK）考试通常以英语进行，但如果您希望用威尔士语或苏格兰盖尔语参加考试，可以安排特殊事宜。

heading: 1.4 Taking the Life in the UK test
en: You can only take the test at a registered and approved Life in the UK test centre. There are about 60 test centres around the UK. You can only book your test online, at www.lifeintheuktest.gov.uk. You should not take your test at any other establishment as the UK Border Agency will only accept certificates from registered test centres. If you live on the Isle of Man or in the Channel Islands, there are different arrangements for taking the Life in the UK test.
zh: 您只能在注册并获批准的“英国生活”（Life in the UK）考试中心参加考试。全英约有60个考试中心。您只能在线预订考试，网址为 www.lifeintheuktest.gov.uk。请勿在其他机构参加考试，因为英国边境管理局仅接受来自注册考试中心的证书。如果您居住在马恩岛或海峡群岛，参加“英国生活”（Life in the UK）考试的安排有所不同。

heading: 1.4 Taking the Life in the UK test
en: When booking your test, read the instructions carefully. Make sure you enter your details correctly. You will need to take some identification and proof of your address with you to the test. If you don’t take these, you will not be able to take the test.
zh: 预约考试时，请仔细阅读说明。确保正确输入您的个人信息。参加考试的当天，您需要携带身份证件和地址证明。如果您未携带这些文件，将无法参加考试。

heading: 1.4 Taking the Life in the UK test
en: Everything that you will need to know to pass the Life in the UK test is included in this handbook. The questions will be based on the whole book, including this introduction, so make sure you study the entire book thoroughly. The handbook has been written to ensure that anyone who can read English at ESOL Entry Level 3 or above should have no difficulty with the language.
zh: 这本手册包含了通过“英国生活”考试所需了解的所有内容。考题将基于全书内容，包括本引言部分，因此请务必仔细研读整本书。本手册的编写旨在确保任何具备ESOL（英语为其他语言）3级或以上阅读能力的人都能轻松理解其中的语言内容。

heading: 1.4 Taking the Life in the UK test
en: The glossary at the back of the handbook contains some key words and phrases, which you might find helpful.
zh: 手册末尾的词汇表包含了一些关键术语和短语，供您参考使用。

heading: 1.4 Taking the Life in the UK test
en: The 'Check that you understand' boxes are for guidance. They will help you to identify particular things that you should understand. Just knowing the things highlighted in these boxes will not be enough to pass the test. You need to make sure that you understand everything in the book, so please read the information carefully.
zh: “检查您是否理解”板块旨在提供指导，帮助您识别需要特别掌握的内容。仅了解这些板块中强调的内容不足以通过考试。您需要确保理解书中的所有内容，因此请仔细阅读相关信息。

heading: 1.4 Taking the Life in the UK test
en: You can find out more information from the following places:
zh: 您可以通过以下渠道获取更多信息：

heading: 1.4 Taking the Life in the UK test
en:
• The UK Border Agency website (www.ukba.homeoffice.gov.uk) for more information about the application process and the forms you will need to complete
• The Life in the UK test website (www.lifeintheuktest.gov.uk) for more information about the test and how to book a place to take one
• Gov.uk (www.gov.uk) for information about ESOL courses and how to find one in your area.
zh:
• 英国边境管理局网站（www.ukba.homeoffice.gov.uk）：了解申请流程及所需填写表格的更多信息
• “英国生活”考试网站（www.lifeintheuktest.gov.uk）：了解考试详情及预约考位的更多信息
• Gov.uk（www.gov.uk）：了解ESOL课程信息及如何在您所在地区找到相关课程的更多信息。

heading: 1.4 Taking the Life in the UK test
en:
• The origin of the values underlying British society
• The fundamental principles of British life
• The responsibilities and freedoms which come with permanent residence
• The process of becoming a permanent resident or citizen.
zh:
• 英国社会价值观的起源
• 英国生活的基本原则
• 永久居留权所带来的责任与自由
• 成为永久居民或公民的过程。

heading: 2.1 Chapter 2: What is the UK?
en: In this chapter you will learn about the countries that make up the Union as well as the various phrases used to describe them. Although this chapter is very short you should make sure that you read and understand all facts presented. For instance it is very likely there will be questions about the differences between Great Britain, the British Isles and Britain, or what the Crown Dependencies are.
zh: 在本章中，您将学习组成联合王国（Union）的国家以及用于描述它们的各种短语。虽然本章非常短，但您应该确保阅读并理解其中呈现的所有事实。例如，极有可能会有关于大不列颠（Great Britain）、不列颠群岛（British Isles）与不列颠（Britain）之间的区别，或者什么是王室属地（Crown Dependencies）的问题。
en_simple: Learn about the countries making up the UK and terms describing them. Understand differences between Great Britain, British Isles, and Britain, and what Crown Dependencies are.

heading: 2.1 Chapter 2: What is the UK?
en: IN THIS CHAPTER THERE IS INFORMATION ABOUT :
zh: 本章包含以下方面的信息：

heading: 2.1 Chapter 2: What is the UK?
en: • The different countries that make up the UK
zh: • 组成联合王国（UK）的不同国家

heading: 2.2 What is the UK?
en: The UK is made up of England, Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland. The rest of Ireland is an independent country.
zh: 联合王国（UK）由英格兰（England）、苏格兰（Scotland）、威尔士（Wales）和北爱尔兰（Northern Ireland）组成。爱尔兰的其余部分是一个独立国家。

heading: 2.2 What is the UK?
en: The official name of the country is the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland. ‘Great Britain’ refers only to England, Scotland and Wales, not to Northern Ireland. The words ‘Britain’, Isles’ or ‘British’, however, are used in this book to refer to everyone in the UK.
zh: 该国的官方名称是大不列颠及北爱尔兰联合王国（United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland）。“大不列颠”（Great Britain）仅指英格兰（England）、苏格兰（Scotland）和威尔士（Wales），不包括北爱尔兰（Northern Ireland）。然而，本书中使用“不列颠”（Britain）、“群岛”（Isles）或“不列颠人／的”（British）这些词来指代联合王国（UK）中的每个人。
en_simple: Official name is United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland. 'Great Britain' includes England, Scotland, and Wales. 'Britain', 'Isles', or 'British' refer to all UK residents.

heading: 2.2 What is the UK?
en: There are also several islands which are closely linked with the UK but are not part of it: the Channel Islands and the Isle of Man. These have their own governments and are called ‘Crown dependencies’. There are also several British overseas territories in other parts of the world, such as St Helena and the Falkland Islands. They are also linked to the UK but are not a part of it.
zh: 还有几个岛屿与联合王国（UK）紧密相连，但不属于它：海峡群岛（Channel Islands）和马恩岛（Isle of Man）。这些岛屿拥有自己的政府，被称为“王室属地”（Crown dependencies）。在世界其他地方也有几个英国海外领地（British overseas territories），例如圣赫勒拿（St Helena）和福克兰群岛（Falkland Islands）。它们也与联合王国（UK）相连，但不属于它。
en_simple: Channel Islands and Isle of Man are self-governing 'Crown dependencies', linked to but not part of the UK. British overseas territories (e.g. St Helena and Falkland Islands) are also linked but not part of the UK.
exam_note_zh: 考点：海峡群岛和马恩岛是不属于联合王国的王室属地

heading: 2.2 What is the UK?
en: The UK is governed by the parliament sitting in Westminster. Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland also have parliaments or assemblies of their own, with devolved powers in defined areas.
zh: 联合王国（UK）由位于威斯敏斯特（Westminster）的议会（parliament）管理。苏格兰（Scotland）、威尔士（Wales）和北爱尔兰（Northern Ireland）也拥有各自的议会或大会，在特定领域拥有下放的权力（devolved powers）。

heading: 2.2 What is the UK?
en: • The different countries that make up the UK
zh: • 组成联合王国（UK）的不同国家

heading: 3.1 Chapter 3: A long and illustrious history
en: In this chapter you will learn about British history starting from the Stone Age. The beginning focuses on arriving populations which affected the language and religions of Britain. After that the focus is on the development of the Monarchy, church and Parliament. Major battles and significant periods are covered, including the civil war, the industrial revolution and the Empire. There is a lot of information about WWI and WWII and after WWII the chapter focuses on the governments of the UK and devolution in Northern Ireland, Wales and Scotland.
zh: 在本章中，您将了解从石器时代开始的英国历史。开篇重点介绍对英国语言和宗教产生影响的早期移民群体。随后，重点转向君主制、教会和议会的发展。本章涵盖了重大战役和重要时期，包括内战、工业革命和大英帝国。关于第一次世界大战（WWI）和第二次世界大战（WWII）的内容很多，二战后则聚焦于联合王国政府及北爱尔兰、威尔士和苏格兰的权力下放。

heading: 3.1 Chapter 3: A long and illustrious history
en: Make sure that you understand the relationship between the monarchy and Parliament, Protestants and Catholics and Britain and the colonies of the Empire. Think about how the right to vote developed. You should also make sure you know about each of the people described. You will need to know about the Acts of Parliament and other major events. You should make sure that you know all of the dates given in this chapter.
zh: 确保您理解君主制与议会、新教徒与天主教徒、英国与大英帝国殖民地之间的关系。思考选举权是如何发展的。您还应确保了解本章中描述的每一位人物。您需要熟悉议会法案及其他重大事件。请确保掌握本章提供的所有日期。

heading: 3.1 Chapter 3: A long and illustrious history
en: IN THIS CHAPTER THERE IS INFORMATION ABOUT :
zh: 本章包含以下信息：

heading: 3.1 Chapter 3: A long and illustrious history
en:
• The history of early Britain
• The medieval period
• The Tudor and Stuart monarchs of the UK
• The establishment of Parliament
• The unification of the United Kingdom
• The Enlightenment and Industrial Revolution
• The Victorian Age and the British Empire
• The First World War
• The Great Depression
• The Second World War
• Britain since 1945 and the welfare state
• Great British inventions and sporting figures
• Government since the Second World War
zh:
• 早期英国历史
• 中世纪时期
• 联合王国的都铎王朝和斯图亚特王朝君主
• 议会的建立
• 联合王国的统一
• 启蒙运动和工业革命
• 维多利亚时代和大英帝国
• 第一次世界大战
• 大萧条
• 第二次世界大战
• 1945年以来的英国及福利国家
• 伟大的英国发明和体育人物
• 二战以来的政府

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
en: Although the UK had won the war, the country was exhausted economically and the people wanted change. During the war, there had been significant reforms to the education system and people now looked for wider social reforms.
zh: 尽管联合王国赢得了战争，但国家在经济上已筋疲力尽，民众渴望变革。在战争期间，教育体系进行了重大改革，人们现在寻求更广泛的社会改革。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
en: In 1945 the British people elected a Labour government. The new Prime Minister was Clement Atlee, who promised to introduce the welfare state outlined in the Beveridge Report. In 1948, Aneurin (Nye) Bevan, the Minister for Health, led the establishment of the National Health Service (NHS), which guaranteed a minimum standard of health care for all, free at the point of use. A national system of benefits was also introduced to provide ‘social security’, so that the population would be protected from the ‘cradle to the grave’. The government took into public ownership (nationalised) the railways, coal mines and gas, water and electricity supplies.
zh: 1945年，英国人民选举工党政府上台。新任首相是克莱门特·艾德礼（Clement Atlee），他承诺实施《贝弗里奇报告》中概述的福利国家。1948年，卫生大臣安奈林·比万（Aneurin (Nye) Bevan）领导建立了国民医疗服务体系（NHS），该体系保证所有人享有最低标准的医疗保健，且在使用时免费。同时还引入了全国性的福利制度以提供“社会保障”，使民众受到“从摇篮到坟墓”的保护。政府将铁路、煤矿以及煤气、供水和电力供应收归国有（即实行国家化）。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
en: Another aspect of change was self-government for former colonies. In 1947, independence was granted to nine countries, including India, Pakistan and Ceylon (now Sri Lanka). Other colonies in Africa, the Caribbean and the Pacific achieved independence over the next 20 years.
zh: 变革的另一个方面是前殖民地的自治。1947年，包括印度、巴基斯坦和锡兰（现斯里兰卡）在内的九个国家获得独立。其他非洲、加勒比海和太平洋地区的殖民地也在随后的20年内陆续获得独立。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
en: The UK developed its own atomic bomb and joined the new North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO), an alliance of nations set up to resist the perceived threat of invasion by the Soviet Union and its allies.
zh: 联合王国研制了自己的原子弹，并加入了新成立的北大西洋公约组织（NATO），这是一个由各国组成的联盟，旨在抵御苏联及其盟友构成的所谓入侵威胁。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
en: Britain had a Conservative government from 1951 to 1964. The 1950s were a period of economic recovery after the war and increasing prosperity for working people. The Prime Minister of the day, Harold Macmillan, was famous for his ‘wind of change’ speech about decolonisation and independence for the countries of the Empire.
zh: 1951年至1964年间，英国由保守党政府执政。20世纪50年代是战后经济复苏的时期，工人的生活日益富裕。当时的首相哈罗德·麦克米伦（Harold Macmillan）因发表关于非殖民化和帝国各国独立的“变革之风”演讲而闻名。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
en: Clement Attlee was born in London in 1883. His father was a solicitor and, after studying at Oxford University, Attlee became a barrister. He gave this up to do social work in East London and eventually became a Labour MP. He was Winston Churchill’s Deputy Prime Minister in the wartime coalition government and became Prime Minister after the Labour Party won the 1945 election. He was Prime Minister from 1945 to 1951 and led the Labour Party for 20 years. Attlee’s government undertook the nationalisation of major industries (like coal and steel), created the National Health Service and implemented many of Beveridge’s plans for a stronger welfare state. Attlee also introduced measures to improve the conditions of workers.
zh: 克莱门特·艾德礼（Clement Attlee）于1883年出生于伦敦。他的父亲是一名事务律师，艾德礼在牛津大学学习后成为了一名出庭律师。他放弃律师职业，前往伦敦东区从事社会工作，最终成为一名下议院议员。他在战时联合政府中担任温斯顿·丘吉尔的副首相，并在工党赢得1945年大选后出任首相。他从1945年至1951年担任首相，并领导工党长达20年。艾德礼政府实施了主要行业（如煤炭和钢铁）的国有化，建立了国民医疗服务体系，并落实了贝弗里奇关于建立更强福利国家的许多计划。艾德礼还推出措施以改善工人的工作条件。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
en: William Beveridge (later Lord Beveridge) was a British economist and social reformer. He served briefly as a Liberal MP and was subsequently the leader of the Liberals in the House of Lords but is best known for the 1942 report Social Insurance and Allied Services (known as the Beveridge Report). The report was commissioned by the wartime government in 1941. It recommended that the government should find ways of fighting the five ‘Giant Evils’ of Want, Disease, Ignorance, Squalor and Idleness and provided the basis of the modern welfare state.
zh: 威廉·贝弗里奇（William Beveridge，后被封为贝弗里奇男爵）是一位英国经济学家和社会改革家。他曾短暂担任自由党下议院议员，随后成为上议院自由党领袖，但最为人所知的是他于1942年撰写的报告《社会保险及相关服务》（简称《贝弗里奇报告》）。该报告是战时政府于1941年委托编写的。报告建议政府寻找方法克服贫困、疾病、无知、肮脏和懒惰这五大“巨恶”，并为现代福利国家奠定了基础。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
en: Richard Austen Butler (later Lord Butler) was born in 1902. He became a Conservative MP in 1923 and held several positions before becoming responsible for education in 1941. In this role, he oversaw the introduction of the Education Act 1944 (often called ‘The Butler Act’), which introduced free secondary education in England and Wales. The education system has changed significantly since the Act was introduced, but the division between primary and secondary schools that it enforced still remains in most areas of Britain.
zh: 理查德·奥斯汀·巴特勒（Richard Austen Butler，后被封为巴特勒男爵）出生于1902年。他于1923年成为保守党下议院议员，在担任多个职位后，于1941年负责教育事务。在此任期内，他监督实施了《1944年教育法》（常被称为“巴特勒法案”），该法案在英格兰和威尔士引入了免费中等教育。自该法案实施以来，教育体系发生了巨大变化，但其规定的初等教育与中等教育之间的划分在英国大部分地区至今仍保留着。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
en: Dylan Thomas was a Welsh poet and writer. He often read and performed his work in public, including for the BBC. His most well-known works include the radio play Under Milk Wood, first performed after his death in 1954, and the poem Do Not Go Gentle into That Good Night, which he wrote for his dying father in 1952. He died at the age of 39 in New York. There are several memorials to him in his birthplace, Swansea, including a statue and the Dylan Thomas Centre.
zh: 迪伦·托马斯（Dylan Thomas）是一位威尔士诗人和作家。他经常在公共场合朗读和表演自己的作品，包括为英国广播公司（BBC）演出。他最著名的作品包括广播剧《牛奶树下》，该剧在他1954年去世后首次上演；以及诗歌《不要温和地走进那个良夜》，这是他在1952年为病危的父亲所写。他于39岁时在纽约去世。在他的出生地斯旺西有几处纪念他的场所，包括一座雕像和迪伦·托马斯中心。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
en: Rebuilding Britain after the Second World War was a huge task. There were labour shortages and the British government encouraged workers from Ireland and other parts of Europe to come to the UK and help with the reconstruction. In 1948, people from the West Indies were also invited to come and work.
zh: 第二次世界大战后重建英国是一项艰巨的任务。当时劳动力短缺，英国政府鼓励来自爱尔兰和其他欧洲地区的工人来到英国协助重建。1948年，还邀请来自西印度群岛的人们前来工作。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
en: During the 1950s, there was still a shortage of labour in the UK. Further immigration was therefore encouraged for economic reasons, and many industries advertised for workers from overseas. For example, centres were set up in the West Indies to recruit people to drive buses. Textile and engineering firms from the north of England and the Midlands sent agents to India and Pakistan to find workers. For about 25 years, people from the West Indies, India, Pakistan and (later) Bangladesh travelled to work and settle in Britain.
zh: 在20世纪50年代，英国仍然面临劳动力短缺。出于经济原因，政府进一步鼓励移民，许多行业在海外招聘工人。例如，在西印度群岛设立了中心以招募公交车司机。来自英格兰北部和米德兰兹地区的纺织和工程公司派遣代理人前往印度和巴基斯坦寻找工人。大约25年间，来自西印度群岛、印度、巴基斯坦以及（后来）孟加拉国的人们来到英国工作并定居。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
en: The decade of the 1960s was a period of significant social change. It was known as ‘the Swinging Sixties’. There was growth in British fashion, cinema and popular music. Two well-known pop music groups at the time were The Beatles and The Rolling Stones. People started to become better off and many bought cars and other consumer goods.
zh: 20世纪60年代是一个社会发生重大变化的时期，被称为“摇摆的六十年代”。英国的时尚、电影和流行音乐蓬勃发展。当时两个著名的流行音乐组合是披头士乐队（The Beatles）和滚石乐队（The Rolling Stones）。人们开始变得富裕起来，许多人购买了汽车和其他消费品。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
en: It was also a time when social laws were liberalised, for example in relation to divorce and to abortion in England, Wales and Scotland. The position of women in the workplace also improved. It was quite common at the time for employers to ask women to leave their jobs when they got married, but Parliament passed new laws giving women the right to equal pay and made it illegal for employers to discriminate against women because of their gender.
zh: 这也是社会法律放宽的时期，例如在英格兰、威尔士和苏格兰的离婚和堕胎方面。女性在 workplace 中的地位也有所改善。当时雇主要求女性结婚后离职是很常见的，但议会通过了新法律，赋予女性同工同酬的权利，并规定雇主因性别歧视女性为非法行为。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
en: The 1960s was also a time of technological progress. Britain and France developed the world’s only supersonic commercial airliner, Concorde. New styles of architecture, including high-rise buildings and the use of concrete and steel, became common.
zh: 20世纪60年代也是技术进步的时代。英国和法国开发了世界上唯一的超音速商用客机协和式飞机（Concorde）。新的建筑风格，包括高层建筑以及混凝土和钢材的使用，变得普遍。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
en: The number of people migrating from the West Indies, India, Pakistan and what is now Bangladesh fell in the late 1960s because the government passed new laws to restrict immigration to Britain. Immigrants were required to have a strong connection to Britain through birth or ancestry. Even so, during the early 1970s, Britain admitted 28,000 people of Indian origin who had been forced to leave Uganda.
zh: 20世纪60年代末，来自西印度群岛、印度、巴基斯坦和现在的孟加拉国地区的人口移民数量下降，因为政府通过了新法律来限制移民进入英国。移民必须通过出生或血统与英国建立紧密联系。尽管如此，在20世纪70年代初，英国接纳了28,000名被迫离开乌干达的印度裔人士。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
subheading: SOME GREAT BRITISH INVENTIONS OF THE 20TH CENTURY
en: Britain has given the world some wonderful inventions. Examples from the 20th century include:
zh: 英国为世界贡献了一些伟大的发明。20世纪的例子包括：

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
subheading: SOME GREAT BRITISH INVENTIONS OF THE 20TH CENTURY
en: The television was developed by Scotsman John Logie Baird (1888–1946) in the 1920s. In 1932 he made the first television broadcast between London and Glasgow.
zh: 电视机由苏格兰人约翰·洛吉·贝尔德（John Logie Baird，1888–1946）在20世纪20年代发明。1932年，他进行了伦敦与格拉斯哥之间的首次电视广播。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
subheading: SOME GREAT BRITISH INVENTIONS OF THE 20TH CENTURY
en: Radar was developed by Scotsman Sir Robert Watson-Watt (1892–1973), who proposed that enemy aircraft could be detected by radio waves. The first successful radar test took place in 1935.
zh: 雷达由苏格兰人罗伯特·沃森-瓦特爵士（Sir Robert Watson-Watt，1892–1973）研发，他提出可以利用无线电波探测敌方飞机。第一次成功的雷达测试于1935年进行。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
subheading: SOME GREAT BRITISH INVENTIONS OF THE 20TH CENTURY
en: Working with radar led Sir Bernard Lovell (1913–2012) to make new discoveries in astronomy. The radio telescope he built at Jodrell Bank in Cheshire was for many years the biggest in the world and continues to operate today.
zh: 与雷达相关的工作促使伯纳德·洛弗尔爵士（Sir Bernard Lovell，1913–2012）在天文学领域取得新发现。他在柴郡乔德雷尔银行（Jodrell Bank）建造射电望远镜，该望远镜曾多年保持世界最大纪录，至今仍在运行。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
subheading: SOME GREAT BRITISH INVENTIONS OF THE 20TH CENTURY
en: A Turing machine is a theoretical mathematical device invented by Alan Turing (1912–54), a British mathematician, in the 1930s. The theory was influential in the development of computer science and the modern-day computer.
zh: 图灵机是一种理论数学装置，由英国数学家艾伦·图灵（Alan Turing，1912–1954）在20世纪30年代发明。该理论对计算机科学和现代计算机的发展产生了深远影响。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
subheading: SOME GREAT BRITISH INVENTIONS OF THE 20TH CENTURY
en: The Scottish physician and researcher John MacLeod (1876–1935) was the co-discoverer of insulin, used to treat diabetes.
zh: 苏格兰医生兼研究员约翰·麦克劳德（John MacLeod，1876–1935）是胰岛素共同发现者之一，胰岛素用于治疗糖尿病。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
subheading: SOME GREAT BRITISH INVENTIONS OF THE 20TH CENTURY
en: The structure of the DNA molecule was discovered in 1953 through work at British universities in London and Cambridge. This discovery contributed to many scientific advances, particularly in medicine and fighting crime. Francis Crick (1916–2004), one of those awarded the Nobel Prize for this discovery, was British.
zh: DNA分子的结构于1953年通过伦敦和剑桥的英国大学的研究工作被发现。这一发现促成了许多科学进步，特别是在医学和打击犯罪方面。弗朗西斯·克里克（Francis Crick，1916–2004）因这一发现获得诺贝尔奖，他是英国人。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
subheading: SOME GREAT BRITISH INVENTIONS OF THE 20TH CENTURY
en: The jet engine was developed in Britain in the 1930s by Sir Frank Whittle (1907–96), a British Royal Air Force engineer Officer.
zh: 喷气式发动机由英国皇家空军军官弗兰克·惠特尔爵士（Sir Frank Whittle，1907–1996）在20世纪30年代于英国研发。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
subheading: SOME GREAT BRITISH INVENTIONS OF THE 20TH CENTURY
en: Sir Christopher Cockerell (1910–99), a British inventor, invented the hovercraft in the 1950s.
zh: 克里斯托弗·科克雷尔爵士（Sir Christopher Cockerell，1910–1999）是一位英国发明家，他在20世纪50年代发明了气垫船。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
subheading: SOME GREAT BRITISH INVENTIONS OF THE 20TH CENTURY
en: Britain and France developed Concorde , the world’s only supersonic passenger aircraft. It first flew in 1969 and began carrying passengers in 1976. Concorde was retired from service in 2003.
zh: 英国和法国共同研发了协和式飞机（Concorde），这是世界上唯一一款超音速客机。它于1969年首次试飞，并于1976年开始投入载客运营。协和式飞机于2003年退役。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
subheading: SOME GREAT BRITISH INVENTIONS OF THE 20TH CENTURY
en: The Harrier jump jet , an aircraft capable of taking off vertically, was also designed and developed in the UK.
zh: 鹞式垂直起降战斗机（Harrier jump jet）也是一种能够在英国设计并研发的、具备垂直起飞能力的飞机。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
subheading: SOME GREAT BRITISH INVENTIONS OF THE 20TH CENTURY
en: In the 1960s, James Goodfellow (1937–) invented the cash-dispensing ATM (automatic teller machine) or ‘cashpoint’. The first of these was put into use by Barclays Bank in Enfield, north London in 1967.
zh: 在20世纪60年代，詹姆斯·古德费洛（James Goodfellow，1937–）发明了自动取款机（ATM，automatic teller machine），即“现金提取机”（cashpoint）。第一台这样的机器于1967年由巴克莱银行（Barclays Bank）在伦敦北部的恩菲尔德（Enfield）投入使用。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
subheading: SOME GREAT BRITISH INVENTIONS OF THE 20TH CENTURY
en: IVF (in-vitro fertilisation) therapy for the treatment of infertility was pioneered in Britain by physiologist Sir Robert Edwards (1925–) and gynaecologist Patrick Steptoe (1913–88). The world’s first ‘test-tube baby’ was born in Oldham, Lancashire in 1978.
zh: 体外受精（IVF，in-vitro fertilisation）治疗不孕症的技术由英国生理学家罗伯特·爱德华兹爵士（Sir Robert Edwards，1925–）和妇科医生帕特里克·斯特普托（Patrick Steptoe，1913–88）在英国率先开展。世界上第一个“试管婴儿”于1978年在兰开夏郡的奥尔德姆（Oldham）出生。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
subheading: SOME GREAT BRITISH INVENTIONS OF THE 20TH CENTURY
en: In 1996, two British scientists, Sir Ian Wilmot (1944–) and Keith Campbell (1954–2012), led a team which was the first to succeed in cloning a mammal, Dolly the sheep. This has led to further research into the possible use of cloning to preserve endangered species and for medical purposes.
zh: 1996年，两位英国科学家伊恩·威尔穆特爵士（Sir Ian Wilmot，1944–）和基思·坎贝尔（Keith Campbell，1954–2012）领导的研究团队首次成功克隆出哺乳动物——多利羊（Dolly the sheep）。这一成果推动了进一步研究，探索利用克隆技术保护濒危物种及用于医疗目的的可能性。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
subheading: SOME GREAT BRITISH INVENTIONS OF THE 20TH CENTURY
en: Sir Peter Mansfield (1933–), a British scientist, is the co-inventor of the MRI (magnetic resonance imaging) scanner . This enables doctors and researchers to obtain exact and non-invasive images of human internal organs and has revolutionised diagnostic medicine.
zh: 英国科学家彼得·曼斯菲尔德爵士（Sir Peter Mansfield，1933–）是磁共振成像扫描仪（MRI，magnetic resonance imaging scanner）的共同发明人。这使得医生和研究人员能够获得人体内部器官精确且无创的图像，从而彻底改变了诊断医学。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
subheading: SOME GREAT BRITISH INVENTIONS OF THE 20TH CENTURY
en: The inventor of the World Wide Web , Sir Tim Berners-Lee (1955–), is British. Information was successfully transferred via the web for the first time on 25 December 1990.
zh: 万维网（World Wide Web）的发明人蒂姆·伯纳斯-李爵士（Sir Tim Berners-Lee，1955–）是英国人。1990年12月25日，信息首次通过万维网成功传输。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
subheading: Problems in the economy in the 1970s
en: In the late 1970s, the post-war economic boom came to an end. Prices of goods and raw materials began to rise sharply and the exchange rate between the pound and other currencies was unstable. This caused problems with the ‘balance of payments’: imports of goods were valued at more than the price paid for exports.
zh: 在20世纪70年代末，战后的经济繁荣期结束。商品和原材料的价格开始急剧上涨，英镑与其他货币之间的汇率不稳定。这导致了“国际收支平衡”（balance of payments）问题：进口商品的估值高于出口商品的价格。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
subheading: Problems in the economy in the 1970s
en: Many industries and services were affected by strikes and this caused problems between the trade unions and the government. People began to argue that the unions were too powerful and that their activities were harming the UK.
zh: 许多行业和服务业受到罢工的影响，这导致了工会与政府之间的问题。人们开始认为工会权力过大，其活动正在损害英国。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
subheading: Problems in the economy in the 1970s
en: The 1970s were also a time of serious unrest in Northern Ireland. In 1972, the Northern Ireland Parliament was suspended and Northern Ireland was directly ruled by the UK government. Some 3,000 people lost their lives in the decades after 1969 in the violence in Northern Ireland.
zh: 20世纪70年代也是北爱尔兰严重动荡的时期。1972年，北爱尔兰议会暂停运作，北爱尔兰由英国政府直接统治。在1969年后的几十年里，约有3,000人在北爱尔兰的暴力冲突中丧生。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
en: Born in Manchester, Mary Peters moved to Northern Ireland as a child. She was a talented athlete who won an Olympic gold medal in the pentathlon in 1972. After this, she raised money for local athletics and became the team manager for the women’s British Olympic team. She continues to promote sport and tourism in Northern Ireland and was made a Dame of the British Empire in 2000 in recognition of her work.
zh: 玛丽·彼得斯（Mary Peters）出生于曼彻斯特，童年时移居北爱尔兰。她是一位才华横溢的运动员，在1972年奥运会上赢得了五项全能金牌。此后，她为当地田径运动筹集资金，并担任英国女子奥运队的领队。她继续促进北爱尔兰的体育和旅游业发展，并于2000年被授予大英帝国女爵士（Dame of the British Empire）称号，以表彰她的工作。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
en: West Germany, France, Belgium, Italy, Luxembourg and the Netherlands formed the European Economic Community (EEC) in 1957. At first the UK did not wish to join the EEC but it eventually did so in 1973. The UK is a full member of the European Union but does not use the Euro currency.
zh: 西德、法国、比利时、意大利、卢森堡和荷兰于1957年成立了欧洲经济共同体（EEC）。起初英国不愿加入欧洲经济共同体，但最终在1973年加入。英国是欧盟的正式成员，但不使用欧元作为货币。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
en: Margaret Thatcher was the daughter of a grocer from Grantham in Lincolnshire. She trained as a chemist and lawyer. She was elected as a Conservative MP in 1959 and became a cabinet minister in 1970 as the Secretary of State for Education and Science. In 1975 she was elected as Leader of the Conservative Party and so became Leader of the Opposition.
zh: 玛格丽特·撒切尔（Margaret Thatcher）是林肯郡格兰瑟姆一位杂货商的女儿。她接受过化学家和律师的培训。1959年，她当选为保守党下议院议员，并于1970年成为内阁大臣，担任教育及科学大臣。1975年，她当选为保守党领袖，从而成为反对党领袖。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
en: Following the Conservative victory in the General Election in 1979, Margaret Thatcher became the first woman Prime Minister of the UK. She was the longest- serving Prime Minister of the 20th century, remaining in Office until 1990. During her premiership, there were a number of important economic reforms within the UK. She worked closely with the United States President, Ronald Reagan, and was one of the first Western leaders to recognise and welcome the changes in the leadership of the Soviet Union which eventually led to the end of the Cold War.
zh: 在1979年大选中保守党获胜后，玛格丽特·撒切尔成为英国首位女性首相。她是20世纪任期最长的首相，任职至1990年。在她的首相任期内，英国实施了一系列重要的经济改革。她与美国总统罗纳德·里根（Ronald Reagan）密切合作，是首批承认并欢迎苏联领导层变革的西方领导人之一，这些变革最终导致了冷战的结束。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
en: Margaret Thatcher, Britain’s first woman Prime Minister, led the Conservative government from 1979 to 1990. The government made structural changes to the economy through the privatisation of nationalised industries and imposed legal controls on trade union powers. Deregulation saw a great increase in the role of the City of London as an international centre for investments, insurance and other financial services. Traditional industries, such as shipbuilding and coal mining, declined. In 1982, Argentina invaded the Falkland Islands, a British overseas territory in the South Atlantic. A naval taskforce was sent from the UK and military action led to the recovery of the islands. John Major was Prime Minister after Mrs Thatcher, and helped establish the Northern Ireland peace process.
zh: 玛格丽特·撒切尔作为英国首位女性首相，于1979年至1990年领导保守党政府。政府通过国有企业私有化对经济进行了结构性调整，并对工会权力实施了法律限制。放松管制使伦敦金融城作为国际投资、保险和其他金融中心的作用大大增加。造船和煤炭开采等传统行业则走向衰落。1982年，阿根廷入侵了位于南大西洋的英国海外领土福克兰群岛。英国派遣海军特遣队前往，军事行动最终收复了该群岛。约翰·梅杰（John Major）在撒切尔夫人之后担任首相，并帮助建立了北爱尔兰和平进程。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
en: Roald Dahl was born in Wales to Norwegian parents. He served in the Royal Air Force during the Second World War. It was during the 1940s that he began to publish books and short stories. He is most well known for his children’s books, although he also wrote for adults. His best-known works include Charlie and the Chocolate Factory and George’s Marvellous Medicine. Several of his books have been made into films.
zh: 罗尔德·达尔（Roald Dahl）出生于威尔士，父母是挪威人。他在第二次世界大战期间服役于皇家空军。20世纪40年代，他开始出版书籍和短篇小说。他最为人熟知的是其儿童文学作品，尽管他也为成人写作。他最著名的作品包括《查理和巧克力工厂》和《乔治的奇妙魔法药》。他的多部作品被改编成电影。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
en: In 1997 the Labour Party led by Tony Blair was elected. The Blair government introduced a Scottish Parliament and a Welsh Assembly. The Scottish Parliament has substantial powers to legislate. The Welsh Assembly was given fewer legislative powers but considerable control over public services. In Northern Ireland, the Blair government was able to build on the peace process, resulting in the Good Friday Agreement signed in 1998. The Northern Ireland Assembly was elected in 1999 but suspended in 2002. It was not reinstated until 2007. Most paramilitary groups in Northern Ireland have decommissioned their arms and are inactive. Gordon Brown took over as Prime Minister in 2007.
zh: 1997年，由托尼·布莱尔（Tony Blair）领导的工党当选执政。布莱尔政府设立了苏格兰议会和威尔士议会。苏格兰议会拥有广泛的立法权。威尔士议会的立法权较少，但在公共服务方面拥有相当大的控制权。在北爱尔兰，布莱尔政府得以在和平进程的基础上推进工作，促成了1998年签署的《受难日协议》。北爱尔兰议会于1999年选举产生，但于2002年被暂停运作，直到2007年才恢复。北爱尔兰的大多数准军事组织已解除武装并停止活动。戈登·布朗（Gordon Brown）于2007年接任首相。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
en: Throughout the 1990s, Britain played a leading role in coalition forces involved in the liberation of Kuwait, following the Iraqi invasion in 1990, and the conflict in the Former Republic of Yugoslavia. Since 2000, British armed forces have been engaged in the global fight against international terrorism and against the proliferation of weapons of mass destruction, including operations in Afghanistan and Iraq. British combat troops left Iraq in 2009. The UK now operates in Afghanistan as part of the United Nations (UN) mandated 50-nation International Security Assistance Force
zh: 在整个20世纪90年代，英国在1990年伊拉克入侵科威特后的解放科威特联军以及前南斯拉夫冲突中发挥了领导作用。自2000年以来，英国武装力量参与了全球打击国际恐怖主义和防止大规模杀伤性武器扩散的斗争，包括在阿富汗和伊拉克的行动。英国作战部队于2009年撤离伊拉克。如今，英国作为联合国（United Nations）授权的50国国际安全援助部队（International Security Assistance Force）的一部分，并在阿富汗政府的邀请下在阿富汗开展行动。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
en: (ISAF) coalition and at the invitation of the Afghan government. ISAF is working to ensure that Afghan territory can never again be used as a safe haven for international terrorism, where groups such as Al Qa’ida could plan attacks on the international community. As part of this, ISAF is building up the Afghan National Security Forces and is helping to create a secure environment in which governance and development can be extended. International forces are gradually handing over responsibility for security to the Afghans, who will have full security responsibility in all provinces by the end of 2014.
zh: 国际安全援助部队致力于确保阿富汗领土不再被用作国际恐怖主义的避风港，例如基地组织（Al Qa’ida）等团体在此策划针对国际社会的袭击。为此，国际安全援助部队正在加强阿富汗国家安全部队建设，并帮助创造一个安全和稳定的环境，以便治理和发展得以延伸。国际部队正逐步将安全责任移交给阿富汗人，到2014年底，阿富汗人将在所有省份承担全部安全责任。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
en: In May 2010, and for the first time in the UK since February 1974, no political party won an overall majority in the General Election. The Conservative and Liberal Democrat parties formed a coalition and the leader of the Conservative Party, David Cameron, became Prime Minister.
zh: 2010年5月，英国自1974年2月以来首次在大选中没有任何政党赢得绝对多数。保守党和自由民主党组成联合政府，保守党领袖戴维·卡梅伦（David Cameron）出任首相。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
en: The Conservative Government, which governed the United Kingdom from 2015 to 2024, saw multiple prime ministers, including David Cameron, Theresa May, Boris Johnson, Liz Truss, and Rishi Sunak. During this period, significant events included the 2016 EU membership referendum (Brexit), leadership changes, and handling of the COVID-19 pandemic. Despite reshuffling his cabinet and local election losses, Rishi Sunak has called for a general election on July 4, 2024. The Conservative Party’s popularity declined during this time, while Labour, the Liberal Democrats, and the Greens made substantial gains.
zh: 保守党政府于2015年至2024年执政联合王国（英国），期间经历了多位首相，包括戴维·卡梅伦、特雷莎·梅、鲍里斯·约翰逊、利兹·特拉斯和里希·苏纳克。在此期间，重大事件包括2016年欧盟成员资格公投（脱欧）、领导层更迭以及对COVID-19大流行的应对。尽管里希·苏纳克进行了内阁重组并在地方选举中失利，但他仍宣布于2024年7月4日举行大选。保守党在此期间人气下滑，而工党、自由民主党及绿党则取得了显著进展。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
en: The Labour party, lead by Keir Starmer, won the majority in the 2024 general election and formed the government.
zh: 由基尔·斯塔默领导的工党在2024年大选中赢得多数席位并组建政府。

heading: 3.2 Britain since 1945
en:
• The establishment of the welfare state
• How life in Britain changed in the 1960s and 1970s
• British inventions of the 20th century (you do not need to remember dates of births and deaths)
• Events since 1979
zh:
• 福利国家的建立
• 1960年代和1970年代英国生活的变化
• 20世纪的英国发明（无需记住生卒年份）
• 1979年以来的事件

heading: 3.3 A global power
subheading: Constitutional monarchy - The Bill of Rights
en: At the coronation of William and Mary, a Declaration of Rights was read. This confirmed that the king would no longer be able to raise taxes or administer justice without agreement from Parliament. The balance of power between monarch and Parliament had now permanently changed. The Bill of Rights, 1689, confirmed the rights of Parliament and the limits of the king’s power. Parliament took control of who could be monarch and declared that the king or queen must be a Protestant. A new Parliament had to be elected at least every three years (later this became seven years and now it is five years). Every year the monarch had to ask Parliament to renew funding for the army and the navy.
zh: 在威廉和玛丽加冕典礼上，宣读了一份《权利宣言》。这确认国王将不再能够在未经议会同意的情况下征税或行使司法权。君主与议会之间的权力平衡从此发生了永久性的改变。1689年的《权利法案》确认了议会的权利以及国王权力的界限。议会掌握了决定谁可以成为君主的权力，并宣布国王或女王必须是新教徒。必须至少每三年选举一次新议会（后来改为七年，现在是五年）。君主每年都必须向议会申请更新军队和海军的经费。

heading: 3.3 A global power
subheading: Constitutional monarchy - The Bill of Rights
en: These changes meant that, to be able to govern effectively, the monarch needed to have advisers, or ministers, who would be able to ensure a majority of votes in the House of Commons and the House of Lords. There were two main groups in Parliament, known as the Whigs and the Tories. (The modern Conservative Party is still sometimes referred to as the Tories.) This was the beginning of party politics.
zh: 这些变化意味着，为了能够有效治理，君主需要拥有顾问或大臣，他们能够确保在下议院和上议院获得多数选票。议会中有两个主要派系，被称为辉格党和托利党。（现代保守党有时仍被称为托利党。）这是政党政治的开端。

heading: 3.3 A global power
subheading: Constitutional monarchy - The Bill of Rights
en: This was also an important time for the development of a free press (newspapers and other publications which are not controlled by the government). From 1695, newspapers were allowed to operate without a government licence. Increasing numbers of newspapers began to be published.
zh: 这也是自由新闻业（不受政府控制的报纸和其他出版物）发展的重要时期。从1695年起，报纸获准在无需政府许可证的情况下运营。越来越多的报纸开始出版。

heading: 3.3 A global power
subheading: Constitutional monarchy - The Bill of Rights
en: The laws passed after the Glorious Revolution are the beginning of what is called ‘constitutional monarchy’. The monarch remained very important but was no longer able to insist on particular policies or actions if Parliament did not agree. After William III, the ministers gradually became more important than the monarch but this was not a democracy in the modern sense. The number of people who had the right to vote for members of Parliament was still very small. Only men who owned property of a certain value were able to vote. No women at all had the vote. Some constituencies were controlled by a single wealthy family. These were called ‘pocket boroughs’. Other constituencies had hardly any voters and were called ‘rotten boroughs’.
zh: 光荣革命后通过的法律标志着所谓‘君主立宪制’的开始。君主仍然非常重要，但如果议会不同意，便不能再坚持特定的政策或行动。威廉三世之后，大臣们逐渐变得比君主更重要，但这并非现代意义上的民主。拥有选举下议院议员权利的人数仍然非常少。只有拥有一定价值财产的男性才能投票。没有任何女性拥有投票权。一些选区由单一富裕家族控制，这些被称为‘口袋选区’。其他选区几乎没有选民，被称为‘腐败选区’。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: This was a time when many people left Britain and Ireland to settle in new colonies in America and elsewhere, but others came to live in Britain. The first Jews to come to Britain since the Middle Ages settled in London in 1656. Between 1680 and 1720 many refugees called Huguenots came from France. They were Protestants and had been persecuted for their religion. Many were educated and skilled and worked as scientists, in banking, or in weaving or other crafts.
zh: 这是一个许多人离开不列颠和爱尔兰前往美洲及其他地方的新殖民地定居的时期，但也有其他人来到不列颠生活。自中世纪以来首批来到不列颠的犹太人于1656年在伦敦定居。在1680年至1720年间，许多被称为胡格诺派的难民从法国来到不列颠。他们是新教徒，因宗教信仰而受到迫害。许多人受过教育且拥有技能，从事科学、银行、纺织或其他手工艺工作。

heading: 3.3 A global power
subheading: The Act of Treaty or Union in Scotland
en: William and Mary’s successor, Queen Anne, had no surviving children. This created uncertainty over the succession in England, Wales and Ireland and in Scotland. The Act of Union, known as the Treaty of Union in Scotland, was therefore agreed in 1707, creating the Kingdom of Great Britain. Although Scotland was no longer an independent country, it kept its own legal and education systems and Presbyterian Church.
zh: 威廉和玛丽（William and Mary）的继任者安妮女王（Queen Anne）没有幸存的子嗣。这导致了英格兰、威尔士、爱尔兰以及苏格兰的王位继承出现不确定性。因此，1707年达成了《联合法案》（Act of Union），在苏格兰被称为《联盟条约》（Treaty of Union），从而建立了大不列颠王国（Kingdom of Great Britain）。虽然苏格兰不再是一个独立的国家，但它保留了自己的法律和教育体系以及长老会（Presbyterian Church）教会。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: When Queen Anne died in 1714, Parliament chose a German, George I, to be the next king, because he was Anne’s nearest Protestant relative. An attempt by Scottish Jacobites to put James II’s son on the throne instead was quickly defeated. George I did not speak very good English and this increased his need to rely on his ministers. The most important minister in Parliament became known as the Prime Minister. The first man to be called this was Sir Robert Walpole, who was Prime Minister from 1721 to 1742.
zh: 1714年安妮女王去世后，议会选择了一位德国人乔治一世（George I）作为下一任国王，因为他是安妮最近的新教亲属。苏格兰雅各布派（Jacobites）试图将詹姆斯二世（James II）的儿子推上王位的尝试很快被击败。乔治一世的英语说得不太好，这增加了他依赖大臣的需要。议会中最重要的大臣被称为首相（Prime Minister）。第一位被称为此职的人是罗伯特·沃波尔爵士（Sir Robert Walpole），他于1721年至1742年担任首相。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: In 1745 there was another attempt to put a Stuart king back on the throne in place of George I’s son, George II. Charles Edward Stuart (Bonnie Prince Charlie), the grandson of James II, landed in Scotland. He was supported by clansmen from the Scottish highlands and raised an army. Charles initially had some successes but was defeated by George II’s army at the Battle of Culloden in 1746. Charles escaped back to Europe.
zh: 1745年，人们再次尝试将斯图亚特王朝（Stuart）的国王推上王位，取代乔治一世（George I）的儿子乔治二世（George II）。詹姆斯二世的孙子查尔斯·爱德华·斯图亚特（Charles Edward Stuart，即“英俊王子查理” Bonnie Prince Charlie）在苏格兰登陆。他得到了苏格兰高地氏族成员的支持并组建了一支军队。查尔斯起初取得了一些成功，但在1746年的卡洛登战役（Battle of Culloden）中被乔治二世的军队击败。查尔斯逃回了欧洲。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: The clans lost a lot of their power and influence after Culloden. Chieftains became landlords if they had the favour of the English king, and clansmen became tenants who had to pay for the land they used.
zh: 卡洛登战役后，氏族失去了大量的权力和影响力。如果得到英国国王的青睐，氏族首领变成了地主，而氏族成员则变成了必须为他们使用的土地付费的佃农。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: A process began which became known as the ‘Highland Clearances’. Many Scottish landlords destroyed individual small farms (known as ‘crofts’) to make space for large flocks of sheep and cattle. Evictions became very common in the early 19th century. Many Scottish people left for North America at this time.
zh: 一个被称为“高地清理”的过程开始了。许多苏格兰地主摧毁了 individual small farms（known as ‘crofts’）以腾出空间饲养大量的羊群和牛群。在19世纪初，驱逐佃农变得非常普遍。当时许多苏格兰人前往北美。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: Known in Scotland as ‘The Bard’, Robert Burns was a Scottish poet. He wrote in the Scots language, English with some Scottish words, and standard English. He also revised a lot of traditional folk songs by changing or adding lyrics. Burns’ best-known work is probably the song Auld Lang Syne , which is sung by people in the UK and other countries when they are celebrating the New Year (or Hogmanay as it is called in Scotland).
zh: 罗伯特·伯恩斯（Robert Burns）被称为苏格兰的“诗人”，是一位苏格兰诗人。他用苏格兰语（一种带有部分苏格兰词汇的英语）和标准英语写作。他还通过修改或添加歌词修订了许多传统民谣。伯恩斯最著名的作品可能是歌曲《友谊地久天长》（Auld Lang Syne），人们在英国和其他国家庆祝新年时都会唱这首歌（在苏格兰称为Hogmanay）。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: During the 18th century, new ideas about politics, philosophy and science were developed. This is often called ‘the Enlightenment’. Many of the great thinkers of the Enlightenment were Scottish. Adam Smith developed ideas about economics which are still referred to today. David Hume’s ideas about human nature continue to influence philosophers. Scientific discoveries, such as James Watt’s work on steam power, helped the progress of the Industrial Revolution. One of the most important principles of the Enlightenment was that everyone should have the right to their own political and religious beliefs and that the state should not try to dictate to them. This continues to be an important principle in the UK today.
zh: 在18世纪，关于政治、哲学和科学的新思想得到了发展。这通常被称为“启蒙运动”。许多伟大的启蒙思想家都是苏格兰人。亚当·斯密（Adam Smith）提出了至今仍被引用的经济学思想。大卫·休谟（David Hume）关于人性的思想继续影响着哲学家。科学发现，如詹姆斯·瓦特（James Watt）对蒸汽动力的研究，帮助推动了工业革命的发展。启蒙运动最重要的原则之一是每个人都应有权利拥有自己的政治和宗教信仰，国家不应试图对此进行干涉。这一原则至今仍是英国的重要原则。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: Before the 18th century, agriculture was the biggest source of employment in Britain. There were many cottage industries, where people worked from home to produce goods such as cloth and lace.
zh: 在18世纪之前，农业是英国最大的就业来源。有许多家庭手工业，人们在家工作生产布料和蕾丝等商品。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: The Industrial Revolution was the rapid development of industry in Britain in the 18th and 19th centuries. Britain was the first country to industrialise on a large scale. It happened because of the development of machinery and the use of steam power. Agriculture and the manufacturing of goods became mechanised. This made things more efficient and increased production. Coal and other raw materials were needed to power the new factories. Many people moved from the countryside and started working in the mining and manufacturing industries.
zh: 工业革命是18世纪和19世纪英国工业的快速发展。英国是第一个大规模实现工业化的国家。这是由于机械的发展和蒸汽动力的使用而发生的。农业和商品制造变得机械化。这使得生产更加高效并增加了产量。煤炭和其他原材料被用来为新的工厂提供动力。许多人从农村搬出，开始在采矿和制造业工作。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: The development of the Bessemer process for the mass production of steel led to the development of the shipbuilding industry and the railways. Manufacturing jobs became the main source of employment in Britain.
zh: 贝塞麦转炉炼钢法（Bessemer process）的大规模生产发展导致了造船业和铁路的发展。制造业工作成为英国主要的就业来源。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: Born in 1732, Arkwright originally trained and worked as a barber. He was able to dye hair and make wigs. When wigs became less popular, he started to work in textiles. He improved the original carding machine. Carding is the process of preparing fibres for spinning into yarn and fabric. He also developed horse-driven spinning mills that used only one machine. This increased the efficiency of production. Later, he used the steam engine to power machinery. Arkwright is particularly remembered for the efficient and profitable way that he ran his factories.
zh: 理查德·阿克莱特（Richard Arkwright）出生于1732年，最初接受训练并从事理发师工作。他能够染发和制作假发。当假发变得不那么流行时，他开始从事纺织业。他改进了原始的梳棉机。梳棉是将纤维准备成纱线和织物的过程。他还开发了仅使用一台机器的马驱动纺纱厂。这提高了生产效率。后来，他使用蒸汽机为机械提供动力。阿克莱特因其高效且有利可图的工厂经营方式而特别为人铭记。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: Better transport links were needed to transport raw materials and manufactured goods. Canals were built to link the factories to towns and cities and to the ports, particularly in the new industrial areas in the middle and north of England.
zh: 需要更好的交通联系来运输原材料和制成品。运河被建造以将工厂与城镇、城市以及港口连接起来，特别是在英格兰中部和北部的新工业区。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: Working conditions during the Industrial Revolution were very poor. There were no laws to protect employees, who were often forced to work long hours in dangerous situations. Children also worked and were treated in the same way as adults. Sometimes they were treated even more harshly.
zh: 工业革命期间的工作条件非常恶劣。当时没有保护员工的法律，员工经常被迫在危险的环境中长时间工作。儿童也从事劳动，并且受到与成年人相同的对待。有时他们受到的待遇甚至更加苛刻。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: This was also a time of increased colonisation overseas. Captain James Cook mapped the coast of Australia and a few colonies were established there. Britain gained control over Canada, and the East India Company, originally set up to trade, gained control of large parts of India. Colonies began to be established in southern Africa.
zh: 这也是海外殖民扩张加剧的时期。詹姆斯·库克船长（Captain James Cook）绘制了澳大利亚海岸地图，并在当地建立了一些殖民地。英国获得了对加拿大的控制权，而最初为贸易目的设立的东印度公司（East India Company）则控制了印度的大片地区。在南非也开始建立殖民地。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: Britain traded all over the world and began to import more goods. Sugar and tobacco came from North America and the West Indies; textiles, tea and spices came from India and the area that is today called Indonesia. Trading and settlements overseas sometimes brought Britain into conflict with other countries, particularly France, which was expanding and trading in a similar way in many of the same areas of the world.
zh: 英国在全世界范围内进行贸易，并开始进口更多商品。糖和烟草来自北美和西印度群岛；纺织品、茶和香料来自印度以及今天被称为印度尼西亚的地区。海外贸易和定居有时使英国与其他国家发生冲突，特别是法国，后者也在世界许多相同地区以类似的方式扩张和贸易。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: Mahomet was born in 1759 and grew up in the Bengal region of India. He served in the Bengal army and came to Britain in 1782. He then moved to Ireland and eloped with an Irish girl called Jane Daly in 1786, returning to England at the turn of the century. In 1810 he opened the Hindoostane Coffee House in George Street, London. It was the first curry house to open in Britain. Mahomet and his wife also introduced ‘shampooing’, the Indian art of head massage, to Britain.
zh: 穆罕默德（Mahomet）出生于1759年，在印度的孟加拉地区长大。他曾在孟加拉军队服役，并于1782年来到英国。随后他移居爱尔兰，并于1786年与一位名叫简·达利（Jane Daly）的爱尔兰女孩私奔，于世纪之交返回英格兰。1810年，他在伦敦乔治街开设了印度咖啡屋（Hindoostane Coffee House）。这是英国第一家咖喱餐厅。穆罕默德和他的妻子还将“洗头按摩”（shampooing，即印度头部按摩艺术）引入英国。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: This commercial expansion and prosperity was sustained in part by the booming slave trade. While slavery was illegal within Britain itself, by the 18th century it was a fully established overseas industry, dominated by Britain and the American colonies. Slaves came primarily from West Africa. Travelling on British ships in horrible conditions, they were taken to America and the Caribbean, where they were made to work on tobacco and sugar plantations. The living and working conditions for slaves were very bad. Many slaves tried to escape and others revolted against their owners in protest at their terrible treatment.
zh: 这种商业扩张和繁荣部分得益于蓬勃发展的奴隶贸易。虽然奴隶制在英国本土是非法的，但到18世纪，它已成为一个完全确立的海外产业，由英国和美国殖民地主导。奴隶主要来自西非。他们在恶劣的条件下乘坐英国船只前往美洲和加勒比地区，被迫在烟草和甘蔗种植园工作。奴隶的生活和工作条件非常糟糕。许多奴隶试图逃跑，其他人则反抗主人，抗议他们遭受的可怕待遇。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: There were, however, people in Britain who opposed the slave trade. The first formal anti-slavery groups were set up by the Quakers in the late 1700s, and they petitioned Parliament to ban the practice. William Wilberforce, an evangelical Christian and a member of Parliament, also played an important part in changing the law. Along with other abolitionists (people who supported the abolition of slavery), he succeeded in turning public opinion against the slave trade. In 1807, it became illegal to trade slaves in British ships or from British ports, and in 1833 the Emancipation Act abolished slavery throughout the British Empire. The Royal Navy stopped slave ships from other countries, freed the slaves and punished the slave traders. After 1833, 2 million Indian and Chinese workers were employed to replace the freed slaves. They worked on sugar plantations in the Caribbean, in mines in South Africa, on railways in East Africa and in the army in Kenya.
zh: 然而，英国也有人反对奴隶贸易。第一个正式的废奴团体由贵格会（Quakers）在18世纪末建立，他们向议会请愿要求禁止这种做法。威廉·威尔伯福斯（William Wilberforce）是一位福音派基督徒和下议院议员，他在改变法律方面也发挥了重要作用。与其他废奴主义者（支持废除奴隶制的人）一起，他成功地将公众舆论转向反对奴隶贸易。1807年，在英国船只上交易奴隶或从英国港口交易奴隶成为非法行为；1833年，《废奴法案》（Emancipation Act）在大英帝国范围内废除了奴隶制。皇家海军拦截其他国家的奴隶船，释放奴隶并惩罚奴隶贩子。1833年之后，雇佣了200万印度和中国工人来取代被解放的奴隶。他们在加勒比地区的甘蔗种植园、南非的矿山、东非的铁路以及肯尼亚的军队中工作。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: By the 1760s, there were substantial British colonies in North America. The colonies were wealthy and largely in control of their own affairs. Many of the colonist families had originally gone to North America in order to have religious freedom. They were well educated and interested in ideas of liberty. The British government wanted to tax the colonies. The colonists saw this as an attack on their freedom and said there should be ‘no taxation without representation’ in the British Parliament. Parliament tried to compromise by repealing some of the taxes, but relationships between the British government and the colonies continued to worsen. Fighting broke out between the colonists and the British forces. In 1776, 13 American colonies declared their independence, stating that people had a right to establish their own governments. The colonists eventually defeated the British army and Britain recognised the colonies’ independence in 1783.
zh: 到1760年代，北美已有大量的英国殖民地。这些殖民地富裕且 largely 自主管理其事务。许多殖民者家庭最初前往北美是为了获得宗教自由。他们受过良好教育，并对自由理念感兴趣。英国政府想要对殖民地征税。殖民者认为这是对自由的侵犯，并声称在议会中“无代表不纳税”。议会试图通过废除部分税收来妥协，但英国政府与殖民地之间的关系继续恶化。殖民者与英军之间爆发了战斗。1776年，13个美国殖民地宣布独立，声明人们有权建立自己的政府。殖民者最终击败了英军，英国于1783年承认了殖民地的独立。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: During the 18th century, Britain fought a number of wars with France. In 1789, there was a revolution in France and the new French government soon declared war on Britain. Napoleon, who became Emperor of France, continued the war. Britain’s navy fought against combined French and Spanish fleets, winning the Battle of Trafalgar in 1805. Admiral Nelson was in charge of the British fleet at Trafalgar and was killed in the battle. Nelson’s Column in Trafalgar Square, London, is a monument to him. His ship, HMS Victory, can be visited in Portsmouth. The British army also fought against the French. In 1815, the French Wars ended with the defeat of the Emperor Napoleon by the Duke of Wellington at the Battle of Waterloo. Wellington was known as the Iron Duke and later became Prime Minister.
zh: 在18世纪，英国与法国打了一系列战争。1789年，法国发生革命，新的法国政府很快对英国宣战。成为法国皇帝的拿破仑（Napoleon）继续这场战争。英国海军对抗了法国和西班牙的联合舰队，并于1805年在特拉法加海战（Battle of Trafalgar）中获胜。纳尔逊海军上将（Admiral Nelson）指挥特拉法加的海军舰队，并在战斗中阵亡。伦敦特拉法加广场的纳尔逊纪念柱（Nelson’s Column）是为纪念他而建的。他的战舰胜利号（HMS Victory）可在朴茨茅斯参观。英军也与法军作战。1815年，法国战争以威灵顿公爵（Duke of Wellington）在滑铁卢战役（Battle of Waterloo）中击败拿破仑皇帝而结束。威灵顿被称为“铁公爵”，后来成为首相。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: Although Ireland had had the same monarch as England and Wales since Henry VIII, it had remained a separate country. In 1801, Ireland became unified with England, Scotland and Wales after the Act of Union of 1800. This created the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland. One symbol of this union between England, Scotland, Wales and Ireland was a new version of the official flag, the Union Flag. This is often called the Union Jack. The flag combined crosses associated with England, Scotland and Ireland. It is still used today as the official flag of the UK.
zh: 尽管自亨利八世（Henry VIII）以来，爱尔兰与英格兰和威尔士拥有同一位君主，但爱尔兰一直是一个独立的国家。1801年，根据1800年《联合法案》（Act of Union），爱尔兰与英格兰、苏格兰和威尔士统一，成立了大不列颠及爱尔兰联合王国（United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland）。英格兰、苏格兰、威尔士和爱尔兰之间这一联盟的象征是一面新的官方旗帜——联合旗（Union Flag）。它通常被称为联合杰克（Union Jack）。这面旗帜融合了代表英格兰、苏格兰和爱尔兰的十字图案。至今，它仍作为英国的官方国旗使用。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: The Union Flag consists of three crosses:
zh: 联合旗由三个十字组成：

heading: 3.3 A global power
en:
• The cross of St George, patron saint of England, is a red cross on a white ground.
• The cross of St Andrew, patron saint of Scotland, is a diagonal white cross on a blue ground.
• The cross of St Patrick, patron saint of Ireland, is a diagonal red cross on a white ground.
zh:
• 圣乔治十字（cross of St George）：英格兰的主保圣人（patron saint）是圣乔治，其图案为白色背景上的红色十字。
• 圣安德鲁十字（cross of St Andrew）：苏格兰的主保圣人是圣安德鲁，其图案为蓝色背景上的白色对角十字。
• 圣帕特里克十字（cross of St Patrick）：爱尔兰的主保圣人是圣帕特里克，其图案为白色背景上的红色对角十字。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: There is also an official Welsh flag, which shows a Welsh dragon. The Welsh dragon does not appear on the Union Flag because, when the first Union Flag was created in 1606 from the flags of Scotland and England, the Principality of Wales was already united with England.
zh: 威尔士还有一面官方旗帜，上面绘有威尔士龙。威尔士龙并未出现在联合旗上，因为当第一面联合旗于1606年根据苏格兰和英格兰的旗帜设计时，威尔士公国（Principality of Wales）已经与英格兰合并。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: In 1837, Queen Victoria became queen of the UK at the age of 18. She reigned until 1901, almost 64 years. At the date of writing (2013) this is the longest reign of any British monarch. Her reign is known as the Victorian Age. It was a time when Britain increased in power and influence abroad. Within the UK, the middle classes because increasingly significant and a number of reformers led moves to improve conditions of life for the poor.
zh: 1837年，维多利亚女王（Queen Victoria）18岁时成为英国女王。她一直统治到1901年，在位近64年。截至本书撰写之时（2013年），这是任何一位英国君主在位时间最长的记录。她的统治时期被称为维多利亚时代。那时，英国在海外的权力和影响力不断增强。在英国国内，中产阶级变得越来越重要，许多改革者领导运动以改善穷人的生活条件。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: During the Victorian period, the British Empire grew to cover all of India, Australia and large parts of Africa. It became the largest empire the world has ever seen, with an estimated population of more than 400 million people.
zh: 在维多利亚时期，大英帝国（the British Empire）的领土扩张至涵盖整个印度、澳大利亚以及非洲的大部分地区。它成为有史以来世界上最大的帝国，估计人口超过4亿。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: Many people were encouraged to leave the UK to settle overseas. Between 1853 and 1913, as many as 13 million British citizens left the country. People continued to come to Britain from other parts of the world. For example, between 1870 and 1914, around 120,000 Russian and Polish Jews came to Britain to escape persecution. Many settled in London’s East End and in Manchester and Leeds. People from the Empire, including India and Africa, also came to Britain to live, work and study.
zh: 许多人被鼓励离开英国，在海外定居。1853年至1913年间，多达1300万英国公民离开该国。人们继续从世界其他地区来到英国。例如，1870年至1914年间，约有12万名俄罗斯和波兰犹太人为了逃避迫害来到英国。许多人定居在伦敦东区以及曼彻斯特和利兹。来自帝国各地（包括印度和非洲）的人们也来到英国生活、工作和学习。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: Britain continued to be a great trading nation. The government began to promote policies of free trade, abolishing a number of taxes on imported goods. One example of this was the repealing of the Corn Laws in 1846. These had prevented the import of cheap grain. The reforms helped the development of British industry, because raw materials could now be imported more cheaply.
zh: 英国继续作为一个伟大的贸易国家存在。政府开始推行自由贸易政策，废除了一些对进口商品的税收。其中一个例子是1846年废除《谷物法》（Corn Laws）。这些法律曾阻止廉价谷物的进口。这些改革有助于英国工业的发展，因为原材料现在可以更便宜地进口。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: Working conditions in factories gradually became better. In 1847, the number of hours that women and children could work was limited by law to 10 hours per day. Better housing began to be built for workers.
zh: 工厂的工作条件逐渐改善。1847年，法律将妇女和儿童的工作时间限制为每天10小时。开始为工人建造更好的住房。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: Transport links also improved, enabling goods and people to move more easily around the country. Just before Victoria came to the throne, the father and son George and Robert Stephenson pioneered the railway engine and a major expansion of the railways took place in the Victorian period. Railways were built throughout the Empire. There were also great advances in other areas, such as the building of bridges by engineers such as Isambard Kingdom Brunel.
zh: 交通联系也得到了改善，使货物和人员能够更轻松地在全国各地流动。在维多利亚登基前不久，乔治·斯蒂芬森（George Stephenson）和他的儿子罗伯特·斯蒂芬森（Robert Stephenson）开创了铁路机车技术，并在维多利亚时期发生了铁路的大规模扩张。铁路在整个帝国修建。其他领域也取得了巨大进步，例如由工程师伊桑巴德·金德姆·布鲁内尔（Isambard Kingdom Brunel）等人建造的桥梁。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: Brunel was originally from Portsmouth, England. He was an engineer who built tunnels, bridges, railway lines and ships. He was responsible for constructing the Great Western Railway, which was the first major railway built in Britain. It runs from Paddington Station in London to the south west of England, the West Midlands and Wales. Many of Brunel’s bridges are still in use today.
zh: 布鲁内尔（Brunel）最初来自英格兰的朴茨茅斯。他是一位工程师，建造了隧道、桥梁、铁路线和船只。他负责修建大西部铁路（Great Western Railway），这是英国修建的第一条主要铁路。它从伦敦的帕丁顿车站（Paddington Station）延伸至英格兰西南部、西米德兰兹和威尔士。布鲁内尔设计的许多桥梁至今仍在使用。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: British industry led the world in the 19th century. The UK produced more than half of the world’s iron, coal and cotton cloth. The UK also became a centre for financial services, including insurance and banking. In 1851, the Great Exhibition opened in Hyde Park in the Crystal Palace, a huge building made of steel and glass. Exhibits ranged from huge machines to handmade goods. Countries from all over the world showed their goods but most of the objects were made in Britain.
zh: 19世纪，英国工业领先世界。英国生产了全球一半以上的铁、煤和棉布。英国还成为金融服务中心，包括保险和银行业。1851年，万国工业博览会（Great Exhibition）在伦敦海德公园的水晶宫（Crystal Palace）开幕，这是一座由钢铁和玻璃制成的大型建筑。展品从巨型机器到手工艺品应有尽有。来自世界各地的国家展示了他们的商品，但大多数物品是在英国制造的。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: From 1853 to 1856, Britain fought with Turkey and France against Russia in the Crimean War. It was the first war to be extensively covered by the media through news stories and photographs. The conditions were very poor and many soldiers died from illnesses they caught in the hospitals, rather than from war wounds. Queen Victoria introduced the Victoria Cross medal during this war. It honours acts of valour by soldiers.
zh: 1853年至1856年间，英国与土耳其和法国一起，在克里米亚战争中对抗俄罗斯。这是第一场通过新闻报道和照片进行广泛媒体覆盖的战争。当时的条件非常恶劣，许多士兵并非死于战争创伤，而是死于在医院感染的疾病。维多利亚女王（Queen Victoria）在这场战争中引入了维多利亚十字勋章（Victoria Cross medal），以表彰士兵们的英勇行为。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: Florence Nightingale was born in Italy to English parents. At the age of 31, she trained as a nurse in Germany. In 1854, she went to Turkey and worked in military hospitals, treating soldiers who were fighting in the Crimean War. She and her fellow nurses improved the conditions in the hospital and reduced the mortality rate. In 1860 she established the Nightingale Training School for nurses at St Thomas’ Hospital in London. The school was the first of its kind and still exists today, as do many of the practices that Florence used. She is often regarded as the founder of modern nursing.
zh: 弗洛伦斯·南丁格尔（Florence Nightingale）出生于意大利，父母是英国人。31岁时，她在德国接受护士培训。1854年，她前往土耳其，在军事医院工作，治疗在克里米亚战争中作战的士兵。她和她的同事护士改善了医院的条件并降低了死亡率。1860年，她在伦敦的圣托马斯医院（St Thomas’ Hospital）建立了南丁格尔护士培训学校。这所学校是同类学校中的第一家，至今仍存在，弗洛伦斯使用的大多数做法也延续至今。她常被视为现代护理学的奠基人。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: Conditions in Ireland were not as good as in the rest of the UK. Two-thirds of the population still depended on farming to make their living, often on very small plots of land. Many depended on potatoes as a large part of their diet. In the middle of the century the potato crop failed, and Ireland suffered a famine. A million people died from disease and starvation. Another million and a half left Ireland. Some emigrated to the United States and others came to England. By 1861 there were large populations of Irish people in cities such as Liverpool, London, Manchester and Glasgow.
zh: 爱尔兰的条件不如联合王国（英国）其他地区好。三分之二的人口仍依靠农业谋生，往往是在非常小的土地上耕作。许多人依赖土豆作为饮食的主要部分。世纪中叶，马铃薯作物歉收，爱尔兰遭受了饥荒。一百万人死于疾病和饥饿。另有150万人离开爱尔兰。一些人移民到美国，另一些人来到英格兰。到1861年，利物浦、伦敦、曼彻斯特和格拉斯哥等城市都有了大量的爱尔兰人口。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: The Irish Nationalist movement had grown strongly through the 19th century. Some, such as the Fenians, favoured complete independence. Others, such as Charles Stuart Parnell, advocated ‘Home Rule’, in which Ireland would remain in the UK but have its own parliament.
zh: 爱尔兰民族主义运动在19世纪蓬勃发展。一些人，如芬尼亚人（Fenians），主张完全独立。另一些人，如查尔斯·斯图尔特·帕内尔（Charles Stuart Parnell），提倡“爱尔兰自治”（Home Rule），即爱尔兰留在联合王国（英国）内，但拥有自己的议会。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: As the middle classes in the wealthy industrial towns and cities grew in influence, they began to demand more political power. The Reform Act of 1832 had greatly increased the number of people with the right to vote. The Act also abolished the old pocket and rotten boroughs (see Chapter 4) and more parliamentary seats were given to the towns and cities. There was a permanent shift of political power from the countryside to the towns but voting was still based on ownership of property. This meant that members of the working class were still unable to vote.
zh: 随着富裕工业城镇和城市中产阶级的影响力增强，他们开始要求更多的政治权力。1832年的《改革法案》（Reform Act）极大地增加了拥有投票权的人数。该法案还废除了旧有的口袋选区和衰败选区（见第4章），并将更多的议会席位分配给城镇和城市。政治权力从农村向城镇发生了永久性转移，但投票权仍基于财产所有权。这意味着工人阶级成员仍然无法投票。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: A movement began to demand the vote for the working classes and other people without property. Campaigners, called the Chartists, presented petitions to Parliament. At first they seemed to be unsuccessful, but in 1867 there was another Reform Act. This created many more urban seats in Parliament and reduced the amount of property that people needed to have before they could vote. However, the majority of men still did not have the right to vote and no women could vote.
zh: 一场要求赋予工人阶级和其他无产者投票权的运动开始了。被称为宪章派（Chartists）的竞选者向议会提交请愿书。起初他们似乎没有成功，但在1867年通过了另一项《改革法案》（Reform Act）。这在议会中创造了更多的城市席位，并降低了人们获得投票权所需的财产门槛。然而，大多数男性仍然没有投票权，女性则完全没有投票权。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: Politicians realised that the increased number of voters meant that they needed to persuade people to vote for them if they were to be sure of being elected to Parliament. The political parties began to create organisations to reach out to ordinary voters. Universal suffrage (the right of every adult, male or female, to vote) followed in the next century.
zh: 政治家们意识到，选民数量的增加意味着如果他们确信能当选为下议院议员（MP），就需要说服人们投他们的票。各政党开始建立组织以接触普通选民。普选权（即每个成年人，无论男女，都有投票权）在下一个世纪得以实现。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: In common with the rest of Europe, women in 19th century Britain had fewer rights than men. Until 1870, when a woman got married, her earnings, property and money automatically belonged to her husband. Acts of Parliament in 1870 and 1882 gave wives the right to keep their own earnings and property. In the late 19th and early 20th centuries, an increasing number of women campaigned and demonstrated for greater rights and, in particular, the right to vote. They formed the women’s suffrage movement and became known as ‘suffragettes’.
zh: 与欧洲其他地区一样，19世纪英国女性的权利少于男性。直到1870年，女性结婚后，她的收入、财产和金钱自动归丈夫所有。1870年和1882年的议会法案赋予妻子保留自己收入和财产的权利。在19世纪末和20世纪初，越来越多的女性为争取更多权利，特别是投票权，进行竞选和示威活动。她们组成了妇女参政运动，被称为“妇女参政论者”（suffragettes）。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: Emmeline Pankhurst was born in Manchester in 1858. She set up the Women’s Franchise League in 1889, which fought to get the vote in local elections for married women. In 1903 she helped found the Women’s Social and Political Union (WSPU). This was the first group whose members were called ‘suffragettes’. The group used civil disobedience as part of their protest to gain the vote for women. They chained themselves to railings, smashed windows and committed arson. Many of the women, including Emmeline, went on hunger strike. In 1918, women over the age of 30 were given voting rights and the right to stand for Parliament, partly in recognition of the contribution women made to the war effort during the First World War. Shortly before Emmeline’s death in 1928, women were given the right to vote at the age of 21, the same as men.
zh: 埃米琳·潘克赫斯特（Emmeline Pankhurst）于1858年出生在曼彻斯特。她于1889年创立了妇女选举权联盟（Women’s Franchise League），致力于争取已婚女性在地方选举中的投票权。1903年，她协助创立了妇女社会政治联盟（WSPU）。这是第一个其成员被称为“女权论者”（suffragettes）的组织。该组织将公民不服从作为其抗议活动的一部分，以争取女性的投票权。她们将自己锁在栏杆上、砸碎窗户并实施纵火。许多女性，包括埃米琳在内，都进行了绝食抗议。1918年，30岁以上的女性获得了投票权和成为下议院议员候选人的权利，部分原因是认可女性在第一次世界大战期间对战争努力的贡献。在埃米琳于1928年去世前不久，女性获得了与男性相同的21岁投票权。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: Although the British Empire continued to grow until the 1920s, there was already discussion in the late 19th century about its future direction. Supporters of expansion believed that the Empire benefited Britain through increased trade and commerce. Others thought the Empire had become over-expanded and that the frequent conflicts in many parts of the Empire, such as India’s north-west frontier or southern Africa, were a drain on resources. Yet the great majority of British people believed in the Empire as a force for good in the world.
zh: 虽然大英帝国一直持续到20世纪20年代仍在扩张，但在19世纪末就已经有关于其未来方向的讨论。扩张的支持者认为，帝国通过增加贸易和商业使英国受益。其他人则认为帝国已经过度扩张，且帝国许多地区频繁发生的冲突，如印度西北边境或南非，正在消耗资源。然而，绝大多数英国人相信帝国是世界上一股向善的力量。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: The Boer War of 1899 to 1902 made the discussions about the future of the Empire more urgent. The British went to war in South Africa with settlers from the Netherlands called the Boers. The Boers fought fiercely and the war went on for over three years. Many died in the fighting and many more from disease. There was some public sympathy for the Boers and people began to question whether the Empire could continue. As different parts of the Empire developed, they won greater freedom and autonomy from Britain. Eventually, by the second half of the 20th century, there was, for the most part, an orderly transition from Empire to Commonwealth, with countries being granted their independence.
zh: 1899年至1902年的布尔战争使关于帝国未来的讨论更加紧迫。英国与来自荷兰的定居者布尔人（Boers）在南非开战。布尔人进行了激烈的战斗，战争持续了三年多。许多人在战斗中死亡，还有更多人死于疾病。公众对布尔人表示同情，人们开始质疑帝国是否还能继续存在。随着帝国不同部分的发展，它们从英国获得了更大的自由和自治权。最终，在20世纪下半叶，帝国大多有序地过渡为英联邦，各国被授予独立地位。

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: Rudyard Kipling was born in India in 1865 and later lived in India, the UK and the USA. He wrote books and poems set in both India and the UK. His poems and novels reflected the idea that the British Empire was a force for good. Kipling was awarded the Nobel Prize in Literature in 1907. His books include the Just So Stories and The Jungle Book, which continue to be popular today. His poem If has often been voted among the UK’s favourite poems. It begins with these words:
zh: 鲁德亚德·吉卜林（Rudyard Kipling）于1865年出生在印度，后来曾在印度、联合王国（英国）和美国生活。他创作了以印度和英国为背景的书籍和诗歌。他的诗歌和小说反映了大英帝国是世界上一股向善力量的观点。吉卜林于1907年获得诺贝尔文学奖。他的作品包括《如果的故事》（Just So Stories）和《丛林之书》（The Jungle Book），至今仍广受欢迎。他的诗作《如果》（If）经常被评为英国人最喜爱的诗歌之一。这首诗以以下文字开头：

heading: 3.3 A global power
en: ‘If you can keep your head when all about you Are losing theirs and blaming it on you; If you can trust yourself when all men doubt you, But make allowance for their doubting too; If you can wait and not be tired by waiting, Or being lied about, don’t deal in lies, Or being hated, don’t give way to hating, And yet don’t look too good, nor talk too wise’ (If, Rudyard Kipling)
zh: “如果你能在周围的人都失去理智并归咎于你时保持冷静；如果你能在所有人都怀疑你时相信自己，但也体谅他们的怀疑；如果你能等待而不因等待而疲惫，或被诬陷时不以谎言回应，或被憎恨时不以仇恨回报，同时也不显得过于自满或卖弄聪明”（《如果》，鲁德亚德·吉卜林）

heading: 3.3 A global power
en:
• The change in the balance of power between Parliament and the monarchy
• When and why Scotland joined England and Wales to become Great Britain
• The reasons for a rebellion in Scotland led by Bonnie Prince Charlie
• The ideas of the Enlightenment
• The importance of the Industrial Revolution and development of industry
• The slave trade and when it was abolished
• The growth of the British Empire
• How democracy developed during this period
zh:
• 议会与王室之间权力平衡的变化
• 苏格兰何时以及为何加入英格兰和威尔士成为大不列颠
• 邦尼王子查理领导苏格兰叛乱的原因
• 启蒙运动的思想
• 工业革命及工业发展的重要性
• 奴隶贸易及其废除时间
• 大英帝国的扩张
• 这一时期民主的发展过程

heading: 3.4 The 20th century
en: The early 20th century was a time of optimism in Britain. The nation, with its expansive Empire, well-admired navy, thriving industry and strong political institutions, was what is now known as a global ‘superpower’. It was also a time of social progress. Financial help for the unemployed, old-age pensions and free school meals were just a few of the important measures introduced. Various laws were passed to improve safety in the workplace; town planning rules were tightened to prevent the further development of slums; and better support was given to mothers and their children after divorce or separation. Local government became more democratic and a salary for members of Parliament (MPs) was introduced for the first time, making it easier for more people to take part in public life.
zh: 20世纪初的英国是一个充满乐观主义的时期。这个拥有广阔帝国、备受赞誉的海军、繁荣工业和强大政治制度的国家，就是如今所称的全球“超级大国”。这也是一个社会进步的时期。为失业者提供经济援助、发放养老金以及提供免费校餐，只是引入的重要措施中的一部分。通过了一系列法律以提高工作场所的安全性；收紧城市规划规则以防止贫民窟的进一步扩张；并在离婚或分居后给予母亲及其子女更好的支持。地方政府变得更加民主，并首次引入了下议院议员（MPs）的薪水，使得更多人能够参与公共生活。

heading: 3.4 The 20th century
en: This era of optimism and progress was cut short when war broke out between several European nations. On 28 June 1914, Archduke Franz Ferdinand of Austria was assassinated. This set off a chain of events leading to the First World War (1914–18). But while the assassination provided the trigger for war, other factors – such as a growing sense of nationalism in many European states; increasing militarism; imperialism; and the division of the major European powers into two camps – all set the conditions for war.
zh: 这一乐观与进步的时期因欧洲多国之间的战争爆发而戛然而止。1914年6月28日，奥地利大公弗朗茨·斐迪南遇刺身亡。这引发了一系列导致第一次世界大战（1914–18）的事件。虽然刺杀事件是战争的导火索，但其他因素——如许多欧洲国家日益增长的民族主义情绪、军国主义的加剧、帝国主义以及主要欧洲强国分裂为两大阵营——都为战争创造了条件。

heading: 3.4 The 20th century
en: The conflict was centred in Europe, but it was a global war involving nations from around the world. Britain was part of the Allied Powers, which included (amongst others) France, Russia, Japan, Belgium, Serbia – and later, Greece, Italy, Romania and the United States. The whole of the British Empire was involved in the conflict – for example, more than a million Indians fought on behalf of Britain in lots of different countries, and around 40,000 were killed. Men from the West Indies, Africa, Australia, New Zealand and Canada also fought with the British. The Allies fought against the Central Powers – mainly Germany, the Austro- Hungarian Empire, the Ottoman Empire and later Bulgaria. Millions of people were killed or wounded, with more than 2 million British casualties.
