One battle, the British attack on the Somme in July 1916, resulted in about 60,000 British casualties on the first day alone.
zh: 这场冲突以欧洲为中心，但这是一场涉及全球各国的世界大战。英国是协约国的一员，协约国包括（但不限于）法国、俄罗斯、日本、比利时、塞尔维亚——后来又有希腊、意大利、罗马尼亚和美国加入。整个大英帝国都卷入了这场冲突——例如，超过一百万印度人在许多不同国家为英国作战，其中约4万人阵亡。来自西印度群岛、非洲、澳大利亚、新西兰和加拿大的男子也与英军并肩作战。协约国对抗的是同盟国——主要是德国、奥匈帝国、奥斯曼帝国以及后来的保加利亚。数百万人伤亡，英国有超过200万人伤亡。其中一场战役，即1916年7月英军对索姆河的进攻，仅在第一天就造成了约6万名英军伤亡。

heading: 3.4 The 20th century
en: The First World War ended at 11.00 am on 11th November 1918 with victory for Britain and its allies.
zh: 第一次世界大战于1918年11月11日上午11点结束，英国及其盟友取得胜利。

heading: 3.4 The 20th century
en: In 1913, the British government promised ‘Home Rule’ for Ireland. The proposal was to have a self-governing Ireland with its own parliament but still part of the UK. A Home Rule Bill was introduced in Parliament. It was opposed by the Protestants in the north of Ireland, who threatened to resist Home Rule by force.
zh: 1913年，英国政府承诺给予爱尔兰“自治”。该提案旨在建立一个拥有自己议会但仍属于联合王国的自治爱尔兰。一项自治法案在议会提出。它遭到爱尔兰北部新教徒的反对，他们威胁要以武力抵抗自治。

heading: 3.4 The 20th century
en: The outbreak of the First World War led the British government to postpone any changes in Ireland. Irish Nationalists were not willing to wait and in 1916 there was an uprising (the Easter Rising) against the British in Dublin. The leaders of the uprising were executed under military law. A guerrilla war against the British army and the police in Ireland followed. In 1921 a peace treaty was signed and in 1922 Ireland became two countries. The six counties in the north which were mainly Protestant remained part of the UK under the name Northern Ireland. The rest of Ireland became the Irish Free State. It had its own government and became a republic in 1949.
zh: 第一次世界大战的爆发导致英国政府推迟了任何针对爱尔兰的改革。爱尔兰民族主义者不愿等待，1916年在都柏林爆发了反抗英国的起义（即复活节起义）。起义领袖依据军法被处决。随后，爱尔兰境内发生了针对英军和警察的游击战。1921年签署了一项和平条约，1922年爱尔兰分裂为两个国家。主要由新教徒组成的北部六个郡作为北爱尔兰继续留在联合王国内。爱尔兰其余部分成为爱尔兰自由邦。它拥有自己的政府，并于1949年成为共和国。

heading: 3.4 The 20th century
en: There were people in both parts of Ireland who disagreed with the split between the North and the South. They still wanted Ireland to be one independent country. Years of disagreement led to a terror campaign in Northern Ireland and elsewhere. The conflict between those wishing for full Irish independence and those wishing to remain loyal to the British government is often referred to as ‘the Troubles’.
zh: 爱尔兰南北两部分都有人反对分裂，他们仍希望爱尔兰成为一个独立的统一国家。多年的分歧导致了北爱尔兰及其他地区的恐怖活动。渴望完全独立者与希望继续效忠英国政府者之间的冲突，通常被称为‘北爱尔兰问题时期’（the Troubles）。

heading: 3.4 The 20th century
en: In the 1920s, many people’s living conditions got better. There were improvements in public housing and new homes were built in many towns and cities. However, in 1929, the world entered the ‘Great Depression’ and some parts of the UK suffered mass unemployment. The effects of the depression of the 1930s were felt differently in different parts of the UK. The traditional heavy industries such as shipbuilding were badly affected but new industries – including the automobile and aviation industries – developed. As prices generally fell, those in work had more money to spend. Car ownership doubled from 1 million to 2 million between 1930 and 1939. In addition, many new houses were built. It was also a time of cultural blossoming, with writers such as Graham Greene and Evelyn Waugh prominent. The economist John Maynard Keynes published influential new theories of economics. The BBC started radio broadcasts in 1922 and began the world’s first regular television service in 1936.
zh: 20世纪20年代，许多人的生活条件得到改善。公共住房有所改进，许多城镇建造了新房。然而，1929年世界进入‘大萧条’（the Great Depression）时期，英国部分地区遭受大规模失业。20世纪30年代经济危机的影响在英国不同地区表现各异。造船等传统重工业受到严重打击，但包括汽车和航空业在内的新工业得以发展。随着物价普遍下跌，就业者拥有更多的可支配收入。1930年至1939年间，汽车保有量从100万辆翻倍至200万辆。此外，许多新房被建造。这也是一个文化繁荣的时期，格雷厄姆·格林（Graham Greene）和伊夫林·沃（Evelyn Waugh）等作家崭露头角。经济学家约翰·梅纳德·凯恩斯（John Maynard Keynes）发表了具有影响力的新经济学理论。英国广播公司（BBC）于1922年开始无线电广播，并于1936年启动了世界上首个定期电视服务。

heading: 3.4 The 20th century
en: Adolf Hitler came to power in Germany in 1933. He believed that the conditions imposed on Germany by the Allies after the First World War were unfair; he also wanted to conquer more land for the German people. He set about renegotiating treaties, building up arms, and testing Germany’s military strength in nearby countries. The British government tried to avoid another war. However, when Hitler invaded Poland in 1939, Britain and France declared war in order to stop his aggression.
zh: 阿道夫·希特勒（Adolf Hitler）于1933年在德国掌权。他认为第一次世界大战后盟国强加给德国的条件是不公平的；他还希望为德意志民族征服更多土地。他开始重新谈判条约，扩充军备，并在邻国测试德国的军事实力。英国政府试图避免另一场战争。然而，当希特勒于1939年入侵波兰时，英国和法国宣战以阻止其侵略。

heading: 3.4 The 20th century
en: The war was initially fought between the Axis powers (fascist Germany and Italy and the Empire of Japan) and the Allies. The main countries on the allied side were the UK, France, Poland, Australia, New Zealand, Canada, and the Union of South Africa.
zh: 战争初期在轴心国（法西斯德国、意大利和日本帝国）与同盟国之间进行。同盟方的主要国家包括英国、法国、波兰、澳大利亚、新西兰、加拿大和南非联邦。

heading: 3.4 The 20th century
en: Having occupied Austria and invaded Czechoslovakia, Hitler followed his invasion of Poland by taking control of Belgium and the Netherlands. Then, in 1940, German forces defeated allied troops and advanced through France. At this time of national crisis, Winston Churchill became Prime Minister and Britain’s war leader.
zh: 在占领奥地利并入侵捷克斯洛伐克后，希特勒在入侵波兰之后又控制了比利时和荷兰。随后，在1940年，德军击败盟军部队并推进至法国。在这个国家危机时刻，温斯顿·丘吉尔（Winston Churchill）成为首相及英国战时领导人。

heading: 3.4 The 20th century
en: As France fell, the British decided to evacuate British and French soldiers from France in a huge naval operation. Many civilian volunteers in small pleasure and fishing boats from Britain helped the Navy to rescue more than 300,000 men from the beaches around Dunkirk. Although many lives and a lot of equipment were lost, the evacuation was a success and meant that Britain was better able to continue the fight against the Germans. The evacuation gave rise to the phrase ‘the Dunkirk spirit’.
zh: 随着法国沦陷，英国决定通过一次大规模的海军行动从法国撤离英法士兵。许多来自英国的民用志愿人员驾驶小型游艇和渔船协助海军从敦刻尔克的海岸救出了超过30万人。虽然损失了许多生命和大量装备，但这次撤离是成功的，使英国能够更好地继续对抗德国人。这次撤离催生了‘敦刻尔克精神’（the Dunkirk spirit）这一说法。

heading: 3.4 The 20th century
en: From the end of June 1940 until the German invasion of the Soviet Union in June 1941, Britain and the Empire stood almost alone against Nazi Germany.
zh: 从1940年6月底到1941年6月德国入侵苏联，英国及英联邦几乎独自对抗纳粹德国。

heading: 3.4 The 20th century
en: Hitler wanted to invade Britain, but before sending in troops, Germany needed to control the air. The Germans waged an air campaign against Britain, but the British resisted with their fighter planes and eventually won the crucial aerial battle against the Germans, called ‘the Battle of Britain’, in the summer of 1940. The most important planes used by the Royal Air Force in the Battle of Britain were the Spitfire and the Hurricane – which were designed and built in Britain. Despite this crucial victory, the German air force was able to continue bombing London and other British cities at night-time. This was called the Blitz. Coventry was almost totally destroyed and a great deal of damage was done in other cities, especially in the East End of London. Despite the destruction, there was a strong national spirit of resistance in the UK. The phrase ‘the Blitz spirit’ is still used today to describe Britons pulling together in the face of adversity.
zh: 希特勒企图入侵英国，但在派遣地面部队之前，德国需要掌握制空权。德国对英国发动了空中战役，但英国凭借战斗机进行抵抗，并最终在1940年夏天的关键空中战役中击败德国人，这场战役被称为‘不列颠之战’（the Battle of Britain）。皇家空军在不列颠之战中使用的重要飞机是喷火式（Spitfire）和飓风式（Hurricane），它们由英国设计并制造。尽管取得了这一关键胜利，德国空军仍能在夜间继续轰炸伦敦和其他英国城市。这被称为‘闪电战’（Blitz）。考文垂几乎被完全摧毁，其他城市也遭受了巨大破坏，尤其是伦敦东区。尽管遭到破坏，英国国内仍保持着强烈的抵抗精神。如今，‘闪电战精神’（the Blitz spirit）一词仍被用来形容英国人在逆境中团结一致。

heading: 3.4 The 20th century
en: Churchill was the son of a politician and, before becoming a Conservative MP in 1900, was a soldier and journalist. In May 1940 he became Prime Minister. He refused to surrender to the Nazis and was an inspirational leader to the British people in a time of great hardship. He lost the General Election in 1945 but returned as Prime Minister in 1951.
zh: 丘吉尔（Winston Churchill）是一位政治家的儿子，在1900年成为保守党下议院议员之前，他是一名士兵和记者。1940年5月，他出任首相。他拒绝向纳粹投降，并在艰难时期激励了英国人民。他在1945年的大选中落败，但于1951年再次担任首相。

heading: 3.4 The 20th century
en: He was an MP until he stood down at the 1964 General Election. Following his death in 1965, he was given a state funeral. He remains a much-admired figure to this day, and in 2002 was voted the greatest Briton of all time by the public. During the War, he made many famous speeches including lines which you may still hear:
zh: 他担任下议院议员直至在1964年大选中退选。1965年去世后，他享有了国葬。直到今天，他仍然是一位备受敬仰的人物，并在2002年被公众票选为有史以来最伟大的英国人。在战争期间，他发表了众多著名演讲，其中一些名言你至今仍可能听到：

heading: 3.4 The 20th century
en: ‘I have nothing to offer but blood, toil, tears and sweat’ Churchill’s first speech to the House of Commons after he became Prime Minister, 1940
zh: “我所能奉献的唯有热血、辛劳、眼泪和汗水。”——丘吉尔就任首相后首次向下议院发表的演讲，1940年

heading: 3.4 The 20th century
en: ‘We shall fight on the beaches, we shall fight on the landing grounds, we shall fight in the fields and in the streets, we shall fight in the hills; we shall never surrender’ Speech to the House of Commons after Dunkirk 1940
zh: “我们将在海滩上战斗，我们将在登陆场上战斗，我们将在田野和街道上战斗，我们将在山丘上战斗；我们绝不投降。”——敦刻尔克之后向下议院发表的演讲，1940年

heading: 3.4 The 20th century
en: ‘Never in the field of human conflict was so much owed by so many to so few’ Speech to the House of Commons during the Battle of Britain, 1940
zh: “在人类冲突的历史中，如此多的受惠者对如此少的人欠下了如此大的恩情。”——1940年不列颠战役期间在下议院的演讲

heading: 3.4 The 20th century
en: At the same time as defending Britain, the British military was fighting the Axis on many other fronts. In Singapore, the Japanese defeated the British and then occupied Burma, threatening India. The United States entered the war when the Japanese bombed its naval base at Pearl Harbour in December 1941.
zh: 与此同时，在保卫英国的同时，英国军队还在许多其他战线上与轴心国作战。在新加坡，日本人击败了英军，随后占领了缅甸，威胁到印度。1941年12月，日本轰炸了美国的珍珠港海军基地，美国因此参战。

heading: 3.4 The 20th century
en: That same year, Hitler attempted the largest invasion in history by attacking the Soviet Union. It was a fierce conflict, with huge losses on both sides. German forces were ultimately repelled by the Soviets, and the damage they sustained proved to be a pivotal point in the war.
zh: 同年，希特勒发动了历史上最大规模的入侵，进攻苏联。这是一场激烈的冲突，双方都遭受了巨大损失。德军最终被苏军击退，他们遭受的损害成为战争的一个转折点。

heading: 3.4 The 20th century
en: The allied forces gradually gained the upper hand, winning significant victories in North Africa and Italy. German losses in the Soviet Union, combined with the support of the Americans, meant that the Allies were eventually strong enough to attack Hitler’s forces in Western Europe. On 6 June 1944, allied forces landed in Normandy (this event is often referred to as ‘D-Day’). Following victory on the beaches of Normandy, the allied forces pressed on through France and eventually into Germany. The Allies comprehensively defeated Germany in May 1945.
zh: 盟军逐渐占据上风，在北非和意大利取得了重大胜利。德军在苏联的损失，加上美国的支持，意味着盟军最终有足够的力量攻击希特勒在西欧的部队。1944年6月6日，盟军在诺曼底登陆（这一事件通常被称为“D-Day”）。在诺曼底海滩取得胜利后，盟军继续推进，穿过法国，最终进入德国。1945年5月，盟军彻底击败了德国。

heading: 3.4 The 20th century
en: The war against Japan ended in August 1945 when the United States dropped its newly developed atom bombs on the Japanese cities of Hiroshima and Nagasaki. Scientists led by Ernest Rutherford, working at Manchester and then Cambridge University, were the first to ‘split the atom’ and took part in the Manhattan Project in the United States, which developed the atomic bomb. The war was finally over.
zh: 对日战争于1945年8月结束，当时美国在日本城市广岛和长崎投下了新开发的原子弹。由欧内斯特·卢瑟福（Ernest Rutherford）领导的科学家团队，先在曼彻斯特工作，后来在剑桥大学工作，是第一批“分裂原子”的人，并参与了美国的曼哈顿计划，该计划研发了原子弹。战争终于结束了。

heading: 3.4 The 20th century
en: Born in Scotland, Fleming moved to London as a teenager and later qualified as a doctor. He was researching influenza (the ‘flu’) in 1928 when he discovered penicillin. This was then further developed into a usable drug by the scientists Howard Florey and Ernst Chain. By the 1940s it was in mass production. Fleming won the Nobel Prize in Medicine in 1945. Penicillin is still used to treat bacterial infections today.
zh: 弗莱明（Fleming）出生于苏格兰，十几岁时搬到伦敦，后来获得了医生资格。1928年，他在研究流感（“flu”）时发现了青霉素。随后，科学家霍华德·弗洛里（Howard Florey）和恩斯特·钱恩（Ernst Chain）将其进一步开发成可用的药物。到20世纪40年代，它已实现大规模生产。弗莱明于1945年获得诺贝尔医学奖。直到今天，青霉素仍用于治疗细菌感染。

heading: 3.4 The 20th century
en:
• What happened during the First World War
• The partition of Ireland and the establishment of the UK as it is today
• The events of the Second World War
zh:
• 第一次世界大战期间发生了什么
• 爱尔兰的分裂以及联合王国如何成为今天的样貌
• 第二次世界大战的事件

heading: 3.5 The Middle Ages
en: The period after the Norman Conquest up until about 1485 is called the Middle Ages (or the medieval period). It was a time of almost constant war.
zh: 从诺曼征服到大约1485年的这段时期被称为中世纪（或中世纪时期）。那是一个几乎持续不断的战争时代。

heading: 3.5 The Middle Ages
en: The English kings fought with the Welsh, Scottish and Irish noblemen for control of their lands. In Wales, the English were able to establish their rule. In 1284 King Edward I of England introduced the Statute of Rhuddlan, which annexed Wales to the Crown of England. Huge castles, including Conwy and Caernarvon, were built to maintain this power. By the middle of the 15th century the last Welsh rebellions had been defeated. English laws and the English language were introduced.
zh: 英格兰国王与威尔士、苏格兰和爱尔兰的贵族为争夺土地控制权而战。在威尔士，英格兰人成功确立了其统治。1284年，英格兰国王爱德华一世（Edward I）颁布《鲁德兰法令》（Statute of Rhuddlan），将威尔士并入英格兰王室。为了维持这一权力，修建了包括康威（Conwy）和卡那封（Caernarvon）在内的大型城堡。到15世纪中叶，最后的威尔士叛乱已被平息。英格兰的法律和语言被引入。

heading: 3.5 The Middle Ages
en: In Scotland, the English kings were less successful. In 1314 the Scottish, led by Robert the Bruce, defeated the English at the Battle of Bannockburn, and Scotland remained unconquered by the English.
zh: 在苏格兰，英格兰国王的成功较少。1314年，由罗伯特·布鲁斯（Robert the Bruce）领导的苏格兰人在班诺克本战役（Battle of Bannockburn）中击败英格兰人，苏格兰未被英格兰征服。

heading: 3.5 The Middle Ages
en: At the beginning of the Middle Ages, Ireland was an independent country. The English first went to Ireland as troops to help the Irish king and remained to build their own settlements. By 1200, the English ruled an area of Ireland known as the Pale, around Dublin. Some of the important lords in other parts of Ireland accepted the authority of the English king.
zh: 在中世纪初期，爱尔兰是一个独立国家。英格兰人最初作为军队前往爱尔兰，帮助爱尔兰国王，并留下来建立自己的定居点。到1200年，英格兰人统治了爱尔兰的一个称为“帕尔”（the Pale）的地区，位于都柏林周围。爱尔兰其他地区的一些重要领主接受了英格兰国王的权威。

heading: 3.5 The Middle Ages
en: During the Middle Ages, the English kings also fought a number of wars abroad. Many knights took part in the Crusades, in which European Christians fought for control of the Holy Land. English kings also fought a long war with France, called the Hundred Years War (even though it actually lasted 116 years). One of the most famous battles of the Hundred Years War was the Battle of Agincourt in 1415, where King Henry V’s vastly outnumbered English army defeated the French. The English left France in the 1450s.
zh: 在中世纪，英格兰国王还在国外进行了一系列战争。许多骑士参加了十字军东征（the Crusades），欧洲基督徒在其中为争夺圣地控制权而战。英格兰国王还与法国进行了一场漫长的战争，称为百年战争（Hundred Years War）（尽管实际上持续了116年）。百年战争中最著名的战役之一是1415年的阿金库尔战役（Battle of Agincourt），亨利五世（Henry V）率领的兵力处于绝对劣势的英格兰军队击败了法军。英格兰人在1450年代离开了法国。

heading: 3.5 The Middle Ages
en: The Normans used a system of land ownership known as feudalism. The king gave land to his lords in return for help in war. Landowners had to send certain numbers of men to serve in the army. Some peasants had their own land but most were serfs.
zh: 诺曼人使用一种称为封建主义（feudalism）的土地所有权制度。国王将土地授予他的领主，以换取战争中的帮助。土地所有者必须派遣一定数量的人服役于军队。一些农民拥有自己的土地，但大多数是农奴。

heading: 3.5 The Middle Ages
en: They had a small area of their lord’s land where they could grow food. In return, they had to work for their lord and could not move away. The same system developed in southern Scotland. In the north of Scotland and Ireland, land was owned by members of the ‘clans’ (prominent families).
zh: 他们拥有领主土地的一小块区域，可以在那里种植食物。作为回报，他们必须为领主工作，且不能搬走。同样的制度也在苏格兰南部发展起来。在苏格兰北部和爱尔兰，土地由“氏族”（clans，即显赫家族）成员拥有。

heading: 3.5 The Middle Ages
en: In 1348, a disease, probably a form of plague, came to Britain. This was known as the Black Death. One third of the population of England died and a similar proportion in Scotland and Wales. This was one of the worst disasters ever to strike Britain. Following the Black Death, the smaller population meant there was less need to grow cereal crops. There were labour shortages and peasants began to demand higher wages. New social classes appeared, including owners of large areas of land (later called the gentry), and people left the countryside to live in the towns. In the towns, growing wealth led to the development of a strong middle class.
zh: 1348年，一种疾病（可能是鼠疫的一种形式）传入不列颠。这被称为黑死病（Black Death）。英格兰三分之一的人口死亡，苏格兰和威尔士也有类似比例的人口丧生。这是袭击不列颠的最严重灾难之一。黑死病之后，人口减少意味着对种植谷物作物的需求降低。劳动力短缺，农民开始要求更高的工资。新的社会阶层出现，包括拥有大片土地的人（后来称为乡绅），人们离开乡村到城镇居住。在城镇中，日益增长的财富促成了一个强大的中产阶级的形成。

heading: 3.5 The Middle Ages
en: In Ireland, the Black Death killed many in the Pale and, for a time, the area controlled by the English became smaller.
zh: 在爱尔兰，黑死病在帕尔地区夺去了许多人的生命，并且在一段时间内，英格兰控制的区域变小了。

heading: 3.5 The Middle Ages
en: In the Middle Ages, Parliament began to develop into the institution it is today. Its origins can be traced to the king’s council of advisers, which included important noblemen and the leaders of the Church.
zh: 在中世纪，议会开始发展成为今天的机构。其起源可追溯至国王的顾问团，其中包括重要的贵族和教会领袖。

heading: 3.5 The Middle Ages
en: There were few formal limits to the king’s power until 1215. In that year, King John was forced by his noblemen to agree to a number of demands. The result was a charter of rights called the Magna Carta (which means the Great Charter). The Magna Carta established the idea that even the king was subject to the law. It protected the rights of the nobility and restricted the king’s power to collect taxes or to make or change laws. In future, the king would need to involve his noblemen in decisions.
zh: 直到1215年，对国王权力的正式限制还很少。那年，约翰王（King John）被迫应对其贵族提出的多项要求。结果产生了一份名为《大宪章》（Magna Carta，意为“大宪章”）的权利宪章。《大宪章》确立了即使是国王也必须服从法律的理念。它保护了贵族的权利，并限制了国王征税或制定、修改法律的权力。此后，国王必须在决策中让贵族参与。

heading: 3.5 The Middle Ages
en: In England, parliaments were called for the king to consult his nobles, particularly when the king needed to raise money. The numbers attending Parliament increased and two separate parts, known as Houses, were established. The nobility, great landowners and bishops sat in the House of Lords. Knights, who were usually smaller landowners, and wealthy people from towns and cities were elected to sit in the House of Commons. Only a small part of the population was able to join in electing the members of the Commons.
zh: 在英格兰，议会召集是为了让国王咨询其贵族，特别是当国王需要筹集资金时。出席议会的人数增加，并建立了两个被称为“院”的独立部分。贵族、大地主和主教坐在上议院（House of Lords）。骑士（通常是较小的土地所有者）以及来自城镇和城市的富裕人士被选举进入下议院（House of Commons）。只有少数人口能够参与选举下议院议员。

heading: 3.5 The Middle Ages
en: A similar Parliament developed in Scotland. It had three Houses, called Estates: the lords, the commons and the clergy.
zh: 苏格兰也发展出了类似的议会。它有三个院，称为“等级”：贵族、平民和神职人员。

heading: 3.5 The Middle Ages
en: This was also a time of development in the legal system. The principle that judges are independent of the government began to be established. In England, judges developed ‘common law’ by a process of precedence (that is, following previous decisions) and tradition. In Scotland, the legal system developed slightly differently and laws were ‘codified’ (that is, written down).
zh: 这也是法律体系发展的时期。法官独立于政府的原则开始确立。在英格兰，法官通过遵循先例（即遵循之前的判决）和传统的方式发展出“普通法”。在苏格兰，法律体系的发展略有不同，法律被“编纂”（即写成文字）。

heading: 3.5 The Middle Ages
en: The Middle Ages saw the development of a national culture and identity. After the Norman Conquest, the king and his noblemen had spoken Norman French and the peasants had continued to speak Anglo-Saxon. Gradually these two languages combined to become one English language. Some words in modern English – for example, ‘park’ and ‘beauty’ – are based on Norman French words. Others – for example, ‘apple’, ‘cow’ and ‘summer’ – are based on Anglo-Saxon words. In modern English there are often words with very similar meanings, one from French and one from Anglo-Saxon. ‘Demand’ (French) and ‘ask’ (Anglo-Saxon) are examples. By 1400, in England, official documents were being written in English, and English had become the preferred language of the royal court and Parliament.
zh: 中世纪见证了民族文化和认同的发展。诺曼征服之后，国王和他的贵族们讲诺曼法语，而农民继续讲盎格鲁-撒克逊语。渐渐地，这两种语言融合成了一种英语。现代英语中的一些词汇——例如“park”（公园）和“beauty”（美丽）——源自诺曼法语词汇。另一些——例如“apple”（苹果）、“cow”（牛）和“summer”（夏天）——则源自盎格鲁-撒克逊语词汇。在现代英语中，经常存在含义非常相似的词，一个来自法语，一个来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语。“demand”（要求，法语来源）和“ask”（询问，盎格鲁-撒克逊语来源）就是例子。到1400年，在英格兰，官方文件开始用英语书写，英语已成为皇家法庭和议会的首选语言。

heading: 3.5 The Middle Ages
en: In the years leading up to 1400, Geoffrey Chaucer wrote a series of poems in English about a group of people going to Canterbury on a pilgrimage. The people decided to tell each other stories on the journey, and the poems describe the travellers and some of the stories they told. This collection of poems is called The Canterbury Tales. It was one of the first books to be printed by William Caxton, the first person in England to print books using a printing press. Many of the stories are still popular. Some have been made into plays and television programmes.
zh: 在1400年之前的岁月里，杰弗里·乔叟（Geoffrey Chaucer）用英语写了一系列关于一群前往坎特伯雷朝圣的人的诗歌。这些人决定在旅途中互相讲故事，这些诗歌描述了旅行者以及他们讲述的一些故事。这部诗集被称为《坎特伯雷故事集》（The Canterbury Tales）。它是威廉·卡克斯顿（William Caxton）印刷的最早的书籍之一，他是英格兰第一位使用印刷机印刷书籍的人。许多故事至今仍很受欢迎。有些已被改编成戏剧和电视节目。

heading: 3.5 The Middle Ages
en: In Scotland, many people continued to speak Gaelic and the Scots language also developed. A number of poets began to write in the Scots language. One example is John Barbour, who wrote The Bruce about the Battle of Bannockburn.
zh: 在苏格兰，许多人继续讲盖尔语，苏格兰语（Scots language）也随之发展。一些诗人开始用苏格兰语写作。一个例子是约翰·巴伯（John Barbour），他写了关于班诺克本战役（Battle of Bannockburn）的《布鲁斯》（The Bruce）。

heading: 3.5 The Middle Ages
en: The Middle Ages also saw a change in the type of buildings in Britain. Castles were built in many places in Britain and Ireland, partly for defence. Today many are in ruins, although some, such as Windsor and Edinburgh, are still in use. Great cathedrals – for example, Lincoln Cathedral – were also built, and many of these are still used for worship. Several of the cathedrals had windows of stained glass, telling stories about the Bible and Christian saints. The glass in York Minster is a famous example.
zh: 中世纪也见证了英国建筑类型的变化。城堡在英国和爱尔兰的许多地方建造，部分是为了防御。如今许多城堡已成废墟，尽管有些如温莎（Windsor）和爱丁堡（Edinburgh）仍在被使用。宏伟的大教堂——例如林肯大教堂（Lincoln Cathedral）——也被建造，其中许多至今仍用于礼拜。几座大教堂拥有彩色玻璃窗，讲述圣经和基督教圣徒的故事。约克大教堂（York Minster）的玻璃就是一个著名的例子。

heading: 3.5 The Middle Ages
en: During this period, England was an important trading nation. English wool became a very important export. People came to England from abroad to trade and also to work. Many had special skills, such as weavers from France, engineers from Germany, glass manufacturers from Italy and canal builders from Holland.
zh: 在这一时期，英格兰是一个重要的贸易国家。英国羊毛成为非常重要的出口商品。人们从国外来到英格兰进行贸易和工作。许多人拥有特殊技能，例如来自法国的织工、来自德国的工程师、来自意大利的玻璃制造商以及来自荷兰的运河建造者。

heading: 3.5 The Middle Ages
en: In 1455, a civil war was begun to decide who should be king of England. It was fought between the supporters of two families: the House of Lancaster and the House of York. This war was called the Wars of the Roses, because the symbol of Lancaster was a red rose and the symbol of York was a white rose. The war ended with the Battle of Bosworth Field in 1485. King Richard III of the House of York was killed in the battle and Henry Tudor, the leader of the House of Lancaster, became King Henry VII. Henry then married King Richard’s niece, Elizabeth of York, and united the two families. Henry was the first king of the House of Tudor. The symbol of the House of Tudor was a red rose with a white rose inside it as a sign that the Houses of York and Lancaster were now allies.
zh: 1455年，一场内战爆发，以决定谁应该成为英格兰国王。这场战争在两个家族的支持者之间进行：兰开斯特家族（House of Lancaster）和约克家族（House of York）。这场战争被称为玫瑰战争（Wars of the Roses），因为兰开斯特的象征是红玫瑰，而约克的象征是白玫瑰。战争于1485年的博斯沃思原野战役（Battle of Bosworth Field）结束。约克家族的理查三世国王（King Richard III）在战斗中阵亡，兰开斯特家族的领袖亨利·都铎（Henry Tudor）成为亨利七世国王（King Henry VII）。随后，亨利娶了理查三世的侄女伊丽莎白·约克（Elizabeth of York），统一了两个家族。亨利是都铎王朝（House of Tudor）的第一位国王。都铎王朝的象征是一朵红玫瑰，中间包含一朵白玫瑰，象征着约克家族和兰开斯特家族现在是盟友。

heading: 3.5 The Middle Ages
en:
• The wars that took place in the Middle Ages
• How Parliament began to develop
• The way that land ownership worked
• The effect of the Black Death
• The development of English language and culture
• The Wars of the Roses and the founding of the House of Tudor
zh:
• 中世纪发生的战争
• 议会如何开始发展
• 土地所有制的运作方式
• 黑死病的影响
• 英语和文化的发展
• 玫瑰战争和都铎王朝的建立

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
en: After his victory in the Wars of the Roses, Henry VII wanted to make sure that England remained peaceful and that his position as king was secure. He deliberately strengthened the central administration of England and reduced the power of the nobles. He was thrifty and built up the monarchy’s financial reserves. When he died, his son Henry VIII continued the policy of centralising power.
zh: 在玫瑰战争（Wars of the Roses）获胜后，亨利七世（Henry VII）希望确保英格兰保持和平，并巩固其作为国王的地位。他有意加强英格兰的中央行政机构，削弱贵族权力。他生活节俭，积累了王室的财政储备。他去世后，其子亨利八世（Henry VIII）继续推行集权政策。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
en: Henry VIII was most famous for breaking away from the Church of Rome and marrying six times.
zh: 亨利八世（Henry VIII）最著名的成就是脱离罗马教会，并六次结婚。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
subheading: THE SIX WIVES OF HENRY VIII
en: Catherine of Aragon – Catherine was a Spanish princess. She and Henry had a number of children but only one, Mary, survived. When Catherine was too old to give him another child, Henry decided to divorce her, hoping that another wife would give him a son to be his heir.
zh: 阿拉贡的凯瑟琳（Catherine of Aragon）——凯瑟琳是一位西班牙公主。她与亨利育有数名子女，但仅有一人玛丽（Mary）存活。当凯瑟琳年事已高，无法再为亨利生下子嗣时，亨利决定与她离婚，希望另一位妻子能为他生下一位继承王位的儿子。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
subheading: THE SIX WIVES OF HENRY VIII
en: Anne Boleyn – Anne Boleyn was English. She and Henry had one daughter, Elizabeth. Anne was unpopular in the country and was accused of taking lovers. She was executed at the Tower of London.
zh: 安妮·博林（Anne Boleyn）——安妮是英格兰人。她与亨利育有一女伊丽莎白（Elizabeth）。安妮在全国范围内不得人心，并被指控有外遇。她最终在伦敦塔被处决。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
subheading: THE SIX WIVES OF HENRY VIII
en: Jane Seymour – Henry married Jane after Anne’s execution. She gave Henry the son he wanted, Edward, but she died shortly after the birth.
zh: 简·西摩（Jane Seymour）——亨利在安妮被处决后娶了简。简为亨利生下了他梦寐以求的儿子爱德华（Edward），但她在分娩后不久便去世了。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
subheading: THE SIX WIVES OF HENRY VIII
en: Anne of Cleves – Anne was a German princess. Henry married her for political reasons but divorced her soon after.
zh: 克列夫斯的安妮（Anne of Cleves）——安妮是一位德国公主。亨利出于政治原因与她结婚，但不久后便与她离婚。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
subheading: THE SIX WIVES OF HENRY VIII
en: Catherine Howard – Catherine was a cousin of Anne Boleyn. She was also accused of taking lovers and executed.
zh: 凯瑟琳·霍华德（Catherine Howard）——凯瑟琳是安妮·博林（Anne Boleyn）的表亲。她也被指控有外遇并被处决。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
subheading: THE SIX WIVES OF HENRY VIII
en: Catherine Parr – Catherine was a widow who married Henry late in his life. She survived him and married again but died soon after.
zh: 凯瑟琳·帕尔（Catherine Parr）——凯瑟琳是一位寡妇，在亨利晚年与他结婚。她比亨利长寿，并再次结婚，但不久后便去世了。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
subheading: THE SIX WIVES OF HENRY VIII
en: To divorce his first wife, Henry needed the approval of the Pope. When the Pope refused, Henry established the Church of England. In this new Church, the king, not the Pope, would have the power to appoint bishops and order how people should worship.
zh: 为了与第一任妻子离婚，亨利需要教皇的批准。当教皇拒绝后，亨利建立了英格兰圣公会（the Church of England）。在这个新教会中，国王而非教皇将拥有任命主教和规定人们崇拜方式的权力。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
subheading: THE SIX WIVES OF HENRY VIII
en: At the same time the Reformation was happening across Europe. This was a movement against the authority of the Pope and the ideas and practices of the Roman Catholic Church. The Protestants formed their own churches. They read the Bible in their own languages instead of in Latin; they did not pray to saints or at shrines; and they believed that a person’s own relationship with God was more important than submitting to the authority of the Church. Protestant ideas gradually gained strength in England, Wales and Scotland during the 16th century.
zh: 与此同时，宗教改革（the Reformation）正在整个欧洲发生。这是一场反对教皇权威以及罗马天主教会理念和实践的運動。新教徒建立了自己的教会。他们用自己的语言阅读《圣经》，而不是拉丁语；他们不向圣人或圣地祈祷；并且他们认为个人与上帝的关系比服从教会的权威更为重要。在16世纪，新教思想逐渐在英格兰、威尔士和苏格兰获得力量。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
subheading: THE SIX WIVES OF HENRY VIII
en: In Ireland, however, attempts by the English to impose Protestantism (alongside efforts to introduce the English system of laws about the inheritance of land) led to rebellion from the Irish chieftains, and much brutal fighting followed.
zh: 然而，在爱尔兰，英国人试图强加新教（连同引入关于土地继承的英国法律体系）的努力导致了爱尔兰首领们的叛乱，随后发生了许多残酷的战斗。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
subheading: THE SIX WIVES OF HENRY VIII
en: During the reign of Henry VIII, Wales became formally united with England by the Act for the Government of Wales. The Welsh sent representatives to the House of Commons and the Welsh legal system was reformed.
zh: 在亨利八世统治期间，威尔士通过《威尔士政府法案》正式与英格兰联合。威尔士人向下议院（House of Commons）派遣代表，威尔士的法律体系也进行了改革。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
subheading: THE SIX WIVES OF HENRY VIII
en: Henry VIII was succeeded by his son Edward VI, who was strongly Protestant. During his reign, the Book of Common Prayer was written to be used in the Church of England. A version of this book is still used in some churches today. Edward died at the age of 15 after ruling for just over six years, and his half-sister Mary became queen. Mary was a devout Catholic and persecuted Protestants (for this reason, she became known as ‘Bloody Mary’). Mary also died after a short reign and the next monarch was her half-sister, Elizabeth, the daughter of Henry VIII and Anne Boleyn.
zh: 亨利八世（Henry VIII）由其子爱德华六世（Edward VI）继位，他是一位坚定的新教徒。在他的统治期间，《公祷书》（Book of Common Prayer）被编写出来，用于英格兰圣公会（the Church of England）。今天仍有一些教堂使用这本书的版本。爱德华在统治了六年多一点后，于15岁去世，他的同父异母姐姐玛丽成为女王。玛丽是一位虔诚的天主教徒，并迫害新教徒（因此她被称为“血腥玛丽”）。玛丽也在短暂的统治后去世，下一位君主是她的同父异母姐姐伊丽莎白，她是亨利八世和安妮·博林（Anne Boleyn）的女儿。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
en: Queen Elizabeth I was a Protestant. She re-established the Church of England as the official Church in England. Everyone had to attend their local church and there were laws about the type of religious services and the prayers which could be said, but Elizabeth did not ask about people’s real beliefs. She succeeded in finding a balance between the views of Catholics and the more extreme Protestants. In this way, she avoided any serious religious conflict within England. Elizabeth became one of the most popular monarchs in English history, particularly after 1588, when the English defeated the Spanish Armada (a large fleet of ships), which had been sent by Spain to conquer England and restore Catholicism.
zh: 伊丽莎白一世女王（Queen Elizabeth I）是一位新教徒。她重新确立英格兰圣公会为英格兰的官方教会。每个人都必须参加当地教堂的礼拜，并且有关于宗教仪式和祈祷内容的法律规定，但伊丽莎白并不询问人们的真实信仰。她成功地在天主教徒和更极端的新教徒之间找到了平衡。通过这种方式，她避免了英格兰境内任何严重的宗教冲突。伊丽莎白成为英国历史上最受欢迎的君主之一，特别是在1588年，当英国击败西班牙舰队（一支大型船队）之后，该舰队由西班牙派遣，旨在征服英格兰并恢复天主教。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
subheading: The Reformation in Scotland and Mary, Queen of Scots
en: Scotland had also been strongly influenced by Protestant ideas. In 1560, the predominantly Protestant Scottish Parliament abolished the authority of the Pope in Scotland and Roman Catholic religious services became illegal. A Protestant Church of Scotland with an leadership was established but, unlike in England, this was not a state Church.
zh: 苏格兰也深受新教思想的影响。1560年，主要由新教徒组成的苏格兰议会（Scottish Parliament）废除了教皇在苏格兰的权威，罗马天主教宗教仪式变为非法。建立了一个由长老领导的新教苏格兰教会（Church of Scotland），但与英格兰不同，它不是国教。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
subheading: The Reformation in Scotland and Mary, Queen of Scots
en: The queen of Scotland, Mary Stuart (often now called ‘Mary, Queen of Scots’) was a Catholic. She was only a week old when her father died and she became queen. Much of her childhood was spent in France. When she returned to Scotland, she was the centre of a power struggle between different groups. When her husband was murdered, Mary was suspected of involvement and fled to England. She gave her throne to her Protestant son, James VI of Scotland. Mary was Elizabeth I’s cousin and hoped that Elizabeth might help her, but Elizabeth suspected Mary of wanting to take over the English throne, and kept her a prisoner for 20 years. Mary was eventually executed, accused of plotting against Elizabeth I.
zh: 苏格兰女王玛丽·斯图亚特（Mary Stuart，现在常被称为“苏格兰的玛丽女王”）是一位天主教徒。她父亲去世时她仅一周大，随即成为女王。她的童年大部分时间在法国度过。当她回到苏格兰时，她成为了不同派系之间权力斗争的中心。当她的丈夫被谋杀后，玛丽涉嫌参与并逃往英格兰。她将王位让给了她的新教儿子、苏格兰的詹姆斯六世（James VI of Scotland）。玛丽是伊丽莎白一世的表亲，希望伊丽莎白能帮助她，但伊丽莎白怀疑玛丽企图夺取英国王位，并将她囚禁了20年。玛丽最终被处决，罪名是策划反对伊丽莎白一世。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
en: The Elizabethan period in England was a time of growing patriotism: a feeling of pride in being English. English explorers sought new trade routes and tried to expand British trade into the Spanish colonies in the Americas. Sir Francis Drake, one of the commanders in the defeat of the Spanish Armada, was one of the founders of England’s naval tradition. His ship, the Golden Hind, was one of the first to sail right around (‘circumnavigate’) the world. In Elizabeth I’s time, English settlers first began to colonise the eastern coast of America. This colonisation, particularly by people who disagreed with the religious views of the next two kings, greatly increased in the next century.
zh: 英格兰的伊丽莎白时代是一个爱国主义日益增长的时期：一种作为英国人的自豪感。英国探险家寻求新的贸易路线，并试图将英国贸易扩展到美洲的西班牙殖民地。弗朗西斯·德雷克爵士（Sir Francis Drake）是击败西班牙舰队的指挥官之一，也是英国海军传统的奠基人之一。他的船“黄金鹿号”（Golden Hind）是最早环绕（“环球航行”）世界的船只之一。在伊丽莎白一世时期，英国定居者开始在美国东海岸建立殖民地。这种殖民活动，特别是由那些不同意接下来两位国王宗教观点的人所推动的，在下一个世纪大大增加。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
en: The Elizabethan period is also remembered for the richness of its poetry and drama, especially the plays and poems of William Shakespeare.
zh: 伊丽莎白时代也因诗歌和戏剧的丰富性而被铭记，尤其是威廉·莎士比亚（William Shakespeare）的戏剧和诗歌。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
en: Shakespeare was born in Stratford-upon-Avon, England. He was a playwright and actor and wrote many poems and plays. His most famous plays include A Midsummer Night’s Dream , Hamlet , Macbeth and Romeo and Juliet . He also dramatised significant events from the past, but he did not focus solely on kings and queens. He was one of the first to portray ordinary Englishmen and women. Shakespeare had a great influence on the English language and invented many words that are still common today. Lines from his plays and poems which are often still quoted include:
zh: 莎士比亚出生于英格兰的埃文河畔斯特拉特福（Stratford-upon-Avon）。他是一位剧作家和演员，写了许多诗歌和戏剧。他最著名的戏剧包括《仲夏夜之梦》（A Midsummer Night’s Dream）、《哈姆雷特》（Hamlet）、《麦克白》（Macbeth）和《罗密欧与朱丽叶》（Romeo and Juliet）。他还改编了历史上重大事件，但他并不只关注国王和王后。他是首批描绘普通英国男女的作家之一。莎士比亚对英语产生了巨大影响，发明了许多至今仍常用的词汇。他的戏剧和诗歌中常被引用的名句包括：

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
en:
• Once more unto the breach ( Henry V )
• To be or not to be ( Hamlet )
• A rose by any other name ( Romeo and Juliet )
• All the world’s a stage ( As You Like It )
• The darling buds of May ( Sonnet 18 – Shall I Compare Thee To a Summer’s Day ).
zh:
• 再次冲向缺口（《亨利五世》）
• 生存还是毁灭（《哈姆雷特》）
• 玫瑰换个名字依然芬芳（《罗密欧与朱丽叶》）
• 全世界是一个舞台（《皆大欢喜》）
• 五月娇嫩的花蕾（《十四行诗第18首——我能否将你比作夏日》）。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
en: Many people regard Shakespeare as the greatest playwright of all time. His plays and poems are still performed and studied in Britain and other countries today. The Globe Theatre in London is a modern copy of the theatres in which his plays were first performed.
zh: 许多人认为莎士比亚是有史以来最伟大的剧作家。他的戏剧和诗歌至今仍在英国及其他国家上演和研究。伦敦的环球剧院（Globe Theatre）是根据他当时演出戏剧的剧场复制而成的现代建筑。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
en: Elizabeth I never married and so had no children of her own to inherit her throne. When she died in 1603 her heir was her cousin James VI of Scotland. He became King James I of England, Wales and Ireland but Scotland remained a separate country.
zh: 伊丽莎白一世（Elizabeth I）终身未嫁，因此没有亲生子女继承王位。1603年她去世后，她的继承人是她的表亲、苏格兰的詹姆斯六世（James VI of Scotland）。他成为英格兰、威尔士和爱尔兰的詹姆斯一世国王（King James I），但苏格兰仍是一个独立的国家。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
en: One achievement of King James’ reign was a new translation of the Bible into English. This translation is known as the ‘King James Version’ or the ‘Authorised Version’. It was not the first English Bible but is a version which continues to be used in many Protestant churches today.
zh: 詹姆斯一世统治期间的一项成就是将《圣经》新译为英文。这一译本被称为“钦定版”（King James Version）或“授权版”（Authorised Version）。它并非第一部英文《圣经》，但至今仍在许多新教教堂中使用。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
en: During this period, Ireland was an almost completely Catholic country. Henry VII and Henry VIII had extended English control outside the Pale and had established English authority over the whole country. Henry VIII took the title ‘King of Ireland’. English laws were introduced and local leaders were expected to follow the instructions of the Lord Lieutenants in Dublin.
zh: 在这一时期，爱尔兰几乎是一个完全天主教的国家。亨利七世（Henry VII）和亨利八世（Henry VIII）将英格兰的控制范围扩展到“帕尔”（Pale）之外，并确立了对整个国家的统治权威。亨利八世采用了“爱尔兰国王”的头衔。英格兰法律被引入，当地领袖被要求遵循都柏林总督（Lord Lieutenants）的指令。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
en: During the reigns of Elizabeth I and James I, many people in Ireland opposed rule by the Protestant government in England. There were a number of rebellions. The English government encouraged Scottish and English Protestants to settle in Ulster, the northern province of Ireland, taking over the land from Catholic landholders. These settlements were known as plantations. Many of the new settlers came from south-west Scotland and other land was given to companies based in London. James later organised similar plantations in several other parts of Ireland. This had serious longterm consequences for the history of England, Scotland and Ireland.
zh: 在伊丽莎白一世和詹姆斯一世统治期间，爱尔兰许多人反对英格兰新教政府的统治。发生了多次叛乱。英格兰政府鼓励苏格兰和英格兰的新教徒定居在爱尔兰北部的省份阿尔斯特（Ulster），从天主教土地所有者手中接管土地。这些定居点被称为“种植园”（plantations）。许多新定居者来自苏格兰西南部，其他土地则分配给位于伦敦的公司。后来詹姆斯一世在爱尔兰其他地区组织了类似的种植园活动。这对英格兰、苏格兰和爱尔兰的历史产生了严重的长期后果。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
en: Elizabeth I was very skilled at managing Parliament. During her reign, she was successful in balancing her wishes and views against those of the House of Lords and those of the House of Commons, which was increasingly Protestant in its views.
zh: 伊丽莎白一世非常擅长管理议会。在她的统治期间，她成功地在自己的意愿和观点与上议院（House of Lords）以及日益倾向于新教立场的下议院（House of Commons）的观点之间取得平衡。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
en: James I and his son Charles I were less skilled politically. Both believed in the ‘Divine Right of Kings’: the idea that the king was directly appointed by God to rule. They thought that the king should be able to act without having to seek approval from Parliament. When Charles I inherited the thrones of England, Wales, Ireland and Scotland, he tried to rule in line with this principle. When he could not get Parliament to agree with his religious and foreign policies, he tried to rule without Parliament at all. For 11 years, he found ways in which to raise money without Parliament’s approval but eventually trouble in Scotland meant that he had to recall Parliament.
zh: 詹姆斯一世（James I）和他的儿子查理一世（Charles I）在政治技巧上较为逊色。两人都信奉“君权神授”（Divine Right of Kings）：即国王由上帝直接任命来统治的理念。他们认为国王应能采取行动，而无需寻求议会的批准。当查理一世继承英格兰、威尔士、爱尔兰和苏格兰的王位时，他试图按照这一原则进行统治。当他无法让议会同意他的宗教和外交政策时，他试图完全绕过议会进行统治。在11年里，他找到了在不获得议会批准的情况下筹集资金的方法，但最终苏格兰的动乱迫使他重新召集议会。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
subheading: The beginning of the English civil war
en: Charles I wanted the worship of the Church of England to include more ceremony and introduced a revised Prayer Book. He tried to impose this Prayer Book on the Presbyterian Church in Scotland and this led to serious unrest. A Scottish army was formed and Charles could not find the money he needed for his own army without the help of Parliament. In 1640, he recalled Parliament to ask it for funds. Many in Parliament were Puritans, a group of Protestants who advocated strict and simple religious doctrine and worship. They did not agree with the king’s religious views and disliked his reforms of the Church of England. Parliament refused to give the king the money he asked for, even after the Scottish army invaded England.
zh: 查理一世希望英格兰圣公会的礼拜包含更多的仪式，并引入了一本修订版的《公祷书》（Prayer Book）。他试图将这本《公祷书》强加给苏格兰的长老会（Presbyterian Church），这导致了严重的动荡。一支苏格兰军队组建起来，而查理一世在没有议会帮助的情况下无法找到组建自己军队所需的资金。1640年，他重新召集议会以请求拨款。议会中的许多人属于清教徒（Puritans），这是一个主张严格且简朴的宗教教义和礼拜的新教团体。他们不同意国王的宗教观点，也不喜欢他对英格兰圣公会的改革。即使苏格兰军队入侵英格兰后，议会仍拒绝向国王提供他所要求的资金。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
subheading: The beginning of the English civil war
en: Another rebellion began in Ireland because the Roman Catholics in Ireland were afraid of the growing power of the Puritans. Parliament took this opportunity to demand control of the English army – a change that would have transferred substantial power from the king to Parliament. In response, Charles I entered the House of Commons and tried to arrest five parliamentary leaders, but they had been warned and were not there. (No monarch has set foot in the Commons since.) Civil war between the king and Parliament could not now be avoided and began in 1642. The country split into those who supported the king (the Cavaliers) and those who supported Parliament (the Roundheads).
zh: 爱尔兰爆发了另一起叛乱，因为爱尔兰的罗马天主教徒担心清教徒日益增长的力量。议会趁机要求控制英国军队——这一变化将把大量权力从国王转移至议会。作为回应，查理一世（Charles I）进入下议院，试图逮捕五名下议院议员领袖，但他们已收到警告并不在场。（此后没有任何君主踏足下议院。）国王与议会之间的内战已无法避免，并于1642年爆发。国家分裂为支持国王的一方（骑士党）和支持议会的一方（圆颅党）。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
subheading: Oliver Cromwell and the English Republic
en: The king’s army was defeated at the Battles of Marston Moor and Naseby. By 1646, it was clear that Parliament had won the war. Charles was held prisoner by the parliamentary army. He was still unwilling to reach any agreement with Parliament and in 1649 he was executed.
zh: 国王的军队在马尔斯顿荒原战役和纳斯比战役中战败。到1646年，议会赢得战争已成定局。查理被议会军俘虏。他仍不愿与议会达成任何协议，并于1649年被处决。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
subheading: Oliver Cromwell and the English Republic
en: England declared itself a republic, called the Commonwealth. It no longer had a monarch. For a time, it was not totally clear how the country would be governed. For now, the army was in control. One of its generals, Oliver Cromwell, was sent to Ireland, where the revolt which had begun in 1641 still continued and where there was still a Royalist army. Cromwell was successful in establishing the authority of the English Parliament but did this with such violence that even today Cromwell remains a controversial figure in Ireland.
zh: 英格兰宣布自己为共和国，称为英联邦。它不再有君主。在一段时间内，国家如何治理并不完全明确。目前，军队掌握控制权。其将领之一奥利弗·克伦威尔（Oliver Cromwell）被派往爱尔兰，那里自1641年开始的叛乱仍在继续，且仍有保王党军队存在。克伦威尔成功确立了英格兰议会的权威，但手段极其暴力，以至于至今克伦威尔在爱尔兰仍是一个极具争议的人物。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
subheading: Oliver Cromwell and the English Republic
en: The Scots had not agreed to the execution of Charles I and declared his son Charles II to be king. He was crowned king of Scotland and led a Scottish army into England. Cromwell defeated this army in the Battles of Dunbar and Worcester. Charles II escaped from Worcester, famously hiding in an oak tree on one occasion, and eventually fled to Europe. Parliament now controlled Scotland as well as England and Wales.
zh: 苏格兰人不同意处决查理一世，并宣布其子查理二世（Charles II）为国王。他在苏格兰加冕为王，并率领苏格兰军队进入英格兰。克伦威尔在多巴战役和伍斯特战役中击败这支军队。查理二世从伍斯特逃脱，曾有一次著名的躲藏在橡树上的经历，最终流亡欧洲。议会现在同时控制着苏格兰、英格兰和威尔士。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
subheading: Oliver Cromwell and the English Republic
en: After his campaign in Ireland and victory over Charles II at Worcester, Cromwell was recognised as the leader of the new republic. He was given the title of Lord Protector and ruled until his death in 1658. When Cromwell died, his son, Richard, became Lord Protector in his place but was not able to control the army or the government. Although Britain had been a republic for 11 years, without Oliver Cromwell there was no clear leader or system of government. Many people in the country wanted stability. People began to talk about the need for a king.
zh: 在爱尔兰战役中获胜并于伍斯特击败查理二世（Charles II）后，克伦威尔被公认为新共和国的领袖。他被授予护国公（Lord Protector）头衔，并执政至1658年去世。克伦威尔去世后，其子理查德继任护国公，但无法控制军队或政府。尽管英国已实行共和制11年，但在奥利弗·克伦威尔（Oliver Cromwell）死后，既无明确的领导人，也无稳定的政府体制。国内许多人渴望稳定，人们开始讨论恢复王位的必要性。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
en: In May 1660, Parliament invited Charles II to come back from exile in the Netherlands. He was crowned King Charles II of England, Wales, Scotland and Ireland. Charles II made it clear that he had ‘no wish to go on his travels again’. He understood that he could not always do as he wished but would sometimes need to reach agreement with Parliament. Generally, Parliament supported his policies. The Church of England again became the established official Church. Both Roman Catholics and Puritans were kept out of power.
zh: 1660年5月，议会邀请查理二世从荷兰流亡中返回。他加冕为英格兰、威尔士、苏格兰和爱尔兰国王查理二世。查理二世明确表示他“无意再次远行”。他明白自己不能随心所欲，有时需要与议会达成共识。总体而言，议会支持他的政策。英格兰圣公会（the Church of England）再次成为官方确立的教会。天主教徒和清教徒被排除在权力核心之外。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
en: During Charles II’s reign, in 1665, there was a major outbreak of plague in London. Thousands of people died, especially in poorer areas. The following year, a great fire destroyed much of the city, including many churches and St Paul’s Cathedral. London was rebuilt with a new St Paul’s, which was designed by a famous architect, Sir Christopher Wren. Samuel Pepys wrote about these events in a diary which was later published and is still read today.
zh: 在查理二世统治期间，1665年伦敦爆发大规模鼠疫。数千人死亡，贫困地区的伤亡尤为严重。次年，一场大火摧毁了城市的大部分区域，包括许多教堂和圣保罗大教堂（St Paul’s Cathedral）。伦敦得以重建，新的圣保罗大教堂由著名建筑师克里斯托弗·雷恩爵士（Sir Christopher Wren）设计。塞缪尔·佩皮斯（Samuel Pepys）在日记中记录了这些事件，该日记后来出版，至今仍被阅读。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
en: The Habeas Corpus Act became law in 1679. This was a very important piece of legislation which remains relevant today. Habeas corpus is Latin for ‘you must present the person in court’. The Act guaranteed that no one could be held prisoner unlawfully. Every prisoner has a right to a court hearing.
zh: 《人身保护法》（Habeas Corpus Act）于1679年成为法律。这是一项非常重要的立法，至今仍然具有现实意义。“Habeas corpus”是拉丁语，意为“你必须将人带到法庭”。该法案保证任何人不得被非法拘禁。每名囚犯都有权获得法庭听证。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
en: Charles II was interested in science. During his reign, the Royal Society was formed to promote ‘natural knowledge’. This is the oldest surviving scientific society in the world. Among its early members were Sir Edmund Halley, who successfully predicted the return of the comet now called Halley’s Comet, and Sir Isaac Newton.
zh: 查理二世对科学感兴趣。在他的统治期间，皇家学会成立，旨在促进“自然知识”的发展。这是世界上现存最古老的科学社团。其早期成员包括埃德蒙·哈雷爵士（Sir Edmund Halley），他成功预测了现在被称为哈雷彗星的彗星回归，以及艾萨克·牛顿爵士（Sir Isaac Newton）。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
en: Born in Lincolnshire, eastern England, Isaac Newton first became interested in science when he studied at Cambridge University. He became an important figure in the field. His most famous published work was Philosophiae Naturalis Principia Mathematica (‘Mathematical Principles of Natural Philosophy’), which showed how gravity applied to the whole universe. Newton also discovered that white light is made up of the colours of the rainbow. Many of his discoveries are still important for modern science.
zh: 艾萨克·牛顿出生于英格兰东部的林肯郡。他在剑桥大学学习期间首次对科学产生兴趣，并在此领域成为重要人物。他最著名的出版作品是《自然哲学的数学原理》（Philosophiae Naturalis Principia Mathematica），阐述了引力如何作用于整个宇宙。牛顿还发现白光由彩虹的各种颜色组成。他的许多发现对现代科学仍然至关重要。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
en: Charles II had no legitimate children. He died in 1685 and his brother, James, who was a Roman Catholic, became King James II in England, Wales and Ireland and King James VII of Scotland. James favoured Roman Catholics and allowed them to be army Officers, which an Act of Parliament had forbidden. He did not seek to reach agreements with Parliament and arrested some of the bishops of the Church of England. People in England worried that James wanted to make England a Catholic country once more. However, his heirs were his two daughters, who were both firmly Protestant, and people thought that this meant there would soon be a Protestant monarch again. Then, James’s wife had a son. Suddenly, it seemed likely that the next monarch would not be a Protestant after all.
zh: 查理二世没有合法子嗣。他于1685年去世，其弟詹姆斯（James）作为天主教徒，在英格兰、威尔士和爱尔兰即位为詹姆斯二世，在苏格兰即位为詹姆斯七世。詹姆斯偏爱天主教徒，允许他们担任军官，而议会法案曾禁止此举。他不寻求与议会达成共识，并逮捕了一些英格兰圣公会的主教。英格兰人担心詹姆斯希望使英格兰再次成为天主教国家。然而，他的继承人是他的两个女儿，她们都是坚定的新教徒，人们认为这意味着很快将有一位新教君主继位。随后，詹姆斯的妻子生下了一个儿子。突然之间，下一位君主很可能不再是新教徒。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
en: James II’s elder daughter, Mary, was married to her cousin William of Orange, the Protestant ruler of the Netherlands. In 1688, important Protestants in England asked William to invade England and proclaim himself king. When William reached England, there was no resistance. James fled to France and William took over the throne, becoming William III in England, Wales and Ireland, and William II of Scotland. William ruled jointly with Mary. This event was later called the ‘Glorious Revolution’ because there was no fighting in England and because it guaranteed the power of Parliament, ending the threat of a monarch ruling on his or her own as he or she wished. James II wanted to regain the throne and invaded Ireland with the help of a French army. William defeated James II at the Battle of the Boyne in Ireland in 1690, an event which is still celebrated by some in Northern Ireland today. William re-conquered Ireland and James fled back to France. Many restrictions were placed on the Roman Catholic Church in Ireland and Irish Catholics were unable to take part in the government.
zh: 詹姆斯二世的长女玛丽嫁给了她的表兄、荷兰的新教统治者奥兰治的威廉（William of Orange）。1688年，英格兰的重要新教徒邀请威廉入侵英格兰并宣布自己为国王。当威廉抵达英格兰时，未遇抵抗。詹姆斯逃往法国，威廉接管王位，在英格兰、威尔士和爱尔兰成为威廉三世，在苏格兰成为威廉二世。威廉与玛丽共同统治。这一事件后来被称为“光荣革命”，因为英格兰境内没有发生战斗，并且它保障了议会的权力，结束了君主随心所欲独自统治的威胁。詹姆斯二世试图夺回王位，并在法国军队的帮助下入侵爱尔兰。1690年，威廉在爱尔兰博因河战役（Battle of the Boyne）中击败詹姆斯二世，这一事件至今仍被北爱尔兰的一些人庆祝。威廉重新征服了爱尔兰，詹姆斯逃回法国。爱尔兰的天主教教会受到诸多限制，爱尔兰天主教徒无法参与政府事务。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
en: There was also support for James in Scotland. An attempt at an armed rebellion in support of James was quickly defeated at Killiecrankie. All Scottish clans were required formally to accept William as king by taking an oath. The MacDonalds of Glencoe were late in taking the oath and were all killed. The memory of this massacre meant some Scots distrusted the new government.
zh: 苏格兰也有支持詹姆斯的人。一次旨在支持詹姆斯的武装叛乱在基利克兰基（Killiecrankie）迅速被击败。所有苏格兰氏族都被要求通过宣誓正式接受威廉为国王。格伦科（Glencoe）的麦克唐纳家族（MacDonalds）因迟于宣誓而被全部杀害。这次大屠杀的记忆导致部分苏格兰人对新政府持不信任态度。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
en: Some continued to believe that James was the rightful king, particularly in Scotland. Some joined him in exile in France; others were secret supporters. James’ supporters became known as Jacobites.
zh: 一些人继续认为詹姆斯才是合法的国王，特别是在苏格兰。有些人随他流亡法国；其他人则是秘密支持者。詹姆斯的支持者被称为“雅各布派”（Jacobites）。

heading: 3.6 The Tudors and Stuarts
en:
• How and why religion changed during this period
• The importance of poetry and drama in the Elizabethan period
• About the involvement of Britain in Ireland
• The development of Parliament and the only period in history when England was a republic
• Why there was a restoration of the monarchy
• How the Glorious Revolution happened
zh:
• 这一时期宗教如何及为何发生变化
• 伊丽莎白时代诗歌和戏剧的重要性
• 英国在爱尔兰的参与情况
• 议会的发展以及历史上英格兰成为共和国的唯一时期
• 君主制得以恢复的原因
• 光荣革命是如何发生的

heading: 3.7 Early Britain
en: The first people to live in Britain were hunter-gatherers, in what we call the Stone Age. For much of the Stone Age, Britain was connected to the continent by a land bridge. People came and went, following the herds of deer and horses which they hunted. Britain only became permanently separated from the continent by the Channel about 10,000 years ago.
zh: 最早居住在英国的居民是狩猎采集者，处于我们所说的石器时代。在石器时代的很长一段时间里，英国通过陆桥与欧洲大陆相连。人们来来往往，跟随他们猎杀的鹿群和马群迁徙。直到大约一万年前，英国才因英吉利海峡而与大陆永久分离。

heading: 3.7 Early Britain
en: The first farmers arrived in Britain about 6,000 years ago. The ancestors of these first farmers probably came from south-east Europe. These people built houses, tombs and monuments on the land. One of these monuments, Stonehenge, still stands in what is now the English county of Wiltshire. Stonehenge was probably a special gathering place for seasonal ceremonies. Other Stone Age sites have also survived. Skara Brae on Orkney, off the north coast of Scotland, is the best preserved prehistoric village in northern Europe, and has helped archaeologists to understand more about how people lived near the end of the Stone Age.
zh: 第一批农民大约在6,000年前抵达英国。这些最早农民的祖先可能来自东南欧。这些人在这片土地上建造房屋、坟墓和纪念碑。其中一座纪念碑巨石阵（Stonehenge）至今仍矗立在现在的英格兰威尔特郡（Wiltshire）。巨石阵可能是用于季节性仪式的特殊聚会场所。其他石器时代遗址也得以保存下来。位于苏格兰北海岸外的奥克尼群岛（Orkney）上的斯卡拉布雷（Skara Brae）是北欧保存最完好的史前村庄，帮助考古学家更多地了解了人们在石器时代末期是如何生活的。

heading: 3.7 Early Britain
en: Around 4,000 years ago, people learned to make bronze. We call this period the Bronze Age. People lived in roundhouses and buried their dead in tombs called round barrows. The people of the Bronze Age were accomplished metalworkers who made many beautiful objects in bronze and gold, including tools, ornaments and weapons. The Bronze Age was followed by the Iron Age, when people learned how to make weapons and tools out of iron. People still lived in roundhouses, grouped together into larger settlements, and sometimes defended sites called hill forts. A very impressive hill fort can still be seen today at Maiden Castle, in the English county of Dorset. Most people were farmers, craft workers or warriors. The language they spoke was part of the Celtic language family. Similar languages were spoken across Europe in the Iron Age, and related languages are still spoken today in some parts of Wales, Scotland and Ireland. The people of the Iron Age had a sophisticated culture and economy. They made the first coins to be minted in Britain, some inscribed with the names of Iron Age kings. This marks the beginnings of British history.
zh: 大约4,000年前，人们学会了制造青铜。我们将这一时期称为青铜时代。人们居住在圆形房屋中，并将死者埋葬在称为圆形土墩墓（round barrows）的坟墓里。青铜时代的人们是技艺精湛的金属工匠，他们用青铜和黄金制作了许多精美的物品，包括工具、饰品和武器。青铜时代之后是铁器时代，人们学会了如何用铁制造武器和工具。人们仍然居住在圆形房屋中，聚集形成更大的定居点，有时还会建立被称为山堡（hill forts）的防御工事。今天仍可在英格兰多塞特郡（Dorset）的马登城堡（Maiden Castle）看到一座非常壮观的山堡。大多数人都是农民、手工艺人或战士。他们使用的语言属于凯尔特语系。在铁器时代，欧洲各地都使用类似的语言，如今威尔士、苏格兰和爱尔兰的部分地区仍在使用相关的语言。铁器时代的人们拥有 sophisticated 的文化和经济。他们铸造了英国最早发行的硬币，有些硬币上刻有铁器时代国王的名字。这标志着英国历史的开端。

heading: 3.7 Early Britain
en: Julius Caesar led a Roman invasion of Britain in 55 BC. This was unsuccessful and for nearly 100 years Britain remained separate from the Roman Empire. In AD 43 the Emperor Claudius led the Roman army in a new invasion. This time, there was resistance from some of the British tribes but the Romans were successful in occupying almost all of Britain. One of the tribal leaders who fought against the Romans was Boudicca, the queen of the Iceni in what is now eastern England. She is still remembered today and there is a statue of her on Westminster Bridge in London, near the Houses of Parliament.
zh: 尤利乌斯·凯撒（Julius Caesar）于公元前55年率领罗马军队入侵英国。这次入侵以失败告终，在随后的近100年里，英国一直与罗马帝国分离。公元43年，克劳狄乌斯皇帝（Emperor Claudius）率领罗马军队发动了新的入侵。这一次，尽管部分不列颠部落进行了抵抗，但罗马人成功占领了几乎整个英国。一位反抗罗马人的部落领袖是布狄卡（Boudicca），她是现今英格兰东部伊切尼族（Iceni）的女王。她至今仍被铭记，在伦敦威斯敏斯特桥（Westminster Bridge）上有一座她的雕像，靠近议会大厦（Houses of Parliament）。

heading: 3.7 Early Britain
en: Areas of what is now Scotland were never conquered by the Romans, and the Emperor Hadrian built a wall in the north of England to keep out the Picts (ancestors of the Scottish people). Included in the wall were a number of forts. Parts of Hadrian’s Wall, including the forts of Housesteads and Vindolanda, can still be seen. It is a popular area for walkers and is a UNESCO (United Nations Education, Scientific and Cultural Organization) World Heritage Site.
zh: 现今苏格兰的部分地区从未被罗马人征服，哈德良皇帝（Emperor Hadrian）在英格兰北部修建了一道墙以阻挡皮克特人（Picts，苏格兰人的祖先）。这道墙包含若干堡垒。哈德良长城（Hadrian’s Wall）的部分遗迹，包括豪斯特兹（Housesteads）和温德罗兰达（Vindolanda）堡垒，至今仍可见。这里是徒步旅行者的热门区域，也是联合国教育、科学及文化组织（UNESCO）的世界遗产地。

heading: 3.7 Early Britain
en: The Romans remained in Britain for 400 years. They built roads and public buildings, created a structure of law, and introduced new plants and animals. It was during the 3rd and 4th centuries AD that the first Christian communities began to appear in Britain.
zh: 罗马人在不列颠停留了400年。他们修建道路和公共建筑，建立了法律体系，并引入了新的动植物。公元3世纪至4世纪期间，英国出现了最早的基督教社区。

heading: 3.7 Early Britain
en: The Roman army left Britain in AD 410 to defend other parts of the Roman Empire and never returned. Britain was again invaded by tribes from northern Europe: the Jutes, the Angles and the Saxons. The languages they spoke are the basis of modern-day English. Battles were fought against these invaders but, by about AD 600, Anglo-Saxon kingdoms were established in Britain. These kingdoms were mainly in what is now England. The burial place of one of the kings was at Sutton Hoo in modern Suffolk. This king was buried with treasure and armour, all placed in a ship which was then covered by a mound of earth. Parts of the west of Britain, including much of what is now Wales, and Scotland, remained free of Anglo-Saxon rule.
zh: 罗马军队于公元410年离开不列颠，去保卫罗马帝国的其他地区，从此再也没有回来。不列颠再次遭到来自北欧部落的入侵：朱特人、盎格鲁人和撒克逊人。他们所说的语言构成了现代英语的基础。虽然曾与这些入侵者进行过战斗，但到公元600年左右，盎格鲁-撒克逊王国在不列颠建立起来。这些王国主要位于现在的英格兰地区。其中一位国王的墓葬位于现代萨福克郡的萨顿胡。这位国王与财宝和盔甲一同下葬，所有物品都放置在一艘船中，然后用土堆覆盖。不列颠西部部分地区，包括现在威尔士的大部分地区以及苏格兰，仍然不受盎格鲁-撒克逊统治。

heading: 3.7 Early Britain
en: The Anglo-Saxons were not Christians when they first came to Britain but, during this period, missionaries came to Britain to preach about Christianity. Missionaries from Ireland spread the religion in the north. The most famous of these were St Patrick, who would become the patron saint of Ireland and St Columba, who founded a monastery on the island of Iona, off the coast of what is now Scotland. St Augustine led missionaries from Rome, who spread Christianity in the south. St Augustine became the first Archbishop of Canterbury.
zh: 盎格鲁-撒克逊人最初来到不列颠时并非基督徒，但在此期间，传教士来到不列颠传播基督教。来自爱尔兰的传教士在北部地区传播该宗教。其中最著名的是圣帕特里克（St Patrick），他后来成为爱尔兰的主保圣人；以及圣科伦巴（St Columba），他在现今苏格兰海岸外的伊奥纳岛建立了一座修道院。圣奥古斯丁（St Augustine）率领来自罗马的传教士，在南部地区传播基督教。圣奥古斯丁成为坎特伯雷大主教的第一人。

heading: 3.7 Early Britain
en: The Vikings came from Denmark and Norway. They first visited Britain in AD 789 to raid coastal towns and take away goods and slaves. Then, they began to stay and form their own communities in the east of England and Scotland. The Anglo-Saxon kingdoms in England united under King Alfred the Great, who defeated the Vikings. Many of the Viking invaders stayed in Britain – especially in the east and north of England, in an area known as the Danelaw (many places names there, such as Grimsby and Scunthorpe, come from the Viking languages). The Viking settlers mixed with local communities and some converted to Christianity.
zh: 维京人来自丹麦和挪威。他们于公元789年首次访问不列颠，袭击沿海城镇并掠夺货物和奴隶。随后，他们开始定居并在英格兰东部和苏格兰形成自己的社区。英格兰的盎格鲁-撒克逊王国在阿尔弗雷德大帝（King Alfred the Great）的领导下联合起来，击败了维京人。许多维京入侵者留在了不列颠——尤其是在英格兰东部和北部，该地区被称为丹洛区（Danelaw，该地区的许多地名，如格里姆斯比和斯卡伯勒，源自维京语言）。维京定居者与当地社区融合，其中一些人皈依了基督教。

heading: 3.7 Early Britain
en: Anglo-Saxon kings continued to rule what is now England, except for a short period when there were Danish kings. The first of these was Cnut, also called Canute.
zh: 盎格鲁-撒克逊国王继续统治现今的英格兰地区，除了丹麦国王短暂统治的一段时期。其中第一位是克努特（Cnut），也被称为卡努特（Canute）。

heading: 3.7 Early Britain
en: In the north, the threat of attack by Vikings had encouraged the people to unite under one king, Kenneth MacAlpin. The term Scotland began to be used to describe that country.
zh: 在北方，维京人袭击的威胁促使人们团结在一位国王肯尼思·麦克阿尔平（Kenneth MacAlpin）的领导下。苏格兰（Scotland）这一名称开始被用来指代这个国家。

heading: 3.7 Early Britain
en: In 1066, an invasion led by William, the Duke of Normandy (in what is now northern France), defeated Harold, the Saxon king of England, at the Battle of Hastings. Harold was killed in the battle. William became king of England and is known as William the Conqueror. The battle is commemorated in a great piece of embroidery, known as the Bayeux Tapestry, which can still be seen in France today.
zh: 1066年，由诺曼底公爵威廉（William, the Duke of Normandy，位于现今法国北部）领导的入侵在黑斯廷斯战役（Battle of Hastings）中击败了英格兰的撒克逊国王哈罗德（Harold）。哈罗德在战斗中阵亡。威廉成为英格兰国王，被称为征服者威廉（William the Conqueror）。这场战役被记录在一件著名的刺绣作品中，即贝叶挂毯（Bayeux Tapestry），至今仍在法国展出。

heading: 3.7 Early Britain
en: The Norman Conquest was the last successful foreign invasion of England and led to many changes in government and social structures in England. Norman French, the language of the new ruling class, influenced the development of the English language as we know it today. Initially the Normans also conquered Wales, but the Welsh gradually won territory back. The Scots and the Normans fought on the border between England and Scotland; the Normans took over some land on the border but did not invade Scotland.
zh: 诺曼征服（the Norman Conquest）是英格兰最后一次成功的外国入侵，并导致了英格兰政府和社会结构的许多变化。新统治阶级使用的诺曼法语影响了我们今天所知的英语的发展。起初，诺曼人也征服了威尔士，但威尔士人逐渐夺回了部分领土。苏格兰人与诺曼人在英格兰与苏格兰的边境地区交战；诺曼人占领了边境的一些土地，但并未入侵苏格兰。

heading: 3.7 Early Britain
en: William sent people all over England to draw up lists of all the towns and villages. The people who lived there, who owned the land and what animals they owned were also listed. This was called the Domesday Book. It still exists today and gives a picture of society in England just after the Norman Conquest.
zh: 威廉派人走遍英格兰，编制所有城镇和村庄的清单。居住在那里的人、土地所有者以及他们拥有的动物也被列入清单。这被称为末日审判书（Domesday Book）。它至今仍存在，描绘了诺曼征服后不久英格兰社会的景象。

heading: 3.7 Early Britain
en:
• The history of the UK before the Romans
• The impact of the Romans on British society
• The different groups that invaded after the Romans
• The importance of the Norman invasion in 1066
zh:
• 罗马人到来之前的英国历史
• 罗马人对不列颠社会的影响
• 罗马人之后入侵的不同群体
• 1066年诺曼入侵的重要性

heading: 4.1 Chapter 4: A modern, thriving society
en: IN THIS CHAPTER you will learn about the population and culture of the UK. The start of the chapter shows you where the major cities of the UK are. You should be sure you can identify the various cities, such as Leeds and Bradford, confidently. Because the UK is a multicultural country you will also have to know the sizes of the different ethnic and religious groups in the UK, as well as their main festivals.
zh: 在本章中，您将了解英国的人口和文化。本章开头介绍了英国主要城市的位置。您应确保能够自信地识别利兹（Leeds）和布拉德福德（Bradford）等各类城市。由于英国是一个多元文化国家，您还需要了解英国不同族裔和宗教群体的人口规模，以及他们的主要节日。

heading: 4.1 Chapter 4: A modern, thriving society
en: The chapter focuses on British culture after that and you should make sure you know who each of the people described are and what they have achieved. Britain’s recent sporting success at the Olympics features heavily so focus on who won medals and for what as well as the general information about sport in the UK. Make sure you familiarise yourself with the poems, films, books and other works listed too, as well as the artists, composers, architects, authors, poets and other famous people. You should also familiarise yourself with the extracts of poems provided and be able to recognise the famous landmarks at the end of the chapter.
zh: 随后本章将聚焦于英国文化，您应确保了解每位被描述人物的身份及其成就。英国在奥运会上的近期体育成功是重点内容，因此请重点关注获奖者及其获奖项目，以及关于英国体育的概况信息。请务必熟悉列出的诗歌、电影、书籍及其他作品，以及艺术家、作曲家、建筑师、作家、诗人和其他著名人物。您还应熟悉提供的诗歌节选，并能够识别本章末尾提到的著名地标。

heading: 4.1 Chapter 4: A modern, thriving society
en: IN THIS CHAPTER THERE IS INFORMATION ABOUT :
zh: 本章包含以下信息：

heading: 4.1 Chapter 4: A modern, thriving society
en:
• The cities and population of the UK
• Religious festivals of the UK
• Popular sports and famous sportsmen and women
• The development of music, including famous composers
• British theatre and cinema through the ages
• British artists and architects
• Famous British poets and authors
• Sections of famous British poems
• British comedy and leisure activities
• Famous British landmarks
zh:
• 英国的城市与人口
• 英国的宗教节日
• 流行运动及著名男女运动员
• 音乐的发展，包括著名作曲家
• 历代英国戏剧与电影
• 英国艺术家与建筑师
• 著名英国诗人与作家
• 著名英国诗歌节选
• 英国喜剧与休闲活动
• 著名英国地标

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: Music is an important part of British culture, with a rich and varied heritage. It ranges from classical music to modern pop. There are many different venues and musical events that take place across the UK.
zh: 音乐是英国文化的重要组成部分，拥有丰富多样的遗产。其范围从古典音乐到现代流行音乐。在英国各地有许多不同的演出场地和音乐活动。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: The Proms is an eight-week summer season of orchestral classical music that takes place in various venues, including the Royal Albert Hall in London. It has been organised by the British Broadcasting Corporation (BBC) since 1927. The Last Night of the Proms is the most well-known concert and (along with others in the series) is broadcast on television.
zh: 逍遥音乐节（The Proms）是一个为期八周的夏季管弦乐古典音乐季，在包括伦敦皇家阿尔伯特音乐厅在内的多个场地举行。自1927年以来，该活动由英国广播公司（BBC）组织。逍遥音乐节最后一晚（The Last Night of the Proms）是最著名的音乐会，并与系列中的其他音乐会一起通过电视播出。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: Classical music has been popular in the UK for many centuries. Henry Purcell (1659–95) was the organist at Westminster Abbey. He wrote church music, operas and other pieces, and developed a British style distinct from that elsewhere in Europe. He continues to be influential on British composers.
zh: 古典音乐在英国已流行数个世纪。亨利·普赛尔（Henry Purcell，1659–1695）是威斯敏斯特教堂的管风琴师。他创作了教堂音乐、歌剧及其他作品，并发展出一种区别于欧洲其他地区的英国风格。他对英国作曲家至今仍具有深远影响。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: The German-born composer George Frederick Handel (1695–1759) spent many years in the UK and became a British citizen in 1727. He wrote the Water Music for King George I and Music for the Royal Fireworks for his son, George II. Both these pieces continue to be very popular. Handel also wrote an oratorio, Messiah , which is sung regularly by choirs, often at Easter time.
zh: 出生于德国的作曲家乔治·弗里德里希·亨德尔（George Frederick Handel，1695–1759）在英国生活多年，并于1727年成为英国公民。他为乔治一世国王创作了《水上音乐》，并为乔治二世创作了《皇家烟火音乐》。这两部作品至今仍广受欢迎。亨德尔还创作了一部清唱剧《弥赛亚》（Messiah），合唱团经常在复活节期间演唱这部作品。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: More recently, important composers include Gustav Holst (1874–1934) , whose work includes The Planets , a suite of pieces themed around the planets of the solar system. He adapted Jupiter , part of the Planets suite, as the tune for I vow to thee my country , a popular hymn in British churches.
zh: 近年来，重要的作曲家包括古斯塔夫·霍尔斯特（Gustav Holst，1874–1934），其作品包括《行星组曲》（The Planets），这是一套以太阳系行星为主题的乐曲集。他将《行星组曲》中的《木星》改编为歌曲《我向你宣誓，我的祖国》（I vow to thee my country）的旋律，这首歌在英国教堂中广为传唱。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: Sir Edward Elgar (1857–1934) was born in Worcester, England. His best-known work is probably the Pomp and Circumstance Marches. March No 1 (Land of Hope and Glory) is usually played at the Last Night of the Proms at the Royal Albert Hall.
zh: 爱德华·埃尔加爵士（Sir Edward Elgar，1857–1934）出生于英格兰伍斯特。他最著名的作品可能是《庄严与荣耀进行曲》（Pomp and Circumstance Marches）。其中第一号进行曲（《希望与荣耀的土地》）通常在皇家阿尔伯特音乐厅举办的逍遥音乐节最后一晚演奏。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: Ralph Vaughan Williams (1872–1958) wrote music for orchestras and choirs. He was strongly influenced by traditional English folk music.
zh: 拉尔夫·沃恩·威廉斯（Ralph Vaughan Williams，1872–1958）为管弦乐团和合唱团创作音乐。他深受传统英国民间音乐的影响。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: Sir William Walton (1902–83) wrote a wide range of music, from film scores to opera. He wrote marches for the coronations of King George VI and Queen Elizabeth II but his best-known works are probably Façade , which became a ballet, and Balthazar’s Feast , which is intended to be sung by a large choir.
zh: 威廉·沃尔顿爵士（Sir William Walton，1902–1983）创作了范围广泛的作品，从电影配乐到歌剧均有涉猎。他为乔治六世国王和伊丽莎白二世女王的加冕典礼创作了进行曲，但他最著名的作品可能是《门面》（Façade），该作品后来被改编为芭蕾舞剧，以及《巴尔萨扎的盛宴》（Balthazar’s Feast），后者旨在由大型合唱团演唱。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: Benjamin Britten (1913–76) is best known for his operas, which include Peter Grimes and Billy Budd . He also wrote A Young Person’s Guide to the Orchestra , which is based on a piece of music by Purcell and introduces the listener to the various different sections of an orchestra. He founded the Aldeburgh festival in Suffolk, which continues to be a popular music event of international importance.
zh: 本杰明·布里顿（Benjamin Britten，1913–1976）最为人熟知的是他的歌剧作品，包括《彼得·格赖姆斯》（Peter Grimes）和《比利·巴德》（Billy Budd）。他还创作了《青年管弦乐队指南》（A Young Person’s Guide to the Orchestra），该作品基于普赛尔的一段音乐，旨在向听众介绍管弦乐团的各个不同声部。他在萨福克郡创立了奥尔德堡音乐节（Aldeburgh festival），该音乐节至今仍是具有重要国际影响力的热门音乐活动。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: Other types of popular music, including folk music, jazz, pop and music, have flourished in Britain since the 20th century. Britain has had an impact on popular music around the world, due to the wide use of the English language, the UK’s cultural links with many countries, and British capacity for invention and innovation. Since the 1960s, British pop music has made one of the most important cultural contributions to life in the UK. Bands including The Beatles and The Rolling Stones continue to have an influence on music both here and abroad. British pop music has continued to innovate – for example, the Punk movement of the late 1970s, and the trend towards boy and girl bands in the 1990s.
zh: 自20世纪以来，包括民间音乐、爵士乐、流行音乐在内的多种流行音乐形式在英国蓬勃发展。由于英语的广泛使用、英国与许多国家的文化联系以及英国的创新创造能力，英国对全球流行音乐产生了深远影响。自20世纪60年代以来，英国流行音乐为英国人的生活做出了最重要的文化贡献之一。披头士乐队（The Beatles）和滚石乐队（The Rolling Stones）等乐队至今仍对国内外的音乐产生影响。英国流行音乐持续创新——例如20世纪70年代末的朋克运动，以及20世纪90年代男孩和女孩乐队的潮流。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: There are many large venues that host music events throughout the year, such as: Wembley Stadium; The O2 in Greenwich, south-east London; and the Scottish Exhibition and Conference Centre (SECC) in Glasgow.
zh: 全年有许多大型场馆举办音乐活动，例如：温布利体育场；位于伦敦东南部格林威治的O2体育馆；以及格拉斯哥的苏格兰展览会议中心（SECC）。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: Festival season takes place across the UK every summer, with major events in various locations. Famous festivals include Glastonbury, the Isle of Wight Festival and the V Festival. Many bands and solo artists, both well-known and up-and-coming, perform at these events.
zh: 每年夏季，英国各地都会举办音乐节季，主要活动分布在各个地点。著名的音乐节包括格拉斯顿伯里音乐节、怀特岛音乐节和V音乐节。许多知名和新兴的乐队及独奏艺术家都会在这些活动中演出。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: The National Eisteddfod of Wales is an annual cultural festival which includes music, dance, art and original performances largely in Welsh. It includes a number of important competitions for Welsh poetry.
zh: 威尔士国家埃斯特福德（Eisteddfod）是一年一度的文化节庆，包含音乐、舞蹈、艺术以及主要以威尔士语呈现的原创表演。其中设有若干重要的威尔士诗歌竞赛。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: The Mercury Music Prize is awarded each September for the best album from the UK and Ireland. The Brit Awards is an annual event that gives awards in a range of categories, such as best British group and best British solo artist.
zh: 水星音乐奖（Mercury Music Prize）每年九月颁发，授予来自英国和爱尔兰的最佳专辑。全英音乐奖（Brit Awards）是一项年度活动，在多个类别中颁发奖项，例如最佳英国组合和最佳英国独唱艺人。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: There are theatres in most towns and cities throughout the UK, ranging from the large to the small. They are an important part of local communities and often show both professional and amateur productions. London’s West End, also known as ‘Theatreland’, is particularly well known. The Mousetrap , a murder-mystery play by Dame Agatha Christie, has been running in the West End since 1952 and has had the longest initial run of any show in history.
zh: 在英国各地的城镇和城市大多都有剧院，规模从大型到小型不等。它们是当地社区的重要组成部分，通常既上演专业制作，也上演业余演出。伦敦西区（West End），又称“剧院区”（Theatreland），尤为著名。阿加莎·克里斯蒂女爵士（Dame Agatha Christie）创作的谋杀悬疑剧《捕鼠器》（The Mousetrap）自1952年起一直在西区演出，拥有历史上任何剧目最长的首演期记录。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: There is also a strong tradition of musical theatre in the UK. In the 19th century, Gilbert and Sullivan wrote comic operas, often making fun of popular culture and politics. These operas include HMS Pinafore , The Pirates of Penzance and The Mikado . Gilbert and Sullivan’s work is still often staged by professional and amateur groups. More recently, Andrew Lloyd Webber has written the music for shows which have been popular throughout the world, including, in collaboration with Tim Rice, Jesus Christ Superstar and Evita , and also Cats and The Phantom of the Opera .
zh: 英国在音乐剧方面也有深厚的传统。在19世纪，吉尔伯特（Gilbert）与沙利文（Sullivan）创作了喜剧歌剧，常常讽刺流行文化和政治。这些歌剧包括《皮纳福号军舰》（HMS Pinafore）、《彭赞斯的海盗》（The Pirates of Penzance）和《天皇》（The Mikado）。吉尔伯特与沙利文的作品至今仍常被专业团体和业余团体搬上舞台。近年来，安德鲁·劳埃德·韦伯（Andrew Lloyd Webber）为一些在全世界广受欢迎的音乐剧谱曲，包括与蒂姆·莱斯（Tim Rice）合作的《耶稣基督万世巨星》（Jesus Christ Superstar）和《艾薇塔》（Evita），以及《猫》（Cats）和《歌剧魅影》（The Phantom of the Opera）。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: One British tradition is the pantomime. Many theatres produce a pantomime at Christmas time. They are based on fairy stories and are light-hearted plays with music and comedy, enjoyed by family audiences. One of the traditional characters is the Dame, a woman played by a man. There is often also a pantomime horse or cow played by two actors in the same costume.
zh: 英国的一项传统是圣诞童话剧（pantomime）。许多剧院在圣诞节期间会制作圣诞童话剧。这些剧目基于童话故事，是配有音乐和喜剧元素的轻松戏剧，深受家庭观众喜爱。其中一个传统角色是“达姆”（Dame），由男性扮演的女性角色。通常还有一匹由两名演员穿着同一套服装扮演的童话剧马或牛。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: The Edinburgh Festival takes place in Edinburgh, Scotland, every summer. It is a series of different arts and cultural festivals, with the biggest and most well-known being the Edinburgh Festival Fringe (‘the Fringe’). The Fringe is a showcase of mainly theatre and comedy performances. It often shows experimental work.
zh: 爱丁堡艺术节（Edinburgh Festival）每年夏季在苏格兰的爱丁堡举行。这是一系列不同的艺术和文化节日，其中最大且最著名的是爱丁堡边缘艺术节（Edinburgh Festival Fringe，简称“边缘艺术节”）。边缘艺术节主要展示戏剧和喜剧表演，经常呈现实验性作品。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: The Laurence Olivier Awards take place annually at different venues in London. There are a variety of categories, including best director, best actor and best actress. The awards are named after the British actor Sir Laurence Olivier, later Lord Olivier, who was best known for his roles in various Shakespeare plays.
zh: 劳伦斯·奥利弗奖（Laurence Olivier Awards）每年在伦敦的不同场地举行。奖项类别多样，包括最佳导演、最佳男演员和最佳女演员。该奖项以英国演员劳伦斯·奥利弗爵士（Sir Laurence Olivier）命名，他后来被封为奥利弗勋爵（Lord Olivier），因在多部莎士比亚戏剧中的角色而闻名。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: During the Middle Ages, most art had a religious theme, particularly wall paintings in churches and illustrations in religious books. Much of this was lost after the Protestant Reformation but wealthy families began to collect other paintings and sculptures. Many of the painters working in Britain in the 16th and 17th centuries were from abroad – for example, Hans Holbein and Sir Anthony Van Dyck. British artists, particularly those painting portraits and landscapes, became well known from the 18th century onwards.
zh: 在中世纪（the Middle Ages），大多数艺术作品具有宗教主题，特别是教堂的壁画和宗教书籍中的插图。这些作品在新教宗教改革（Protestant Reformation）后大多遗失，但富裕家庭开始收藏其他绘画和雕塑。16和17世纪在英国工作的许多画家来自国外——例如汉斯·霍尔拜因（Hans Holbein）和安东尼·范·戴克爵士（Sir Anthony Van Dyck）。从18世纪开始，英国艺术家，尤其是那些绘制肖像画和风景画的艺术家，变得广为人知。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: Works by British and international artists are displayed in galleries across the UK. Some of the most well-known galleries are The National Gallery, Tate Britain and Tate Modern in London, the National Museum in Cardiff, and the National Gallery of Scotland in Edinburgh.
zh: 英国和国际艺术家的作品在英国各地的画廊中展出。一些最著名的画廊包括伦敦的国家美术馆（The National Gallery）、泰特不列颠美术馆（Tate Britain）和泰特现代美术馆（Tate Modern），加的夫的威尔士国家博物馆（National Museum in Cardiff），以及爱丁堡的苏格兰国家美术馆（National Gallery of Scotland）。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: The Turner Prize was established in 1984 and celebrates contemporary art. It was named after Joseph Turner. Four works are shortlisted every year and shown at Tate Britain before the winner is announced. The Turner Prize is recognised as one of the most prestigious visual art awards in Europe. Previous winners include Damien Hirst and Richard Wright.
zh: 透纳奖（Turner Prize）于1984年设立，旨在表彰当代艺术。该奖项以约瑟夫·透纳（Joseph Turner）命名。每年有四件作品入围，并在获奖者公布前于泰特不列颠美术馆展出。透纳奖被公认为欧洲最具声望的视觉艺术奖项之一。往届获奖者包括达米恩·赫斯特（Damien Hirst）和理查德·赖特（Richard Wright）。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: Thomas Gainsborough (1727–88) was a portrait painter who often painted people in country or garden scenery.
zh: 托马斯·庚斯博罗（Thomas Gainsborough，1727–88）是一位肖像画家，常将人物描绘在乡村或花园风景中。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: David Allan (1744–96) was a Scottish painter who was best known for painting portraits. One of his most famous works is called The Origin of Painting .
zh: 大卫·艾伦（David Allan，1744–96）是一位苏格兰画家，以绘制肖像画而闻名。他最著名的作品之一名为《绘画的起源》（The Origin of Painting）。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: Joseph Turner (1775–1851) was an influential landscape painter in a modern style. He is considered the artist who raised the profile of landscape painting.
zh: 约瑟夫·特纳（Joseph Turner，1775–1851）是一位具有影响力的现代风格风景画家。他被认为是提升了风景画地位的艺术大师。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: John Constable (1776–1837) was a landscape painter most famous for his works of Dedham Vale on the Suffolk–Essex border in the east of England.
zh: 约翰·康斯特布尔（John Constable，1776–1837）是一位风景画家，以描绘英格兰东部萨福克郡与埃塞克斯郡边境的迪德姆山谷（Dedham Vale）作品而闻名。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: The Pre-Raphaelites were an important group of artists in the second half of the 19th century. They painted detailed pictures on religious or literary themes in bright colours. The group included Holman Hunt, Dante Gabriel Rossetti and Sir John Millais.
zh: 前拉斐尔派兄弟会（Pre-Raphaelites）是19世纪下半叶重要的艺术家团体。他们以鲜艳的色彩绘制宗教或文学题材的精细画作。该团体包括霍尔曼·亨特（Holman Hunt）、但丁·加布里埃尔·罗塞蒂（Dante Gabriel Rossetti）和约翰·米莱斯爵士（Sir John Millais）。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: Sir John Lavery (1856–1941) was a very successful Northern Irish portrait painter. His work included painting the Royal Family.
zh: 约翰·拉弗蒂爵士（Sir John Lavery，1856–1941）是一位非常成功的北爱尔兰肖像画家。他的作品包括为英国王室成员画像。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: Henry Moore (1898–1986) was an English sculptor and artist. He is best known for his large bronze abstract sculptures.
zh: 亨利·摩尔（Henry Moore，1898–1986）是一位英国雕塑家和艺术家。他以其大型青铜抽象雕塑作品而最为人所知。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: John Petts (1914–91) was a Welsh artist, best known for his engravings and stained glass.
zh: 约翰·佩茨（John Petts，1914–1991）是一位威尔士艺术家，以他的版画和彩色玻璃窗作品而闻名。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: Lucian Freud (1922–2011) was a German-born British artist. He is best known for his portraits.
zh: 卢西安·弗洛伊德（Lucian Freud，1922–2011）是一位出生于德国的英国艺术家。他以其肖像画而最为人所知。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: David Hockney (1937–) was an important contributor to the ‘pop art’ movement of the 1960s and continues to be influential today.
zh: 大卫·霍克尼（David Hockney，1937–）是20世纪60年代“波普艺术”运动的重要贡献者，至今仍然具有影响力。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: The architectural heritage of the UK is rich and varied. In the Middle Ages, great cathedrals and churches were built, many of which still stand today. Examples are the cathedrals in Durham, Lincoln, Canterbury and Salisbury. The White Tower in the Tower of London is an example of a Norman castle keep, built on the orders of William the Conqueror.
zh: 英国的建筑遗产丰富多样。在中世纪，宏伟的大教堂和教堂被建造起来，其中许多至今仍屹立不倒。例如达勒姆、林肯、坎特伯雷和索尔兹伯里的大教堂。伦敦塔内的白塔是诺曼式城堡主楼的典范，由威廉一世（William the Conqueror）下令修建。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: Gradually, as the countryside became more peaceful and landowners became richer, the houses of the wealthy became more elaborate and great country houses such as Hardwick Hall in Derbyshire were built. British styles of architecture began to evolve.
zh: 随着乡村逐渐变得和平，地主们变得更加富有，富人的住宅也变得更加精致，德比郡的哈德威克大厅（Hardwick Hall）等宏伟的乡间别墅应运而生。英国的建筑风格开始演变。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: In the 17th century, Inigo Jones took inspiration from classical architecture to design the Queen’s House at Greenwich and the Banqueting House in Whitehall in London. Later in the century, Sir Christopher Wren helped develop a British version of the ornate styles popular in Europe in buildings such as the new St Paul’s Cathedral.
zh: 在17世纪，伊尼戈·琼斯（Inigo Jones）从古典建筑中汲取灵感，设计了格林威治的女王宫和伦敦白厅的宴会厅。在该世纪后期，克里斯托弗·雷恩爵士（Sir Christopher Wren）帮助发展出一种英国版的欧洲流行华丽风格，例如在新圣保罗大教堂等建筑中体现出来。

heading: 4.2 Arts and culture
en: In the 18th century, simpler designs became popular. The Scottish architect Robert Adam influenced the development of architecture in the UK, Europe and America. He designed the inside decoration as well as the building itself in great houses such as Dumfries House in Scotland. His ideas influenced architects in cities such as Bath, where the Royal Crescent was built.
