heading: 5.1 Chapter 5: The UK government, the law and your role
en: In this chapter you will learn how the UK is governed. You need to understand the Queen’s role in government and her powers. Make sure you understand how people are appointed to the two Houses of Parliament and the specific roles detailed, such as the Speaker and cabinet, and your rights to vote and stand for election. Everyone should understand the workings of the devolved administrations but pay particular attention if you are taking the test in Scotland, Wales or Northern Ireland.
zh: 在本章中，您将了解英国的治理方式。您需要理解女王在政府中的角色及其权力。确保您了解人们如何被任命到议会的两个议院，以及议长和内阁等具体角色的职责，还有您的投票权和参选权。每个人都应了解权力下放政府的运作情况，但如果您在苏格兰、威尔士或北爱尔兰参加测试，请特别关注这部分内容。

heading: 5.1 Chapter 5: The UK government, the law and your role
en: The UK’s international role is explained through the Commonwealth, EU and others so focus on the differences between these bodies and their member countries. The chapter then explains common laws, fundamental rights, policing and the courts of the UK. Pay particular attention to this so you know the differences between the various courts and offences they deal with. Finally the chapter deals with taxation, driving and community activity.
zh: 本章通过英联邦、欧盟等组织解释英国的国际角色，因此请重点关注这些机构及其成员国之间的区别。随后，本章将介绍英国的一般法律、基本权利、警务和法院系统。请特别注意这部分内容，以便您了解不同法院及其处理的罪行之间的区别。最后，本章涉及税收、驾驶和社区活动。

heading: 5.1 Chapter 5: The UK government, the law and your role
en: IN THIS CHAPTER THERE IS INFORMATION ABOUT :
zh: 本章包含以下信息：

heading: 5.1 Chapter 5: The UK government, the law and your role
en:
• Britain as a constitutional monarchy
• How Parliament works
• Elections, the government and the opposition
• Devolved administrations of the UK
• The Commonwealth, EU, UN and NATO
• British law and justice
• The courts
• Fundamental principles and rights
• Tax
• Driving
• Community work and getting involved
zh:
• 作为君主立宪制的英国
• 议会的运作方式
• 选举、政府和反对党
• 英国的权力下放政府
• 英联邦、欧盟、联合国和北约
• 英国法律与司法
• 法院
• 基本原则与权利
• 税收
• 驾驶
• 社区工作与参与

heading: 5.1 Chapter 5: The UK government, the law and your role
en: The UK is a parliamentary democracy with the monarch as head of state. This section will tell you about the different institutions which make up this democratic system and explain how you can play a part in the democratic process.
zh: 英国是一个议会民主制国家，君主为国家元首。本节将为您介绍构成这一民主体系的不同机构，并解释您如何在民主进程中发挥作用。

heading: 5.2 The development of British democracy
en: Democracy is a system of government where the whole adult population gets a say. This might be by direct voting or by choosing representatives to make decisions on their behalf.
zh: 民主是一种政府制度，全体成年人口都有发言权。这可以通过直接投票或选择代表代为决策来实现。

heading: 5.2 The development of British democracy
en: At the turn of the 19th century, Britain was not a democracy as we know it today. Although there were elections to select members of Parliament (MPs), only a small group of people could vote. They were men who were over 21 years of age and who owned a certain amount of property.
zh: 在19世纪之交，英国并非我们今天所知的民主国家。虽然通过选举产生下议院议员（MP），但只有少数人有投票权。他们是年满21岁且拥有一定数量财产的男性。

heading: 5.2 The development of British democracy
en: The franchise (that is, the number of people who had the right to vote) grew over the course of the 19th century and political parties began to involve ordinary men and women as members.
zh: 在整个19世纪期间，选举权（即拥有投票权的人数）逐渐扩大，政党开始吸纳普通男女成为成员。

heading: 5.2 The development of British democracy
en: In the 1830s and 1840s, a group called the Chartists campaigned for reform. They wanted six changes:
zh: 在19世纪30年代和40年代，一个名为宪章派的团体开展改革运动。他们要求六项变革：

heading: 5.2 The development of British democracy
en:
• for every man to have the vote
• elections every year
• for all regions to be equal in the electoral system
• secret ballots
• for any man to be able to stand as an MP
• for MPs to be paid.
zh:
• 每个男性都拥有投票权
• 每年举行选举
• 选举制度中各地区平等
• 秘密投票
• 任何男性都有资格参选下议院议员
• 向下议院议员支付薪水。

heading: 5.2 The development of British democracy
en: At the time, the campaign was generally seen as a failure. However, by 1918 most of these reforms had been adopted. The voting franchise was also extended to women over 30, and then in 1928 to men and women over 21. In 1969, the voting age was reduced to 18 for men and women.
zh: 在当时，这场运动通常被视为失败。然而，到1918年，大多数这些改革已被采纳。投票权也扩展到了30岁以上的女性，随后在1928年扩展到了21岁以上的男性和女性。1969年，男女的投票年龄降至18岁。

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: A constitution is a set of principles by which a country is governed. It includes all of the institutions that are responsible for running the country and how their power is kept in check. The constitution also includes laws and conventions. The British constitution is not written down in any single document, and therefore it is described as ‘unwritten’. This is mainly because the UK, unlike America or France, has never had a revolution which led permanently to a totally new system of government. Our most important institutions have developed over hundreds of years. Some people believe that there should be a single document, but others believe an unwritten constitution allows for more flexibility and better government.
zh: 宪法是一个国家治理所依据的原则集合。它包括负责管理国家的所有机构及其权力制衡方式。宪法还包括法律和惯例。英国宪法并未记载于单一文件中，因此被称为“不成文”宪法。这主要是因为与美国或法国不同，英国从未发生过导致政府体制彻底更替的革命。我们最重要的机构是在数百年间逐渐发展起来的。有些人认为应该有一份单一的文件，但另一些人则认为不成文宪法能提供更灵活性和更好的治理。

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: In the UK, there are several different parts of government. The main ones are:
zh: 在英国，政府由几个不同的部分组成。主要部分包括：

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en:
• the monarchy
• Parliament (the House of Commons and the House of Lords)
• the Prime Minister
• the cabinet
• the judiciary (courts)
• the police
• the civil service
• local government.
zh:
• 君主制
• 议会（下议院和上议院）
• 首相
• 内阁
• 司法机构（法院）
• 警察
• 公务员系统
• 地方政府。

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: In addition, there are devolved governments in Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland that have the power to legislate on certain issues.
zh: 此外，苏格兰、威尔士和北爱尔兰还有权力下放政府，有权就某些议题制定法律。

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: The monarch is the head of state of the UK. The monarch or head of state for many countries in the Commonwealth. The UK has a constitutional monarchy. This means that the king or queen does not rule the country but appoints the government, which the people have chosen in a democratic election. The monarch invites the leader of the party with the largest number of MPs, or the leader of a coalition between more than one party, to become the Prime Minister. The monarch has regular meetings with the Prime Minister and can advise, warn and encourage, but the decisions on government policies are made by the Prime Minister and cabinet.
zh: 君主是联合王国的国家元首。君主也是英联邦许多国家的国家元首或国家元首代表。英国实行君主立宪制。这意味着国王或女王并不统治国家，而是任命由人民在民主选举中选择的政府。君主邀请在下议院拥有最多下议院议员的政党领袖，或多个政党联盟的领袖担任首相。君主与首相定期会面，可以提供建议、警告和鼓励，但政府政策的决定由首相和内阁作出。

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: Queen Elizabeth II reigned since her father’s death in 1952, and in 2012 she celebrated her Diamond Jubilee (60 years as queen). She died on September 8, 2022. She was married to Prince Philip. Her eldest son, Charles (King Charles III), is the current ruling monarch. Prince Edward, became the Duke of Edinburgh in 2023 on his 59th birthday by his eldest brother, King Charles III, who formerly held the title.
zh: 伊丽莎白二世女王（Queen Elizabeth II）自1952年其父亲逝世后开始统治，并于2012年庆祝了钻石禧（即位60周年）。她于2022年9月8日去世。她与菲利普亲王（Prince Philip）结婚。她的长子查尔斯（King Charles III）是现任君主。爱德华王子（Prince Edward）于2023年在其59岁生日时，由其长兄查尔斯三世国王（King Charles III）授予爱丁堡公爵（Duke of Edinburgh）头衔，该头衔此前由查尔斯三世持有。

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: King Charles III has been the sovereign since 2022, and his heir apparent is his elder son, William, Prince of Wales (title for the heir apparent to the throne). William's three children are next, in order of birth: Prince George, Princess Charlotte, and Prince Louis.
zh: 查尔斯三世国王（King Charles III）自2022年起成为君主，其法定继承人是他的长子威尔士亲王威廉（William, Prince of Wales）（这是王位继承人的头衔）。威廉的三个孩子按出生顺序排在后面：乔治王子（Prince George）、夏洛特公主（Princess Charlotte）和路易斯王子（Prince Louis）。

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: The monarch has important ceremonial roles, such as the opening of the new parliamentary session each year. On this occasion the King (or Queen) makes a speech which summarises the government’s policies for the year ahead. All Acts of Parliament are made in the Majesty's name.
zh: 君主具有重要的礼仪职能，例如每年主持新一届议会开幕。在此场合，国王（或女王）发表演讲，总结政府未来一年的政策。所有议会法案均以君主的名义颁布。

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: The monarch represents the UK to the rest of the world. The monarch receives foreign ambassadors and high commissioners, entertains visiting heads of state, and makes state visits overseas in support of diplomatic and economic relationships with other countries.
zh: 君主代表联合王国与世界各国交往。君主接待外国大使和高级专员，招待来访的国家元首，并出访海外以支持与各国的外交和经济关系。

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: The monarch has an important role in providing stability and continuity. While governments and Prime Ministers change regularly, the monarch continues as head of state. The monarch provides a focus for national identity and pride, which was demonstrated through the celebrations of the Queen Elizabeth's Jubilee.
zh: 君主在提供稳定性和延续性方面发挥着重要作用。虽然政府和首相定期更替，但君主作为国家元首始终如一。君主为民族认同和自豪感提供了焦点，这在庆祝伊丽莎白女王（Queen Elizabeth）禧年的活动中得到了体现。

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: The National Anthem of the UK is ‘God Save the King (or Queen)’. It is played at important national occasions and at events attended by the King (or Queen) or the Royal Family. The first verse is:
zh: 联合王国的国歌是《天佑国王（或女王）》。它在重要的国家场合以及国王（或女王）或王室成员出席的活动中演奏。第一段歌词如下：

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: ‘God save our gracious King!
zh: “天佑我们仁慈的国王！

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: Long live our noble King!
zh: 愿我们尊贵的国王长寿！

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: God save the King!
zh: 天佑国王！

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: Send her victorious,
zh: 愿她战无不胜，

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: Happy and glorious,
zh: 幸福而光荣，

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: Long to reign over us,
zh: 长久统治我们，

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: God save the King!’
zh: “愿上帝保佑国王！”

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: New citizens swear or affirm loyalty to the King as part of the citizenship ceremony.
zh: 新公民在入籍仪式上向国王宣誓或确认效忠。

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: ‘I (name) swear by Almighty God that on becoming a British citizen, I will be faithful and bear true allegiance to His Majesty King Charles the Third, his Heirs and Successors, according to law.’
zh: “我（姓名）以上帝之名发誓，在成为英国公民后，我将忠于查理三世国王陛下及其继承人和继任者，并依法履行效忠义务。”

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: ‘I (name) do solemnly, sincerely and truly declare and affirm that on becoming a British citizen, I will be faithful and bear true allegiance to His Majesty King Charles the Third, his Heirs and Successors, according to law.’
zh: “我（姓名）郑重、真诚且真实地声明并确认，在成为英国公民后，我将忠于查理三世国王陛下及其继承人和继任者，并依法履行效忠义务。”

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: The system of government in the UK is a parliamentary democracy. The UK is divided into parliamentary constituencies. Voters in each constituency elect their member of Parliament (MP) in a General Election. All of the elected MPs form the House of Commons. Most MPs belong to a political party, and the party with the majority of MPs forms the government. If one party does not get a majority, two parties can join together to form a coalition.
zh: 英国的政府体制是议会民主制。联合王国（英国）被划分为若干议会选区。每个选区的选民在大选中选举他们的下议院议员（MP）。所有当选的下议院议员组成下议院。大多数下议院议员属于某个政党，拥有最多下议院议员的政党组成政府。如果一个政党未获得多数席位，两个政党可以联合组建联合政府。

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: The House of Commons is regarded as the more important of the two chambers in Parliament because its members are democratically elected. The Prime Minister and almost all the members of the cabinet are members of the House of Commons (MPs). Each MP represents a parliamentary constituency, which is a small area of the country. MPs have a number of different responsibilities. They:
zh: 下议院被视为议会两院中更重要的一院，因为其成员由民主选举产生。首相和内阁几乎所有成员都是下议院议员（MP）。每位下议院议员代表一个议会选区，即国家内的一个小区域。下议院议员承担多项不同职责。他们：

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en:
• represent everyone in their constituency
• help to create new laws
• scrutinise and comment on what the government is doing
• debate important national issues.
zh:
• 代表其选区内的所有人
• 协助制定新法律
• 审查并评论政府的工作
• 辩论重要的国家议题。

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: Members of the House of Lords, known as peers, are not elected by the people and do not represent a constituency. The role and membership of the House of Lords has changed over the last 50 years.
zh: 上议院成员被称为贵族，不由民众选举产生，也不代表任何选区。在过去50年中，上议院的角色和成员构成发生了变化。

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: Until 1958, all peers were:
zh: 1958年之前，所有贵族都是：

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en:
• ‘hereditary’, which means they inherited their title, or
• senior judges, or
• bishops of the Church of England.
zh:
• “世袭”的，即他们继承了爵位；或
• 高级法官；或
• 英格兰圣公会的主教。

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: Since 1958, the Prime Minister has had the power to nominate peers just for their own lifetime. These are called life peers. They have usually had an important career in politics, business, law or another profession. Life peers are appointed by the monarch on the advice of the Prime Minister. They also include people nominated by the leaders of the other main political parties or by an independent Appointments Commission for non-party peers.
zh: 自1958年以来，首相有权提名仅在其本人终身有效的贵族。这些被称为终身贵族。他们通常在政治、商业、法律或其他专业领域拥有重要的职业生涯。终身贵族由君主根据首相的建议任命。此外，还包括由其他主要政党领袖或独立的非党派贵族任命委员会提名的候选人。

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: Since 1999, hereditary peers have lost the automatic right to attend the House of Lords. They now elect a few of their number to represent them in the House of Lords.
zh: 自1999年以来，世袭贵族失去了自动出席上议院的资格。现在，他们选举少数几位代表在上议院为他们发声。

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: The House of Lords is normally more independent of the government than the House of Commons. It can suggest amendments or propose new laws, which are then discussed by MPs. The House of Lords checks laws that have been passed by the House of Commons to ensure they are fit for purpose. It also holds the government to account to make sure that it is working in the best interests of the people. There are peers who are specialists in particular areas, and their knowledge is useful in making and checking laws. The House of Commons has powers to overrule the House of Lords, but these are not used often.
zh: 上议院通常比下议院更具独立性。它可以提出修正案或建议新法律，然后由下议院议员进行讨论。上议院对下议院通过的法律进行审查，以确保其切实可行。它还监督政府，确保政府以人民的最佳利益行事。上议院中有某些领域的专家，他们的知识对于制定和审查法律非常有用。下议院有权推翻上议院的决定，但这种情况并不常见。

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: Debates in the House of Commons are chaired by the Speaker. This person is the chief Officer of the House of Commons. The Speaker is neutral and does not represent a political party, even though he or she is an MP, represents a constituency and deals with constituents’ problems like any other MP. The Speaker is chosen by other MPs in a secret ballot.
zh: 下议院的辩论由议长主持。议长是下议院的首席官员。议长保持中立，不代表任何政党，尽管他或她是下议院议员，代表一个选区，并像其他下议院议员一样处理选民的问题。议长由其他下议院议员通过秘密投票选出。

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: The Speaker keeps order during political debates to make sure the rules are followed. This includes making sure the opposition has a guaranteed amount of time to debate issues which it chooses. The Speaker also represents Parliament on ceremonial occasions.
zh: 议长在政治辩论期间维持秩序，确保遵守规则。这包括确保反对党有保证的时间来讨论其选择的问题。议长还在礼仪场合代表议会。

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: MPs are elected at a General Election, which is held at least every five years.
zh: 下议院议员在大选中选出，大选至少每五年举行一次。

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: If an MP dies or resigns, there will be a fresh election, called a byelection, in his or her constituency.
zh: 如果一名下议院议员去世或辞职，其选区将举行新的选举，称为补选。

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: MPs are elected through a system called ‘first past the post’. In each constituency, the candidate who gets the most votes is elected. The government is usually formed by the party that wins the majority of constituencies. If no party wins a majority, two parties may join together to form a coalition.
zh: 下议院议员通过一种称为“简单多数制”的制度选出。在每个选区，获得票数最多的候选人当选。政府通常由赢得多数选区的政党组成。如果没有政党赢得多数，两个政党可能会联合组成联合政府。

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: Elections for the European Parliament are also held every five years. Elected members are called members of the European Parliament (MEPs). Elections to the European Parliament use a system of proportional representation, where seats are allocated to each party in proportion to the total number of votes it has won.
zh: 欧洲议会的选举也每五年举行一次。当选成员称为欧洲议会议员（MEPs）。欧洲议会选举采用比例代表制，席位根据各党获得的总票数比例分配给各党。

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: All elected members have a duty to serve and represent their constituents. You can get contact details for all your representatives and their parties from your local library and from www.parliament.uk. MPs, Assembly members, members of the Scottish Parliament (MSPs) and MEPs are also listed in The Phone Book , published by BT, and in_Yellow Pages_.
zh: 所有当选成员都有服务和代表其选民的责任。您可以从当地图书馆和 www.parliament.uk 获取所有代表及其政党的联系方式。下议院议员、威尔士议会成员、苏格兰议会议员（MSPs）和欧洲议会议员也列在 BT 出版的《电话簿》和《黄页》中。

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en: You can contact MPs by letter or telephone at their constituency Office, or at their Office in the House of Commons: The House of Commons, Westminster, London SW1A 0AA, telephone 020 7729 3000. In addition, many MPs, Assembly members, MSPs and MEPs hold regular local ‘surgeries’, where constituents can go in person to talk about issues that are of concern to them. These surgeries are often advertised in the local newspaper.
zh: 您可以通过信函或电话联系下议院议员（MP），地点可以是其选区办公室，也可以是位于伦敦威斯敏斯特英国下议院的办公室：地址为 The House of Commons, Westminster, London SW1A 0AA，电话 020 7729 3000。此外，许多下议院议员、威尔士议会成员、苏格兰议会成员（MSP）和欧洲议会议员（MEP）会定期举办当地“咨询门诊”，选民可以亲自前往就他们关心的问题与议员交谈。这些咨询门诊通常会在当地报纸上刊登广告。

heading: 5.3 The British constitution
en:
• How democracy has developed in the UK
• What a constitution is and how the UK’s constitution is different from those of most other countries
• The role of the monarch
• The role of the House of Commons and House of Lords
• What the Speaker does
• How the UK elects MPs and MEPs
zh:
• 英国民主的发展过程
• 什么是宪法以及英国的宪法为何与其他大多数国家的宪法不同
• 君主的作用
• 下议院和上议院的作用
• 议长的工作内容
• 英国如何选举下议院议员（MP）和欧洲议会议员（MEP）

heading: 5.4 Fundamental principles
en: Britain has a long history of respecting an individual’s rights and ensuring essential freedoms. These rights have their roots in Magna Carta, the Habeas Corpus Act and the Bill of Rights of 1689, and they have developed over a period of time. British diplomats and lawyers had an important role in drafting the European Convention on Human Rights and Fundamental Freedoms. The UK was one of the first countries to sign the Convention in 1950.
zh: 英国有着尊重个人权利并确保基本自由的悠久历史。这些权利源于《大宪章》、《人身保护法》以及1689年的《权利法案》，并随着时间的推移而不断发展。英国外交官和律师在起草《欧洲人权与基本自由公约》方面发挥了重要作用。英国是1950年首批签署该公约的国家之一。

heading: 5.4 Fundamental principles
en: Some of the principles included in the European Convention on Human Rights are:
zh: 《欧洲人权公约》中包含的一些原则包括：

heading: 5.4 Fundamental principles
en:
• right to life
• prohibition of torture
• prohibition of slavery and forced labour
• right to liberty and security
• right to a fair trial
• freedom of thought, conscience and religion
• freedom of expression (speech).
zh:
• 生命权
• 禁止酷刑
• 禁止奴隶制和强迫劳动
• 自由和安全权
• 公平审判权
• 思想、良心和宗教自由
• 言论自由（表达自由）。

heading: 5.4 Fundamental principles
en: The Human Rights Act 1998 incorporated the European Convention on Human Rights into UK law. The government, public bodies and the courts must follow the principles of the Convention.
zh: 1998年《人权法案》将《欧洲人权公约》纳入英国法律。政府、公共机构和法院必须遵守该公约的原则。

heading: 5.4 Fundamental principles
en: UK laws ensure that people are not treated unfairly in any area of life or work because of their age, disability, sex, pregnancy and maternity, race, religion or belief, sexuality or marital status. If you face problems with discrimination, you can get more information from the Citizens Advice Bureau or from one of the following organisations:
zh: 英国法律确保人们不会因年龄、残疾、性别、怀孕和产假、种族、宗教或信仰、性取向或婚姻状况而在生活或工作的任何领域受到不公平对待。如果您面临歧视问题，可以从公民咨询局（Citizens Advice Bureau）或以下组织获取更多信息：

heading: 5.4 Fundamental principles
en:
• England and Wales: Equality and Human Rights Commission (www.equalityhumanrights.com)
• Scotland: Equality and Human Rights Commission in Scotland (www.equalityhumanrights.com/scotland/the-commission-inscotland) and Scottish Human Rights Commission (www.scottishhumanrights.com)
• Northern Ireland: Equality Commission for Northern Ireland (www.equalityni.org)
• Northern Ireland Human Rights Commission (www.nihrc.org).
zh:
• 英格兰和威尔士：平等与人权委员会（www.equalityhumanrights.com）
• 苏格兰：苏格兰平等与人权委员会（www.equalityhumanrights.com/scotland/the-commission-inscotland）和苏格兰人权委员会（www.scottishhumanrights.com）
• 北爱尔兰：北爱尔兰平等委员会（www.equalityni.org）
• 北爱尔兰人权委员会（www.nihrc.org）。

heading: 5.4 Fundamental principles
en: In the UK, brutality and violence in the home is a serious crime. Anyone who is violent towards their partner – whether they are a man or woman, married or living together – can be prosecuted. Any man who forces a woman to have sex, including a woman’s husband, can be charged with rape.
zh: 在英国，家庭中的暴力和虐待行为是严重犯罪。任何对伴侣施加暴力的人——无论男女、已婚或同居——都可能被起诉。任何强迫女性发生性行为的人，包括女性的丈夫，均可被控强奸罪。

heading: 5.4 Fundamental principles
en: It is important for anyone facing domestic violence to get help as soon as possible. A solicitor or the Citizens Advice Bureau can explain the available options. In some areas there are safe places to go and stay in, called refuges or shelters. There are emergency telephone numbers in the helpline section at the front of Yellow Pages, including, for women, the number of the nearest women’s centre. You can also phone the 24-hour National Domestic Violence Freephone Helpline on 0808 2000 247 at any time, or the police can help you find a safe place to stay.
zh: 面临家庭暴力的人应尽快寻求帮助。事务律师或公民咨询局可解释可用的选项。在某些地区，有称为“避难所”或“庇护所”的安全住所可供前往和居住。在《黄色 pages》前部的求助热线部分列出了紧急电话号码，其中包括女性可拨打的最近妇女中心的号码。您还可以随时拨打24小时全国家庭暴力免费热线0808 2000 247，或者警方可以帮助您找到安全住所。

heading: 5.4 Fundamental principles
en: Female genital mutilation (FGM), also known as cutting or female circumcision, is illegal in the UK. Practising FGM or taking a girl or woman abroad for FGM is a criminal offence.
zh: 女性生殖器切割（FGM），也称为割礼或女性 circumcision，在英国是非法的。实施女性生殖器切割或将女孩或女性带往国外进行该行为均属刑事犯罪。

heading: 5.4 Fundamental principles
en: A marriage should be entered into with the full and free consent of both people involved. Arranged marriages, where both parties agree to the marriage, are acceptable in the UK.
zh: 婚姻应基于双方完全且自由的同意而缔结。在双方均同意结婚的情况下，包办婚姻在英国是可以接受的。

heading: 5.4 Fundamental principles
en: Forced marriage is where one or both parties do not or cannot give their consent to enter into the partnership. Forcing another person to marry is a criminal offence.
zh: 强迫婚姻是指一方或双方未同意或无法同意建立伴侣关系。强迫他人结婚是一项刑事犯罪。

heading: 5.4 Fundamental principles
en: Forced Marriage Protection Orders were introduced in 2008 for England, Wales and Northern Ireland under the Forced Marriage (Civil Protection) Act 2007. Court
zh: 根据2007年《强迫婚姻（民事保护）法》，英格兰、威尔士和北爱尔兰于2008年引入了强迫婚姻保护令。法院

heading: 5.4 Fundamental principles
en: orders can be obtained to protect a person from being forced into a marriage, or to protect a person in a forced marriage. Similar Protection Orders were introduced in Scotland in November 2011.
zh: 可以申请保护令，以保护某人免受强迫婚姻，或保护处于强迫婚姻中的人。苏格兰于2011年11月引入了类似的保护令。

heading: 5.4 Fundamental principles
en: A potential victim, or someone acting for them, can apply for an order. Anyone found to have breached an order can be jailed for up to two years for contempt of
zh: 潜在受害者或代表其行事的人可以申请该命令。任何被认定违反命令的人可能因藐视法庭罪被判处最高两年的监禁。

heading: 5.4 Fundamental principles
en: court.
zh: 法院。

heading: 5.4.1 Taxation
en: People in the UK have to pay tax on their income, which includes:
zh: 英国人必须就其收入缴税，收入包括：

heading: 5.4.1 Taxation
en:
• wages from paid employment
• profits from self-employment
• taxable benefits
• pensions
• income from property, savings and dividends.
zh:
• 受雇工作的工资
• 自雇所得利润
• 应税福利
• 养老金
• 来自房产、储蓄和股息的收入。

heading: 5.4.1 Taxation
en: Money raised from income tax pays for government services such as roads, education, police and the armed forces.
zh: 所得税筹集的资金用于资助政府服务，如道路、教育、警察和武装部队。

heading: 5.4.1 Taxation
en: For most people, the right amount of income tax is automatically taken from their income from employment by their employer and paid directly to HM Revenue & Customs (HMRC), the government department that collects taxes. This system is called ‘Pay As You Earn’ (PAYE). If you are self- employed, you need to pay your own tax through a system called ‘self- assessment’, which includes completing a tax return. Other people may also need to complete a tax return. If HMRC sends you a tax return, it is important to complete and return the form as soon as you have all the necessary information.
zh: 对于大多数人来说，雇主会通过称为“实时工资系统”（Pay As You Earn，简称PAYE）的制度，自动从其就业收入中扣除应缴的所得税，并直接支付给负责征收税款的政府部门——英国税务海关总署（HMRC）。如果您是自雇人士，您需要通过称为“自我评估”的系统自行缴税，其中包括填写纳税申报表。其他人也可能需要填写纳税申报表。如果英国税务海关总署向您发送纳税申报表，在获得所有必要信息后，及时完成并退回表格非常重要。

heading: 5.4.1 Taxation
en: You can find out more about income tax at www.hmrc.gov.uk/incometax. You can get help and advice about taxes and completing tax forms from the HMRC self-assessment helpline, on 0845 300 0627, and the HMRC website at www.hmrc.gov.uk.
zh: 您可以在 www.hmrc.gov.uk/incometax 了解有关所得税的更多信息。如需关于税务及填写税表的帮助和建议，可拨打 HMRC 自我评估热线 0845 300 0627，或访问 HMRC 网站 www.hmrc.gov.uk。

heading: 5.4.1 Taxation
en: Almost everybody in the UK who is in paid work, including self-employed people, must pay National Insurance Contributions. The money raised from National Insurance Contributions is used to pay for state benefits and services such as the state retirement pension and the National Health Service (NHS).
zh: 英国几乎所有受雇人员，包括自雇人士，都必须缴纳国民保险供款。征收的国民保险供款用于支付国家福利和服务，如国家养老金和国民医疗服务体系（NHS）。

heading: 5.4.1 Taxation
en: Employees have their National Insurance Contributions deducted from their pay by their employer. People who are self-employed need to pay National Insurance Contributions themselves. Anyone who does not pay enough National Insurance Contributions will not be able to receive certain contributory benefits such as Jobseeker’s Allowance or a full state retirement pension. Some workers, such as part-time workers, may not qualify for statutory payments such as maternity pay if they do not earn enough.
zh: 雇员由雇主从其工资中扣除国民保险供款。自雇人士需自行缴纳国民保险供款。如果任何人缴纳的国民保险供款不足，将无法领取某些缴费型福利，如求职者津贴或全额国家养老金。部分工人（如兼职人员）如果收入不足，可能不符合领取法定付款（如产假工资）的资格。

heading: 5.4.1 Taxation
en: Further guidance about National Insurance Contributions is available on HMRC’s website at www.hmrc.gov.uk/ni.
zh: 有关国民保险供款的更多指导信息可在 HMRC 网站 www.hmrc.gov.uk/ni 上获取。

heading: 5.4.1 Taxation
en: A National Insurance number is a unique personal account number. It makes sure that the National Insurance Contributions and tax you pay are properly recorded against your name. All young people in the UK are sent a National Insurance number just before their 16th birthday.
zh: 国民保险号码是一个独特的个人账户号码。它确保您缴纳的国民保险供款和税款正确记录在您的名下。英国所有年轻人在年满16岁生日前夕都会收到一个国民保险号码。

heading: 5.4.1 Taxation
en: A non-UK national living in the UK and looking for work, starting work or setting up as self-employed will need a National Insurance number. However, you can start work without one. If you have permission to work in the UK, you will need to telephone the Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) to arrange to get a National Insurance number. You may be required to attend an interview. The DWP will advise you of the appropriate application process and tell you which documents you will need to bring to an interview if one is necessary. You will usually need documents that prove your identity and that you have permission to work in the UK. A National Insurance number does not on its own prove to an employer that you have the right to work in the UK.
zh: 居住在英国并正在找工作、开始工作或成为自雇人士的非英国公民需要国民保险号码。然而，您可以在没有国民保险号码的情况下开始工作。如果您获准在英国工作，您需要致电工作与养老金部（DWP）安排获取国民保险号码。您可能需要参加面试。DWP 将告知您适当的申请流程，并在必要时告知您需要携带哪些文件参加面试。您通常需要提供证明您身份以及您获准在英国工作的文件。仅凭国民保险号码并不能向雇主证明您有权在英国工作。

heading: 5.4.1 Taxation
en: You can find out more information about how to apply for a National Insurance number at www.gov.uk.
zh: 您可以在 www.gov.uk 了解有关如何申请国民保险号码的更多信息。

heading: 5.4.2 Driving
en: In the UK, you must be at least 17 years old to drive a car or motor cycle and you must have a driving licence to drive on public roads. To get a UK driving licence you must pass a driving test, which tests both your knowledge and your practical skills. You need to be at least 16 years old to ride a moped, and there are other age requirements and special tests for driving large vehicles.
zh: 在英国，您必须年满17岁才能驾驶汽车或摩托车，并且必须持有驾驶执照才能在公共道路上驾驶。要获得英国驾驶执照，您必须通过驾驶考试，该考试测试您的知识和实际操作技能。您需要年满16岁才能驾驶轻便摩托车，驾驶大型车辆还有其他年龄要求及特殊考试。

heading: 5.4.2 Driving
en: Follow the government guidelines to learn what you need to drive. The first step is applying for a provisional driving license and finding a driving instuctor. Then you will need to pass:
zh: 遵循政府指南，了解驾驶所需条件。第一步是申请临时驾驶执照并寻找一位驾驶教练。随后你需要通过：

heading: 5.4.2 Driving
en:
• A driving theory test (Consists of a multiple choice theory test and a hazard perception test)
• A practical driving test
zh:
• 驾驶理论考试（包括多项选择题理论和危险感知测试）
• 实际驾驶考试

heading: 5.4.2 Driving
en: Drivers can use their driving licence until they are 70 years old. After that, the licence is valid for three years at a time.
zh: 驾驶员可以使用其驾驶执照直至70岁。此后，执照每次有效期为三年。

heading: 5.4.2 Driving
en: In Northern Ireland, a newly qualified driver must display an ‘R’ place (for restricted driver) for one year after passing the test.
zh: 在北爱尔兰，新获得资格的驾驶员在通过考试后的一年内必须展示‘R’牌（代表受限驾驶员）。

heading: 5.4.2 Driving
en: If your driving licence is from a country in the European Union (EU), Iceland, Liechtenstein or Norway, you can drive in the UK for as long as your licence is valid. If you have a licence from any other country, you may use it in the UK for up to 12 months. To continue driving after that, you must get a UK full driving licence.
zh: 如果你的驾驶执照来自欧盟（EU）、冰岛、列支敦士登或挪威的国家，只要你的执照有效，你就可以在英国驾驶。如果你持有其他国家的执照，你可以在英国使用长达12个月。此后若要继续驾驶，你必须获得英国的正式驾驶执照。

heading: 5.4.2 Driving
en: If you are resident in the UK, your car or motor cycle must be registered at Check that you understand: the Driver and Vehicle Licensing Agency (DVLA). You must pay an annual road tax and display the tax disc, which shows that the tax has been paid, on
zh: 如果您居住在英国，您的汽车或摩托车必须在车辆管理局（Driver and Vehicle Licensing Agency, DVLA）注册。您必须缴纳年度道路税并张贴税标，以证明税款已缴清。

heading: 5.4.2 Driving
en: • The fundamental principles of UK law
zh: • 英国法律的基本原则

heading: 5.4.2 Driving
en: the windscreen. You must also have valid motor insurance. It is a serious
zh: 挡风玻璃。您还必须持有有效的机动车保险。这是一项严重的

heading: 5.4.2 Driving
en: • That domestic violence, FGM and forced marriage are illegal in the UK
zh: • 家庭暴力、女性生殖器切割和强迫婚姻在英国是非法的

heading: 5.4.2 Driving
en: criminal offence to drive without insurance. If your vehicle is over three years old, you must take it for a Ministry of Transport (MOT) test every • The system of income tax and National Insurance year. It is an offence not to have an MOT certificate if your vehicle is more
zh: 无保险驾驶属于刑事犯罪。如果您的车辆超过三年，您必须每年将其送至进行车辆年检（MOT test）。如果车辆超过三年而没有车辆年检证书，则构成违法。

heading: 5.4.2 Driving
en: • The requirements for driving a car
zh: • 驾驶汽车的资格要求

heading: 5.4.2 Driving
en: than three years old. You can find out more about vehicle tax and MOT requirements from www.gov.uk.
zh: 超过三年车龄的车辆。您可以从 www.gov.uk 了解有关车辆税和 MOT（车辆年检）要求的更多信息。

heading: 5.5 Respecting the law
en: One of the most important responsibilities of all residents in the UK is to know and obey the law. This section will tell you about the legal system in the UK and some of the laws that may affect you. Britain is proud of being a welcoming country, but all residents, regardless of their background, are expected to comply with the law and to understand that some things which may be allowed in other legal systems are not acceptable in the UK. Those who do not respect the law should not expect to be allowed to become permanent residents in the UK.
zh: 所有英国居民最重要的责任之一就是了解并遵守法律。本节将为您介绍英国的法律体系以及可能影响您的部分法律。英国以包容 welcoming 的国家为荣，但所有居民，无论其背景如何，都期望他们遵守法律，并理解在其他法律体系中可能被允许的一些行为在英国是不可接受的。不尊重法律的人不应期望被允许成为英国的永久居民。

heading: 5.5 Respecting the law
en: The law is relevant to all areas of life in the UK. You should make sure that you are aware of the laws which affect your everyday life, including both your personal and business affairs.
zh: 法律与英国生活的各个领域都息息相关。您应确保自己了解影响日常生活的法律，包括您的个人事务和商业事务。

heading: 5.5 Respecting the law
en: Every person in the UK receives equal treatment under the law. This means that the law applies in the same way to everyone, no matter who they are or where they are from.
zh: 在英国，每个人都受到法律的平等对待。这意味着法律对所有人一视同仁，无论其身份或出身如何。

heading: 5.5 Respecting the law
en: Laws can be divided into criminal law and civil law:
zh: 法律可分为刑法和民法：

heading: 5.5 Respecting the law
en:
• Criminal law relates to crimes, which are usually investigated by the police or another authority such as a council, and which are punished by the courts.
• Civil law is used to settle disputes between individuals or groups.
zh:
• 刑法涉及犯罪行为，通常由警方或其他当局（如地方议会）进行调查，并由法院进行惩罚。
• 民法用于解决个人之间或团体之间的纠纷。

heading: 5.5 Respecting the law
en: Examples of criminal laws are:
zh: 刑法的例子包括：

heading: 5.5 Respecting the law
en:
• Carrying a weapon: it is a criminal offence to carry a weapon of any kind, even if it is for self-defence. This includes a gun, a knife or anything that is made or adapted to cause injury.
• Drugs: selling or buying drugs such as heroin, cocaine, ecstasy and cannabis is illegal in the UK.
• Racial crime: it is a criminal offence to cause harassment, alarm or distress to someone because of their religion or ethnic origin.
• Selling tobacco: it is illegal to sell tobacco products (for example, cigarettes, cigars, roll-up tobacco) to anyone under the age of 18.
• Smoking in public places: it is against the law to smoke tobacco products in nearly every enclosed public place in the UK. There are signs displayed to tell you where you cannot smoke.
• Buying alcohol: it is a criminal offence to sell alcohol to anyone who is under 18 or to buy alcohol for people who are under the age of 18. (There is one exception: people aged 16 or over can drink alcohol with a meal in a hotel or restaurant.
• Drinking in public: some places have alcohol-free zones where you cannot drink in public. The police can also confiscate alcohol or move young people on from public places. You can be fined or arrested.
zh:
• 携带武器：携带任何类型的武器均属刑事犯罪，即使是出于自卫目的。这包括枪支、刀具或任何制造或改装用于造成伤害的物品。
• 毒品：在英国，买卖海洛因、可卡因、摇头丸和大麻等毒品是非法的。
• 种族犯罪：因某人的宗教或族裔背景而对其造成骚扰、恐慌或困扰属于刑事犯罪。
• 销售烟草：向未满18岁的人销售烟草产品（例如香烟、雪茄、卷烟丝）是非法的。
• 在公共场所吸烟：在英国，几乎在所有封闭的公共场所吸烟均属违法。现场会设有标识告知您禁止吸烟的区域。
• 购买酒精饮料：向未满18岁的人出售酒精饮料，或为未满18岁的人购买酒精饮料均属刑事犯罪。（有一个例外：16岁或以上的人可以在酒店或餐厅用餐时饮酒。
• 在公共场所饮酒：某些地区设有禁酒区，禁止在公共场所饮酒。警方还可以没收酒精饮料或将年轻人从公共场所驱离。您可能被罚款或逮捕。

heading: 5.5 Respecting the law
en: This list does not include all crimes. There are many that apply in most countries, such as murder, theft and assault. You can find out more about types of crime in the UK at www.gov.uk.
zh: 此列表并未涵盖所有犯罪行为。许多罪行在大多数国家都存在，例如谋杀、盗窃和袭击。您可以在 www.gov.uk 上了解英国更多类型的犯罪信息。

heading: 5.5 Respecting the law
en: Examples of civil laws are:
zh: 民法的例子包括：

heading: 5.5 Respecting the law
en:
• Housing law: this includes disputes between landlords and tenants over issues such as repairs and eviction.
• Consumer rights: an example of this is a dispute about faulty goods or services.
• Employment law: these cases include disputes over wages and cases of unfair dismissal or discrimination in the workplace.
• Debt: people might be taken to court if they owe money to someone.
zh:
• 住房法：这包括房东与租客之间关于维修和驱逐等问题的纠纷。
• 消费者权益：例如关于缺陷商品或服务的纠纷。
• 就业法：此类案件包括关于工资的纠纷，以及职场中不公平解雇或歧视的案例。
• 债务：如果某人欠钱不还，可能会被起诉至法院。

heading: 5.5 Respecting the law
en: The job of the police in the UK is to:
zh: 英国警察的职责包括：

heading: 5.5 Respecting the law
en:
• protect life and property
• prevent disturbances (also known as keeping the peace)
• prevent and detect crime.
zh:
• 保护生命和财产
• 防止骚乱（也称为维持治安）
• 预防和侦查犯罪。

heading: 5.5 Respecting the law
en: The police are organised into a number of separate police forces headed by Chief Constables. They are independent of the government.
zh: 警察由多个独立的警察部队组成，各部队由警务总监领导。他们独立于政府运作。

heading: 5.5 Respecting the law
en: In November 2012, the public elected Police and Crime Commissioners (PCCs) in England and Wales. These are directly elected individuals who are responsible for the delivery of an efficient and effective police force that reflects the needs of their local communities. PCCs set local police priorities and the local policing budget. They also appoint the Chief Constable.
zh: 2012年11月，英格兰和威尔士的公众选举产生了警务与犯罪事务专员（PCCs）。这些专员由直接选举产生，负责提供高效且有效的警察服务，以满足当地社区的需求。警务与犯罪事务专员制定地方警务优先事项和地方警务预算，并任命警务总监。

heading: 5.5 Respecting the law
en: The police force is a public service that helps and protects everyone, no matter what their background or where they live. Police Officers must themselves obey the law. They must not misuse their authority, make a false statement, be rude or abusive, or commit racial discrimination. If police Officers are corrupt or misuse their authority they are severely punished.
zh: 警察部队是一项公共服务，帮助和保护所有人，无论其背景如何或居住在哪里。警官必须遵守法律。他们不得滥用职权、作虚假陈述、态度粗鲁或辱骂他人，或实施种族歧视。如果警官腐败或滥用职权，将受到严厉惩罚。

heading: 5.5 Respecting the law
en: Police Officers are supported by police community support Officers (PSCOs). PSCOs have different roles according to the area but usually patrol the streets, work with the public, and support police Officers at crime scenes and major events.
zh: 警官由警察社区支持官员（PSCOs）提供支持。PSCOs的角色因地区而异，但通常负责巡逻街道、与公众合作，并在犯罪现场和重大活动中支持警官。

heading: 5.5 Respecting the law
en: All people in the UK are expected to help the police prevent and detect crimes whenever they can. If you are arrested and taken to a police station, a police Officer will tell you the reason for your arrest and you will be able to seek legal advice.
zh: 英国所有人都被期望在力所能及的范围内帮助警察预防和侦查犯罪。如果你被捕并被带到警察局，警官会告知你被捕的原因，你将能够获得法律建议。

heading: 5.5 Respecting the law
en: If something goes wrong, the police complaints system tries to put it right. Anyone can make a complaint about the police by going to a police station or writing to the Chief Constable of the police force involved. Complaints can also be made to an independent body: the Independent Police Complaints Commission in England and Wales, the Police Complaints Commissioner for Scotland or the Police Ombudsman for Northern Ireland.
zh: 如果出现问题，警察投诉系统会尝试纠正。任何人都可以通过前往警察局或写信给相关警察部队的警务总监来对警察提出投诉。也可以向独立机构提出投诉：英格兰和威尔士的独立警察投诉委员会、苏格兰的警察投诉专员或北爱尔兰的警察监察员。

heading: 5.5 Respecting the law
en: The UK faces a range of terrorist threats. The most serious of these is from Al Qa’ida, its affiliates and like-minded organisations. The UK also faces threats from other kinds of terrorism, such as Northern Ireland-related terrorism.
zh: 英国面临一系列恐怖主义威胁。其中最严重的是来自基地组织（Al Qa’ida）、其附属组织及理念相似组织的威胁。英国还面临其他类型恐怖主义的威胁，例如与北爱尔兰问题时期相关的恐怖主义。

heading: 5.5 Respecting the law
en: All terrorist groups try to radicalise and recruit people to their cause. How, where and to what extent they try to do so will vary. Evidence shows that these groups attract very low levels of public support, but people who want to make their home in the UK should be aware of this threat. It is important that all citizens feel safe.
zh: 所有恐怖组织都试图激进化并招募人员为其事业效力。他们采取的方式、地点和程度各不相同。证据表明，这些组织获得的公众支持率极低，但希望在英国定居的人应意识到这一威胁。让所有公民感到安全至关重要。

heading: 5.5 Respecting the law
en: This includes feeling safe from all kinds of extremism (vocal or active opposition to fundamental British values), including religious extremism and far-right extremism.
zh: 这包括免受各种极端主义（对基本英国价值观的公开或积极反对）的威胁，其中包括宗教极端主义和极右翼极端主义。

heading: 5.5 Respecting the law
en: If you think someone is trying to persuade you to join an extremist or terrorist cause, you should notify your local police force.
zh: 如果您认为有人正试图说服您加入极端主义或恐怖主义事业，您应向当地警方报告。

heading: 5.5 Respecting the law
en:
• The difference between civil and criminal law and some examples of each
• The duties of the police
• The possible terrorist threats facing the UK
zh:
• 民法与刑法的区别及各自的示例
• 警察的职责
• 英国面临的潜在恐怖主义威胁

heading: 5.5.1 The role of the courts
en: Judges (who are together called ‘the judiciary’) are responsible for interpreting the law and ensuring that trials are conducted fairly. The government cannot interfere with this.
zh: 法官（统称为‘司法界’）负责解释法律并确保审判公正进行。政府不得干涉此事。

heading: 5.5.1 The role of the courts
en: Sometimes the actions of the government are claimed to be illegal. If the judges agree, then the government must either change its policies or ask Parliament to change the law. If judges find that a public body is not respecting someone’s legal rights, they can order that body to change its practices and/or pay compensation. Judges also make decisions in disputes between members of the public or organisations. These might be about contracts, property or employment rights or after an accident.
zh: 有时，政府的行为被指控为非法。如果法官同意这一指控，政府必须要么改变其政策，要么要求议会修改法律。如果法官发现公共机构未尊重某人的合法权利，他们可以命令该机构改变其做法和/或支付赔偿。法官还在公众成员或组织之间的纠纷中做出决定。这些纠纷可能涉及合同、财产或就业权利，也可能是在事故之后。

heading: 5.5.1 The role of the courts
en: There are some differences between the court systems in England and Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland.
zh: 英格兰和威尔士、苏格兰以及北爱尔兰的法院系统之间存在一些差异。

heading: 5.5.1 The role of the courts
subheading: Magistrates’ and Justice of the Peace Courts
en: In England, Wales and Northern Ireland, most minor criminal cases are dealt with in a Magistrates’ Court. In Scotland, minor criminal offences go to a Justice of the Peace Court.
zh: 在英格兰、威尔士和北爱尔兰，大多数轻微刑事案件由治安法院（Magistrates’ Court）处理。在苏格兰，轻微刑事犯罪案件由治安法官法院（Justice of the Peace Court）审理。

heading: 5.5.1 The role of the courts
subheading: Magistrates’ and Justice of the Peace Courts
en: Magistrates and Justices of the Peace (JPs) are members of the local community. In England, Wales and Scotland they usually work unpaid and do not need legal qualifications. They receive training to do the job and are supported by a legal adviser. Magistrates decide the verdict in each case that comes before them and, if the person is found guilty, the sentence that they are given. In Northern Ireland, cases are heard by a District Judge or Deputy District Judge, who is legally qualified and paid.
zh: 治安法官（Magistrates，亦称 Justices of the Peace，简称 JPs）是当地社区的成员。在英格兰、威尔士和苏格兰，他们通常无偿工作，且不需要法律资格。他们接受从事该工作的培训，并得到法律顾问的支持。治安法官对提交给他们的每个案件作出裁决，如果被告被判有罪，则由他们决定刑罚。在北爱尔兰，案件由具有法律资格并领取薪酬的地区法官或副地区法官审理。

heading: 5.5.1 The role of the courts
en: In England, Wales and Northern Ireland, serious offences are tried in front of a judge and a jury in a Crown Court. In Scotland, serious cases are heard in a Sheriff Court with either a sheriff or a sheriff with a jury. The most serious cases in Scotland, such as murder, are heard at a High Court with a judge and jury. A jury is made up of members of the public chosen at random from the local electoral register. In England, Wales and Northern Ireland a jury has 12 members, and in Scotland a jury has 15 members. Everyone who is summoned to do jury service must do it unless they are not eligible (for example, because they have a criminal conviction) or they provide a good reason to be excused, such as ill health.
zh: 在英格兰、威尔士和北爱尔兰，严重罪行由刑事法院（Crown Court）的法官和陪审团审理。在苏格兰，严重案件由郡长法院（Sheriff Court）审理，由一名郡长单独审理，或由一名郡长与陪审团共同审理。苏格兰最严重的案件（如谋杀案）由高等法院（High Court）的法官和陪审团审理。陪审团由从当地选民登记册中随机选出的公众成员组成。在英格兰、威尔士和北爱尔兰，陪审团有12名成员；在苏格兰，陪审团有15名成员。所有被传唤履行陪审团义务的人都必须参加，除非他们不符合资格（例如因有犯罪定罪记录）或提供正当理由获准豁免，如健康状况不佳。

heading: 5.5.1 The role of the courts
en: The jury has to listen to the evidence presented at the trial and then decide a verdict of ‘guilty’ or ‘not guilty’ based on what they have heard. In Scotland, a third verdict of ‘not proven’ is also possible. If the jury finds a defendant guilty, the judge decides on the penalty.
zh: 陪审团必须听取审判中提出的证据，然后根据所听到的内容作出“有罪”或“无罪”的裁决。在苏格兰，还可以作出第三种裁决：“证据不足”（not proven）。如果陪审团判定被告有罪，法官将决定刑罚。

heading: 5.5.1 The role of the courts
en: In England, Wales and Northern Ireland, if an accused person is aged 10 to 17, the case is normally heard in a Youth Court in front of up to three specially trained magistrates or a District Judge. The most serious cases will go to the Crown Court. The parents or carers of the young person are expected to attend the hearing. Members of the public are not allowed in Youth Courts, and the name or photographs of the accused young person cannot be published in newspapers or used by the media.
zh: 在英格兰、威尔士和北爱尔兰，如果被指控者年龄在10至17岁之间，案件通常由少年法院（Youth Court）审理，由最多三名经过专门培训的治安法官或一名地区法官主持。最严重的案件将移交刑事法院审理。预计未成年人的父母或监护人必须出席听证会。公众不得进入少年法院，被指控的未成年人姓名或照片不得在报纸上发表或被媒体使用。

heading: 5.5.1 The role of the courts
en: In Scotland a system called the Children’s Hearings System is used to deal with children and young people who have committed an offence.
zh: 在苏格兰，采用一种称为“儿童聆讯制度”（Children’s Hearings System）的制度来处理实施犯罪行为的儿童和青少年。

heading: 5.5.1 The role of the courts
en: Northern Ireland has a system of youth conferencing to consider how a child should be dealt with when they have committed an offence.
zh: 北爱尔兰有一套青年会议制度，用于审议对实施犯罪行为的儿童应如何处理。

heading: 5.5.1 The role of the courts
en: County Courts deal with a wide range of civil disputes. These include people trying to get back money that is owed to them, cases involving personal injury, family matters, breaches of contract, and divorce. In Scotland, most of these matters are dealt with in the Sheriff Court. More serious civil cases – for example, when a large amount of compensation is being claimed – are dealt with in the High Court in England, Wales and Northern Ireland. In Scotland, they are dealt with in the Court of Session in Edinburgh.
zh: 郡法院（County Courts）处理各种各样的民事纠纷。这些包括追讨欠款、人身伤害案件、家庭事务、违约行为以及离婚案件。在苏格兰，大多数此类事项由郡长法院（Sheriff Court）处理。更严重的民事案件——例如索赔大额赔偿金——在英格兰、威尔士和北爱尔兰由高等法院（High Court）处理。在苏格兰，则由位于爱丁堡的上诉法院（Court of Session）处理。

heading: 5.5.1 The role of the courts
en: The small claims procedure is an informal way of helping people to settle minor disputes without spending a lot of time and money using a lawyer. This procedure is used for claims of less than £10,000 in England and Wales and £5,000 in Scotland and Northern Ireland. The hearing is held in front of a judge in an ordinary room, and people from both sides of the dispute sit around a table. Small claims can also be issued online through Money Claims Online (www.moneyclaim.gov.uk).
zh: 小额索赔程序是一种非正式方式，帮助人们在不花费大量时间和律师费的情况下解决轻微纠纷。该程序适用于英格兰和威尔士金额低于10,000英镑、苏格兰和北爱尔兰金额低于5,000英镑的索赔。听证会在普通房间由法官主持，争议双方围坐在一张桌子旁。小额索赔也可通过“在线金钱索赔”（Money Claims Online，网址：www.moneyclaim.gov.uk）在线提起。

heading: 5.5.1 The role of the courts
en: You can get details about the small claims procedure from your local County Court or Sheriff Court. Details of your local court can be found as follows:
zh: 您可以从当地郡法院或郡长法院获取关于小额索赔程序的详细信息。您当地法院的详细信息可按以下方式查找：

heading: 5.5.1 The role of the courts
en:
• England and Wales: at www.gov.uk
• Scotland: at www.scotcourts.gov.uk
• Northern Ireland: at www.courtsni.gov.uk.
zh:
• 英格兰和威尔士：www.gov.uk
• 苏格兰：www.scotcourts.gov.uk
• 北爱尔兰：www.courtsni.gov.uk。

heading: 5.5.1 The role of the courts
en: Solicitors are trained lawyers who give advice on legal matters, take action for their clients and represent their clients in court.
zh: 事务律师是受过专业训练的律师，他们就法律事务提供建议，代表客户采取行动并在法庭上为客户辩护。

heading: 5.5.1 The role of the courts
en: There are solicitors’ Offices throughout the UK. It is important to find out which aspects of law a solicitor specialises in and to check that they have
zh: 联合王国各地均有事务律师事务所。了解事务律师专精的法律领域并确认他们拥有

heading: 5.5.1 The role of the courts
en: the right experience to help you with your case. Many advertise in local newspapers and in Yellow Pages. The Citizens Advice Bureau (www.citizensadvice.org.uk) can give you names of local solicitors and which areas of law they specialise in. You can also get this information from the Law Society (www.lawsociety.org.uk) in England and Wales, the Law Society of Scotland (www.lawscot.org.uk) or the Law Society of Northern Ireland (www.lawsoc-ni.org). Solicitors’ charges are usually based on how much time they spend on a case. It is very important to find out at the start how much a case is likely to cost.
zh: 处理您案件所需的适当经验非常重要。许多事务律师会在当地报纸和《黄页》（Yellow Pages）上刊登广告。公民咨询局（Citizens Advice Bureau，网址：www.citizensadvice.org.uk）可以为您提供当地事务律师的名单及其专精的法律领域。您也可以从英格兰和威尔士的法学会（Law Society，网址：www.lawsociety.org.uk）、苏格兰法学院（Law Society of Scotland，网址：www.lawscot.org.uk）或北爱尔兰法学院（Law Society of Northern Ireland，网址：www.lawsoc-ni.org）获取这些信息。事务律师的收费通常基于他们在案件上花费的时间。在开始时了解案件可能产生的费用非常重要。

heading: 5.5.1 The role of the courts
en:
• The role of the judiciary
• About the different criminal courts in the UK
• About the different civil courts in the UK
• How you can settle a small claim
zh:
• 司法体系的作用
• 关于联合王国不同刑事法院的信息
• 关于联合王国不同民事法院的信息
• 如何解决小额索赔

heading: 5.6 The government
en: The Prime Minister (PM) is the leader of the political party in power. He or she appoints the members of the cabinet and has control over many important public appointments. The official home of the Prime Minister is 10 Downing Street, in central London, near the Houses of Parliament. He or she also has a country house outside London called Chequers.
zh: 首相（Prime Minister，简称PM）是执政党的领袖。他或她任命内阁成员，并控制许多重要的公共职位任命。首相的官方住所位于伦敦市中心的唐宁街10号，靠近议会大厦（Houses of Parliament）。他在伦敦郊外还拥有一处名为切克尼（Chequers）的乡间别墅。

heading: 5.6 The government
en: The Prime Minister can be changed if the MPs in the governing party decide to do so, or if he or she wishes to resign. The Prime Minister usually resigns if his or her party loses a General Election.
zh: 如果执政党的下议院议员（MPs）决定更换，或者首相本人希望辞职，首相职位可以发生变动。通常，如果首相所在的政党在大选（General Election）中失利，首相会辞职。

heading: 5.6 The government
en: The Prime Minister appoints about 20 senior MPs to become ministers in charge of departments. These include:
zh: 首相任命约20名资深下议院议员担任各部大臣。其中包括：

heading: 5.6 The government
en:
• Chancellor of the Exchequer – responsible for the economy
• Home Secretary – responsible for crime, policing and immigration
• Foreign Secretary – responsible for managing relationships with foreign countries
• other ministers (called ‘Secretaries of State’) responsible for subjects such as education, health and defence.
zh:
• 财政大臣——负责经济事务
• 内政大臣——负责犯罪、警务和移民事务
• 外交大臣——负责管理与外国的关系
• 其他大臣（称为‘国务大臣’）负责教育、卫生和国防等议题。

heading: 5.6 The government
en: These ministers form the cabinet, a committee which usually meets weekly and makes important decisions about government policy. Many of these decisions have to be debated or approved by Parliament.
zh: 这些大臣组成内阁，这是一个通常每周开会并就政府政策作出重要决定的委员会。许多这些决定必须在议会进行辩论或获得批准。

heading: 5.6 The government
en: Each department also has a number of other ministers, called Ministers of State and Parliamentary Under-Secretaries of State, who take charge of particular areas of the department’s work.
zh: 每个部门还有若干其他大臣，称为国务次官和议会副秘书，他们负责该部门工作的特定领域。

heading: 5.6 The government
en: The second-largest party in the House of Commons is called the opposition. The leader of the opposition usually becomes Prime Minister if his or her party wins the next General Election.
zh: 下议院中第二大政党称为反对党。如果反对党的领袖在其政党赢得下次大选后，通常会成为首相。

heading: 5.6 The government
en: The leader of the opposition leads his or her party in pointing out what they see as the government’s failures and weaknesses. One important opportunity to do this is at Prime Minister’s Questions, which takes place every week while Parliament is sitting. The leader of the opposition also appoints senior opposition MPs to be ‘shadow ministers’. They form the shadow cabinet and their role is to challenge the government and put forward alternative policies.
zh: 反对党领袖领导其政党指出他们认为政府的失败和弱点。一个重要的机会是在每周议会开会期间的“首相问答”环节。反对党领袖还任命资深反对党下议院议员担任“影子大臣”。他们组成影子内阁，其角色是挑战政府并提出替代政策。

heading: 5.6 The government
en: Anyone aged 18 or over can stand for election as an MP but they are unlikely to win unless they have been nominated to represent one of the major political parties. These are the Conservative Party, the Labour Party, the Liberal Democrats, or one of the parties representing Scottish, Welsh or Northern Irish interests.
zh: 任何年满18岁的人都可以参选下议院议员，但除非他们被提名代表主要政党之一，否则不太可能获胜。这些政党包括保守党、工党、自由民主党，或代表苏格兰、威尔士或北爱尔兰利益的政党。

heading: 5.6 The government
en: There are a few MPs who do not represent any of the main political parties. They are called ‘independents’ and usually represent an issue important to their constituency.
zh: 有少数下议院议员不代表任何主要政党。他们被称为“独立人士”，通常代表对其选区重要的议题。

heading: 5.6 The government
en: The main political parties actively look for members of the public to join their debates, contribute to their costs, and help at elections for Parliament or for local government. They have branches in most constituencies and hold policy-making conferences every year.
zh: 主要政党积极寻找公众成员加入他们的辩论、为他们的活动提供资金，并在议会或地方政府选举中提供帮助。他们在大多数选区设有分支机构，并每年举行制定政策的会议。

heading: 5.6 The government
en: Pressure and lobby groups are organisations which try to influence government policy. They play an important role in politics. Some are representative organisations such as the CBI (Confederation of British Industry), which represents the views of British business. Others campaign on particular topics, such as the environment (for example, Greenpeace) or human rights (for example, Liberty).
zh: 压力和游说团体是试图影响政府政策的组织。它们在政治中发挥着重要作用。有些是代表性组织，如英国工业联合会（CBI），代表英国企业的观点。其他则针对特定主题进行倡导，例如环境（例如绿色和平）或人权（例如自由）。

heading: 5.6 The government
en: Civil servants support the government in developing and implementing its policies. They also deliver public services. Civil servants are accountable to ministers. They are chosen on merit and are politically neutral – they are not political appointees. People can apply to join the civil service through an application process, like other jobs in the UK. Civil servants are expected to carry out their role with dedication and a commitment to the civil service and its core values. These are: integrity, honesty, objectivity and impartiality (including being politically neutral).
zh: 公务员支持政府制定和实施政策。他们还提供公共服务。公务员对大臣负责。他们根据才能选拔，且保持政治中立——他们不是政治任命人员。人们可以通过申请程序加入公务员系统，就像英国其他工作一样。公务员被期望以奉献精神和对公务员系统及核心价值观的承诺来履行职责。这些价值观包括：正直、诚实、客观和公正（包括保持政治中立）。

heading: 5.6 The government
en: Towns, cities and rural areas in the UK are governed by democratically elected councils, often called ‘local authorities’. Some areas have both district and county councils, which have different functions. Most large towns and cities have a single local authority.
zh: 英国的城镇、城市和农村地区由民主选举产生的地方议会治理，通常称为“地方政府”。一些地区同时设有区议会和县议会，它们具有不同的职能。大多数大型城镇和城市只有一个单一的地方政府机构。

heading: 5.6 The government
en: Local authorities provide a range of services in their areas. They are funded by money from central government and by local taxes.
zh: 地方政府在其辖区内提供一系列服务。它们的资金来源于中央政府拨款和地方税收。

heading: 5.6 The government
en: Many local authorities appoint a mayor, who is the ceremonial leader of the council. In some towns, a mayor is elected to be the effective leader of the administration. London has 33 local authorities, with the Greater London Authority and the Mayor of London coordinating policies across the capital. For most local authorities, local elections for councillors are held in May every year. Many candidates stand for council election as members of a political party.
zh: 许多地方议会任命一位市长，作为该议会的礼仪性领袖。在一些城镇，市长通过选举产生，担任行政管理的实际领导者。伦敦有33个地方政府单位，由大伦敦管理局和伦敦市长协调全城的政策。对于大多数地方政府而言，议员的地方选举每年五月举行。许多候选人以政党成员的身份参选地方议会席位。

heading: 5.6 The government
en: Since 1997, some powers have been devolved from the central government to give people in Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland more control over matters that directly affect them. There has been a Welsh Assembly and a Scottish Parliament since 1999. There is also a Northern Ireland Assembly, although this has been suspended on a few occasions.
zh: 自1997年以来，部分权力已从中央政府下放，使威尔士、苏格兰和北爱尔兰的人民能够更多地掌控直接影响他们的事务。自1999年起，设立了威尔士议会和苏格兰议会。此外还设有北爱尔兰议会，尽管该议会在某些时期曾一度暂停运作。

heading: 5.6 The government
en: Policy and laws governing defence, foreign affairs, immigration, taxation and social security all remain under central UK government control. However, many other public services, such as education, are controlled by the devolved administrations.
zh: 关于国防、外交事务、移民、税收和社会保障的政策和法律仍由英国中央政府控制。然而，许多其他公共服务（如教育）则由权力下放政府管理。

heading: 5.6 The government
en: The devolved administrations each have their own civil service.
zh: 各权力下放政府各自拥有自己的公务员系统。

heading: 5.6 The government
en: The Welsh government and National Assembly for Wales are based in Cardiff, the capital city of Wales. The National Assembly has 60 Assembly members (AMs) and elections are held every four years using a form of proportional representation. Members can speak in either Welsh or English and all of the Assembly’s publications are in both languages.
zh: 威尔士政府和威尔士国民议会位于威尔士首府加的夫。国民议会共有60名议员（AMs），每四年举行一次选举，采用一种比例代表制。议员可以使用威尔士语或英语发言，议会的所有出版物均使用这两种语言。

heading: 5.6 The government
en: The Assembly has the power to make laws for Wales in 20 areas, including:
zh: 威尔士议会在20个领域拥有为威尔士制定法律的权力，包括：

heading: 5.6 The government
en:
• education and training
• health and social services
• economic development
• housing.
zh:
• 教育和培训
• 健康和社会服务
• 经济发展
• 住房。

heading: 5.6 The government
en: Since 2011, the National Assembly for Wales has been able to pass laws on these topics without the agreement of the UK Parliament.
zh: 自2011年起，威尔士议会（Welsh Assembly）无需英国议会的同意即可就这些议题通过法律。

heading: 5.6 The government
en: The Scottish Parliament was formed in 1999. It sits in Edinburgh, the capital city of Scotland.
zh: 苏格兰议会（Scottish Parliament）于1999年成立。它位于苏格兰首府爱丁堡（Edinburgh）。

heading: 5.6 The government
en: There are 129 members of the Scottish Parliament (MSPs), elected by a form of proportional representation. The Scottish Parliament can pass laws for Scotland on all matters which are not specifically reserved to the UK Parliament. The matters on which the Scottish Parliament can legislate include:
zh: 苏格兰议会有129名议员（MSPs），通过一种比例代表制选举产生。苏格兰议会可就所有未明确保留给英国议会的事项为苏格兰制定法律。苏格兰议会可立法的领域包括：

heading: 5.6 The government
en:
• civil and criminal law
• health
• education
• planning
• additional tax-raising powers.
zh:
• 民事和刑法
• 卫生
• 教育
• 规划
• 额外的征税权。

heading: 5.6 The government
en: A Northern Ireland Parliament was established in 1922, when Ireland was divided, but it was abolished in 1972, shortly after the Troubles broke out in 1969.
zh: 1922年爱尔兰分裂时，北爱尔兰议会（Northern Ireland Assembly）成立，但在1972年被废除，当时北爱尔兰问题时期（the Troubles）于1969年爆发不久。

heading: 5.6 The government
en: The Northern Ireland Assembly was established soon after the Belfast Agreement (or Good Friday Agreement) in 1998. There is a power-sharing agreement which distributes ministerial Offices amongst the main parties. The Assembly has 90 elected members, known as MLAs (members of the Legislative Assembly). They are elected with a form of proportional representation.
zh: 北爱尔兰议会（Northern Ireland Assembly）于1998年《贝尔法斯特协议》（或称《受难日协议》Good Friday Agreement）后不久成立。该协议是一项权力分享安排，将部长职位分配给主要政党。议会共有90名民选成员，称为立法会议员（MLAs）。他们通过一种比例代表制选举产生。

heading: 5.6 The government
en: The Northern Ireland Assembly can make decisions on issues such as:
zh: 北爱尔兰议会（Northern Ireland Assembly）可就以下问题作出决定：

heading: 5.6 The government
en:
• education
• agriculture
• the environment
• health
• social services.
zh:
• 教育
• 农业
• 环境
• 卫生
• 社会服务。

heading: 5.6 The government
en: The UK government has the power to suspend all devolved assemblies. It has used this power several times in Northern Ireland when local political leaders found it difficult to work together. However, the Assembly has been running successfully since 2007.
zh: 英国政府有权暂停所有权力下放政府（devolved administrations）。当地方政治领导人难以合作时，英国政府曾在北爱尔兰多次行使这一权力。然而，自2007年以来，该议会一直成功运作。

heading: 5.6 The government
en: Proceedings in Parliament are broadcast on television and published in official reports called Hansard . Written reports can be found in large libraries and
zh: 议会 proceedings 通过电视广播播出，并发表在名为《汉萨德》（Hansard）的官方报告中。书面报告可在大型图书馆找到，以及

heading: 5.6 The government
en: at www.parliament.uk. Most people get information about political issues and events from newspapers (often called ‘the press’), television, radio and the internet.
zh: 网址为 www.parliament.uk。大多数人通过报纸（通常称为“媒体”）、电视、广播和互联网获取有关政治议题和事件的信息。

heading: 5.6 The government
en: The UK has a free press. This means that what is written in newspapers is free from government control. Some newspaper owners and editors hold strong political opinions and run campaigns to try to influence government policy and public opinion.
zh: 英国实行新闻自由。这意味着报纸上的内容不受政府控制。一些报纸所有者和编辑持有强烈的政治观点，并开展运动以试图影响政府政策和公众舆论。

heading: 5.6 The government
en: By law, radio and television coverage of the political parties must be balanced and so
zh: 根据法律规定，广播电台和电视台对各大政党的报道必须保持平衡，因此

heading: 5.6 The government
en:
• The role of the Prime Minister, cabinet, opposition and shadow cabinet
• The role of political parties in the UK system of government
• Who the main political parties are
• What pressure and lobby groups do
• The role of the civil service
• The role of local government
• The powers of the devolved governments in Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland
• How proceedings in Parliament are recorded
• The role of the media in keeping people informed about political issues
zh:
• 首相、内阁、反对党和影子内阁的作用
• 政党在英国政府体制中的作用
• 主要政党有哪些
• 压力和游说团体做什么
• 公务员系统的作用
• 地方政府的作用
• 威尔士、苏格兰和北爱尔兰的权力下放政府的权力
• 议会会议记录的方式
• 媒体在让人们了解政治问题方面的作用

heading: 5.6.1 Who can vote?
en: The UK has had a fully democratic voting system since 1928. The present voting age of 18 was set in 1969 and (with a few exceptions) all UK-born and naturalised adult citizens have the right to vote.
zh: 自1928年起，英国已实行完全民主的投票制度。目前的18岁投票年龄于1969年确立，（除少数例外情况外）所有在英国出生及入籍的成年公民均拥有投票权。

heading: 5.6.1 Who can vote?
en: Adult citizens of the UK, and citizens of the Commonwealth and the Irish Republic who are resident in the UK, can vote in all public elections. Adult citizens of other EU states who are resident in the UK can vote in all elections except General Elections.
zh: 英国成年公民、居住在英国的英联邦公民以及爱尔兰共和国公民有权参加所有公共选举。居住在英国的其他欧盟国家成年公民可以参加除大选以外的所有选举。

heading: 5.6.1 Who can vote?
en: To be able to vote in a parliamentary, local or European election, you must have your name on the electoral register.
zh: 若要参加议会选举、地方选举或欧洲议会选举，您的名字必须登记在选民名册上。

heading: 5.6.1 Who can vote?
en: If you are eligible to vote, you can register by contacting your local council electoral registration Office. This is usually based at your local council (in Scotland it may be based elsewhere). If you don’t know which local authority you come under, you can find out by visiting www.aboutmyvote.co.uk and entering your postcode. You can also
zh: 如果您有资格投票，可以通过联系您所在的地方议会选民登记办公室进行登记。该办公室通常设在您的地方议会所在地（在苏格兰可能位于其他地方）。如果您不清楚自己隶属于哪个地方政府，可以访问 www.aboutmyvote.co.uk 并输入您的邮政编码以查询。您还可以

heading: 5.6.1 Who can vote?
en: equal time has to be given to rival viewpoints.
zh: 必须给予对立观点同等的播出时间。

heading: 5.6.1 Who can vote?
en: download voter registration forms in English, Welsh and some other languages.
zh: 下载英文、威尔士文和一些其他语言的选民登记表。

heading: 5.6.1 Who can vote?
en: The electoral register is updated every year in September or October. An electoral registration form is sent to every household and this has to be completed and returned with the names of everyone who is resident in the household and eligible to vote.
zh: 选民名册每年9月或10月更新一次。每个家庭都会收到一份选民登记表，需填写并寄回，列出所有居住在该户内且有资格投票的人员姓名。

heading: 5.6.1 Who can vote?
en: In Northern Ireland a different system operates. This is called ‘individual registration’ and all those entitled to vote must complete their own registration form. Once registered, people stay on the register provided their personal details do not change. For more information see the Electoral Office for Northern Ireland website at www.eoni.org.uk
zh: 在北爱尔兰，实行的是不同的制度。这被称为“个人登记”，所有有投票资格的人都必须亲自完成自己的登记表。一旦登记成功，只要个人信息没有变更，人们就会一直保留在选民名册上。更多信息请参阅北爱尔兰选举办公室网站 www.eoni.org.uk

heading: 5.6.1 Who can vote?
en: By law, each local authority has to make its electoral register available for anyone to look at, although this has to be supervised. The register is kept at each local electoral registration Office (or council Office in England and Wales). It is also possible to see the register at some public buildings such as libraries.
zh: 根据法律规定，每个地方政府都必须提供其选民名册供任何人查阅，尽管这需要受到监督。选民名册保存在各地的地方选举登记办公室（在英格兰和威尔士则为地方议会办公室）。在一些公共建筑（如图书馆）也可以查阅选民名册。

heading: 5.6.1 Who can vote?
en: People vote in elections at places called polling stations, or polling places in Scotland. Before the election you will be sent a poll card. This tells you where your polling station or polling place is and when the election will take place. On election day, the polling station or place will be open from 7.00 am until 10.00 pm.
zh: 人们在称为投票站的地点进行投票，在苏格兰则称为投票点。选举前，你会收到一张投票卡。这张卡片会告知你的投票站或投票点的位置以及选举举行的时间。在选举日，投票站或投票点的开放时间是从上午7点至晚上10点。

heading: 5.6.1 Who can vote?
en: When you arrive at the polling station, the staff will ask for your name and address. In Northern Ireland you will also have to show photographic identification. You will then get your ballot paper, which you take to a polling booth to fill in privately. You should make up your own mind who to vote for. No one has the right to make you vote for a particular candidate. You should follow the instructions on the ballot paper. Once you have completed it, put it in the ballot box.
zh: 当你到达投票站时，工作人员会要求你提供姓名和地址。在北爱尔兰，你还必须出示带有照片的身份证件。然后你会拿到选票，带到投票隔间内私下填写。你应该根据自己的意愿决定投票给谁。任何人无权强迫你投票给特定候选人。请按照选票上的说明进行操作。填好后，将选票投入票箱。

heading: 5.6.1 Who can vote?
en: If it is difficult for you to get to a polling station or polling place, you can register for a postal ballot. Your ballot paper will be sent to your home before the election. You then fill it in and post it back. You can choose to do this when you register to vote.
zh: 如果你难以前往投票站或投票点，可以登记参加邮寄投票。你的选票将在选举前寄送到你家。然后你填写好并寄回。你可以在登记投票时选择这种方式。

heading: 5.6.1 Who can vote?
en: Most citizens of the UK, the Irish Republic or the Commonwealth aged 18 or over can stand for public Office. There are some exceptions, including:
zh: 大多数年满18岁的英国、爱尔兰共和国或英联邦公民可以竞选公职。但也有一些例外情况，包括：

heading: 5.6.1 Who can vote?
en:
• members of the armed forces
• civil servants
• people found guilty of certain criminal offences.
zh:
• 武装部队成员
• 公务员
• 被判定犯有某些刑事罪行的人。

heading: 5.6.1 Who can vote?
en: Members of the House of Lords may not stand for election to the House of Commons but are eligible for all other public Offices.
zh: 上议院议员不得竞选下议院席位，但有资格竞选其他所有公职。

heading: 5.6.1 Who can vote?
en: The public can listen to debates in the Palace of Westminster from public galleries in both the House of Commons and the House of Lords.
zh: 公众可以在下议院和上议院的公共旁听席旁听威斯敏斯特宫的辩论。

heading: 5.6.1 Who can vote?
en: You can write to your local MP in advance to ask for tickets or you can queue on the day at the public entrance. Entrance is free. Sometimes there are long queues for the House of Commons and people have to wait for at least one to two hours. It is usually easier to get in to the House of Lords.
zh: 你可以提前写信给你当地的下议院议员（MP）索要门票，或者在当天在公众入口排队入场是免费的。有时下议院会有很长的队伍，人们需要等待至少一到两个小时。进入上议院通常更容易。

heading: 5.6.1 Who can vote?
en: You can find further information on the UK Parliament website at www.parliament.uk.
zh: 你可以在英国议会网站 www.parliament.uk 上找到更多信息。

heading: 5.6.1 Who can vote?
en: There are two ways to arrange a visit to Stormont. You can either contact the Education Service (details are on the Northern Ireland Assembly website at www.niassembly.gov.uk) or contact an MLA.
zh: 安排参观 Stormont（北爱尔兰议会大厦）有两种方式。您可以联系教育服务部门（详细信息见北爱尔兰议会网站 www.niassembly.gov.uk），或者直接联系一名北爱尔兰议会议员（MLA）。

heading: 5.6.1 Who can vote?
en: In Scotland the elected members, called MSPs, meet in the Scottish Parliament building at Holyrood in Edinburgh (for more information, see www.scottish.parliament.uk).
zh: 在苏格兰，当选议员称为苏格兰议会议员（MSP），他们在爱丁堡荷里路德的苏格兰议会大楼开会（更多信息，见 www.scottish.parliament.uk）。

heading: 5.6.1 Who can vote?
en: You can get information, book tickets or arrange tours through visitor services. You can write to them at the Scottish Parliament, Edinburgh, EH99 1SP, telephone 0131 348 5200 or email sp.bookings@scottish.parliament.uk
zh: 您可以通过游客服务中心获取信息、预订门票或安排参观。您可以写信至：苏格兰议会，爱丁堡，EH99 1SP，电话 0131 348 5200，或电子邮件 sp.bookings@scottish.parliament.uk。

heading: 5.6.1 Who can vote?
en: In Wales the elected members, known as AMs, meet in the Welsh Assembly in the Senedd in Cardiff Bay (for more information, see www.wales.gov.uk).
zh: 在威尔士，当选议员称为威尔士议会议员（AM），他们在加的夫湾的威尔士议会（Senedd）开会（更多信息，见 www.wales.gov.uk）。

heading: 5.6.1 Who can vote?
en: The Senedd is an open building. You can book guided tours or seats in the public galleries for the Welsh Assembly. To make a booking, contact the Assembly Booking Service on 0845 010 5500 or email assembly.bookings@wales.gsi.gov.uk
zh: Senedd（威尔士议会大厦）是一座开放式建筑。您可以预订威尔士议会的导览团或公众席位的座位。如需预订，请联系议会预订服务部门，电话 0845 010 5500，或电子邮件 assembly.bookings@wales.gsi.gov.uk。

heading: 5.6.1 Who can vote?
en:

• How you register to vote

• How you can visit Parliament, the Northern Ireland Assembly, the Scottish Parliament and the Welsh Assembly
zh:

heading: 5.7 The UK and international institutions
en: The Commonwealth is an association of countries that support each other and work together towards shared goals in democracy and development. Most member states were once part of the British Empire, although a few countries which were not have also joined.
zh: 英联邦是一个国家联盟，各国相互支持，并在民主与发展方面共同努力以实现共同目标。大多数成员国曾是大英帝国的一部分，尽管也有一些非前帝国成员加入。

heading: 5.7 The UK and international institutions
en: The monarch (currently King Charles III) is the ceremonial head of the Commonwealth, which currently has 54 member states. Membership is voluntary. The Commonwealth has no power over its members, although it can suspend membership. The Commonwealth is based on the core values of democracy, good government and the rule of law.
zh: 君主（现任为查尔斯三世国王）是英联邦的礼仪性元首，英联邦目前拥有54个成员国。 membership 是自愿的。英联邦对成员国没有管辖权，但可以暂停其成员资格。英联邦建立在民主、善治和法治的核心价值基础之上。

heading: 5.7 The UK and international institutions
en: There 56 member states:
zh: 共有56个成员国：

heading: 5.7 The UK and international institutions
en: The European Union (EU), originally called the European Economic Community (EEC), was set up by six western European countries (Belgium, France, Germany, Italy, Luxembourg and the Netherlands) who signed the Treaty of Rome on 25 March 1957.
zh: 欧洲联盟（EU），最初称为欧洲经济共同体（EEC），由六个西欧国家（比利时、法国、德国、意大利、卢森堡和荷兰）于1957年3月25日签署《罗马条约》后建立。

heading: 5.7 The UK and international institutions
en: The UK originally decided not to join this group but it became a member in 1973. The UK left the EU after the Brexit vote. Brexit officially took place at 23:00 GMT on 31 January 2020.
zh: 英国最初决定不加入该组织，但于1973年成为成员国。英国在脱欧公投后离开了欧盟。脱欧于2020年1月31日格林尼治标准时间23:00正式生效。

heading: 5.7 The UK and international institutions
en: There are now 27 EU member states.
zh: 目前共有27个欧盟成员国。
