heading: 5.7 The UK and international institutions
en: With effect from 2024, no general principle of EU law is part of UK law. European laws are called directives, regulations or framework decisions.
zh: 自2024年起，欧盟法律的一般原则不再构成英国法律的一部分。欧洲法律被称为指令、条例或框架决定。

heading: 5.7 The UK and international institutions
en: The Council of Europe is separate from the EU. It has 47 member countries, including the UK, and is responsible for the protection and promotion of human rights in those countries. It has no power to make laws but draws up conventions and charters, the most well-known of which is the European Convention on Human Rights and Fundamental Freedoms, usually called the European Convention on Human Rights.
zh: 欧洲委员会与欧盟是分开的。它有47个成员国，包括英国，负责保护及促进这些国家的人权。它无权制定法律，但起草公约和宪章，其中最著名的是《欧洲人权与基本自由公约》，通常简称为《欧洲人权公约》。

heading: 5.7 The UK and international institutions
en: The UK is part of the United Nations (UN), an international organisation with more than 190 countries as members. The UN was set up after the Second World War and aims to prevent war and promote international peace and security. There are 15 members on the UN Security Council, which recommends action when there are international crises and threats to peace. The UK is one of five permanent members of the Security Council.
zh: 英国是联合国（United Nations）的成员，这是一个拥有超过190个成员国的国际组织。联合国在第二次世界大战后成立，旨在防止战争并促进国际和平与安全。联合国安全理事会有15个成员国，当出现国际危机和和平威胁时，安理会会建议采取行动。英国是安理会五个常任理事国之一。

heading: 5.7 The UK and international institutions
en: The UK is also a member of NATO. NATO is a group of European and North American countries that have agreed to help each other if they come under attack. It also aims to maintain peace between all of its members.
zh: 英国也是北约（NATO）的成员。北约是由欧洲和北美国家组成的集团，这些国家同意在遭受攻击时相互援助。它还旨在维持所有成员国之间的和平。

heading: 5.7 The UK and international institutions
en:
• What the Commonwealth is and its role
• Other international organisations of which the UK is a member
zh:
• 英联邦是什么及其作用
• 英国是成员的其他国际组织

heading: 5.8 Your role in the community
en: Becoming a British citizen or settling in the UK brings responsibilities but also opportunities. Everyone has the opportunity to participate in their community. This section looks at some of the responsibilities of being a citizen and gives information about how you can help to make your community a better place to live and
zh: 成为英国公民或定居英国既带来责任，也带来机遇。每个人都有机会参与社区事务。本节将介绍作为公民的一些责任，并提供关于如何帮助使您的社区成为更宜居地方的信息，以及

heading: 5.8 Your role in the community
en: work.
zh: 工作方面的内容。

heading: 5.8 Your role in the community
en: Although Britain is one of the world’s most diverse societies, there is a set of shared values and responsibilities that everyone can agree with. These values and
zh: 尽管英国是世界上最多元化的社会之一，但有一套所有人都能认同的共同价值观和责任。这些价值观和

heading: 5.8 Your role in the community
en: responsibilities include:
zh: 责任包括：

heading: 5.8 Your role in the community
en:
• to obey and respect the law
• to be aware of the rights of others and respect those rights
• to treat others with fairness
• to behave responsibly
• to help and protect your family
• to respect and preserve the environment
• to treat everyone equally, regardless of sex, race, religion, age, disability, class or sexual orientation
zh:
• 遵守并尊重法律
• 了解他人的权利并尊重这些权利
• 公平对待他人
• 负责任地行为
• 帮助和保护你的家人
• 尊重并保护环境
• 平等对待每个人，无论其性别、种族、宗教、年龄、残疾、阶级或性取向如何

heading: 5.8 Your role in the community
en:
• to work to provide for yourself and your family
• to help others
• to vote in local and national government elections.
zh:
• 努力工作以供养自己和家人
• 帮助他人
• 在地方和国家政府选举中投票。

heading: 5.8 Your role in the community
en: Taking on these values and responsibilities will make it easier for you to become a full and active citizen.
zh: 承担这些价值观和责任将使你更容易成为一位全面且积极的公民。

heading: 5.8 Your role in the community
en: When you move into a new house or apartment, introduce yourself to the people who live near you. Getting to know your neighbours can help you to become part of the community and make friends. Your neighbours are also a good source of help – for example, they may be willing to feed your pets if you are away, or offer
zh: 当你搬进新的房子或公寓时，向住在附近的人自我介绍。与邻居建立联系可以帮助你融入社区并结交朋友。你的邻居也是很好的帮助来源——例如，如果你外出，他们可能愿意喂养你的宠物，或者提供

heading: 5.8 Your role in the community
en: advice on local shops and services.
zh: 关于当地商店和服务的建议。

heading: 5.8 Your role in the community
en: You can help prevent any problems and conflicts with your neighbours by respecting their privacy and limiting how much noise you make. Also try to keep your
zh: 通过尊重他们的隐私并限制你制造的噪音，你可以帮助预防与邻居之间的任何问题和冲突。此外，尽量保持你的

heading: 5.8 Your role in the community
en: garden tidy, and only put your refuse bags and bins on the street or in communal areas if they are due to be collected.
zh: 花园整洁，并且只在垃圾袋和垃圾桶需要收集时才将其放在街道上或公共区域。

heading: 5.8 Your role in the community
en: Volunteering and helping your community are an important part of being a good citizen. They enable you to integrate and get to know other people. It helps to make your community a better place if residents support each other. It also helps you to fulfil your duties as a citizen, such as behaving responsibly and helping others.
zh: 志愿服务和帮助社区是成为好公民的重要组成部分。它们使你能够融入社会并结识他人。如果居民之间相互支持，有助于使你的社区变得更好。这也有助于你履行作为公民的义务，例如负责任地行事和帮助他人。

heading: 5.8.1 How you can support your community
en: There are a number of positive ways in which you can support your community and be a good citizen.
zh: 您可以通过多种积极的方式支持社区，成为一名好公民。

heading: 5.8.1 How you can support your community
en: As well as getting the right to vote, people on the electoral register are randomly selected to serve on a jury. Anyone who is on the electoral register and is aged 18 to 70 can be asked to do this.
zh: 除了获得投票权外，选民名册上的人还会被随机选中担任陪审团成员。任何在选民名册上且年龄在18至70岁之间的人都可能被要求履行这一义务。

heading: 5.8.1 How you can support your community
en: If you have children, there are many ways in which you can help at their schools. Parents can often help in classrooms, by supporting activities or listening to children read.
zh: 如果您有孩子，您可以通过多种方式在学校提供帮助。家长通常可以在教室里帮忙，例如支持各类活动或倾听孩子们朗读。

heading: 5.8.1 How you can support your community
en: Many schools organise events to raise money for extra equipment or out-of- school activities. Activities might include book sales, toy sales or bringing food to sell. You might have good ideas of your own for raising money. Sometimes events are organised by parent-teacher associations (PTAs). Volunteering to help with their events or joining the association is a way of doing something good for the school and also making new friends in your local community. You can find out about these opportunities from notices in the school or notes your children bring home.
zh: 许多学校会组织活动为额外设备或课外活动筹集资金。活动可能包括图书义卖、玩具义卖或带来食物出售。您可能也有自己的筹款好主意。有时，这些活动由家长教师协会（PTA）组织。志愿协助他们的活动或加入该协会，既是为学校做点好事，也是在当地社区结交新朋友的一种方式。您可以通过学校的公告栏或孩子带回家的通知了解这些机会。

heading: 5.8.1 How you can support your community
en: School governors, or members of the school board in Scotland, are people from the local community who wish to make a positive contribution to children’s education. They must be aged 18 or over at the date of their election or appointment. There is no upper age limit.
zh: 学校董事（在学校董事会中称为学校董事会成员）是来自当地社区、希望为儿童教育做出积极贡献的人士。他们在当选或被任命时必须年满18岁。没有年龄上限。

heading: 5.8.1 How you can support your community
en: Governors and school boards have an important part to play in raising school standards. They have three key roles:
zh: 董事和学校董事会在提高学校标准方面发挥着重要作用。他们有三个关键角色：

heading: 5.8.1 How you can support your community
en:
• setting the strategic direction of the school
• ensuring accountability
• monitoring and evaluating school performance.
zh:
• 设定学校的战略方向
• 确保问责制
• 监督和评估学校表现。

heading: 5.8.1 How you can support your community
en: You can contact your local school to ask if they need a new governor or school board member. In England, you can also apply online at the School Governors’ One-Stop Shop at www.sgoss.org.uk. In England, parents and other community groups can apply to open a free school in their local area. More information about this can be found on the Department for Education website at www.dfe.gov.uk.
zh: 您可以联系当地学校，询问他们是否需要新的董事或学校董事会成员。在英格兰，您还可以在线申请，访问学校董事一站式服务网站 www.sgoss.org.uk。在英格兰，家长和其他社区团体可以申请在当地开设免费学校。更多信息可在教育部网站 www.dfe.gov.uk 上找到。

heading: 5.8.1 How you can support your community
en: Political parties welcome new members. Joining one is a way to demonstrate your support for certain views and to get involved in the democratic process.
zh: 政党欢迎新成员加入。加入一个政党是表明你支持某些观点并参与民主进程的一种方式。

heading: 5.8.1 How you can support your community
en: Political parties are particularly busy at election times. Members work hard to persuade people to vote for their candidates – for instance, by handing out leaflets in the street or by knocking on people’s doors and asking for their support. This is called ‘canvassing’. You don’t have to tell a canvasser how you intend to vote if you don’t want to.
zh: 在选举期间，各政党尤为忙碌。党员们努力工作，说服人们投票给他们的候选人——例如，通过在街上分发传单或挨家挨户敲门请求支持。这被称为“拉票”。如果你不愿意，你不必告诉拉票者你打算如何投票。

heading: 5.8.1 How you can support your community
en: British citizens can stand for Office as a local councillor, a member of Parliament (or the devolved equivalents) or a member of the European Parliament. This is an opportunity to become even more involved in the political life of the UK. You may also be able to stand for Office if you are an Irish citizen, an eligible Commonwealth citizen or (except for standing to be an MP) a citizen of another EU country.
zh: 英国公民可以竞选地方议会议员、下议院议员（或权力下放政府的对应职位）或欧洲议会议员。这是进一步参与英国政治生活的机会。如果你是爱尔兰公民、符合条件的英联邦公民，或者是其他欧盟国家公民（除竞选下议院议员外），你也可以参选。

heading: 5.8.1 How you can support your community
en: You can find out more about joining a political party from the individual party websites.
zh: 你可以从各政党的官方网站上了解有关加入政党的更多信息。

heading: 5.8.1 How you can support your community
en: There are opportunities to volunteer with a wide range of local service providers, including local hospitals and youth projects. Services often want to involve local people in decisions about the way in which they work. Universities, housing associations, museums and arts councils may advertise for people to serve as volunteers in their governing bodies.
zh: 有许多机会可以在各种本地服务提供商处进行志愿服务，包括当地医院和青年项目。服务机构通常希望让当地人参与关于其工作方式的决策。大学、住房协会、博物馆和艺术委员会可能会招聘人员在其管理机构中担任志愿者。

heading: 5.8.1 How you can support your community
en: You can volunteer with the police, and become a special constable or a lay (non-police) representative. You can also apply to become a magistrate. You will often find advertisements for vacancies in your local newspaper or on local radio. You can also find out more about these sorts of roles at www.gov.uk.
zh: 你可以为警方提供志愿服务，成为特别警员或平民（非警察）代表。你也可以申请成为治安法官。你通常会在当地报纸或当地广播电台上找到空缺职位的广告。你还可以在 www.gov.uk 上了解更多关于这类角色的信息。

heading: 5.8.1 How you can support your community
en: Donated blood is used by hospitals to help people with a wide range of injuries and illnesses. Giving blood only takes about an hour to do. You can register to give blood at:
zh: 捐赠的血液被医院用于帮助患有各种伤病的人。献血大约需要一个小时。你可以注册献血：

heading: 5.8.1 How you can support your community
en:
• England and North Wales: www.blood.co.uk
• Rest of Wales: www.welsh-blood.org.uk
• Scotland: www.scotblood.co.uk
• Northern Ireland: www.nibts.org
zh:
• 英格兰和北威尔士：www.blood.co.uk
• 威尔士其他地区：www.welsh-blood.org.uk
• 苏格兰：www.scotblood.co.uk
• 北爱尔兰：www.nibts.org

heading: 5.8.1 How you can support your community
en: Many people in the UK are waiting for organ transplants. If you register to be an organ donor, it can make it easier for your family to decide whether to donate your organs when you die. You can register to be an organ donor at www.organdonation.nhs.uk. Living people can also donate a kidney.
zh: 许多英国人正在等待器官移植。如果您注册成为器官捐献者，这将使您的家人在您去世时更容易决定是否捐献您的器官。您可以在 www.organdonation.nhs.uk 注册成为器官捐献者。活人也可以捐赠肾脏。

heading: 5.8.1 How you can support your community
en: Other ways to volunteer
zh: 其他志愿服务方式

heading: 5.8.1 How you can support your community
en: Volunteering is working for good causes without payment. There are many benefits to volunteering, such as meeting new people helping make your community a better place. Some volunteer activities will give you a chance to practise your English or develop work skills that will help you find a job or improve your curriculum vitae (CV). Many people volunteer simply because they want to help other people.
zh: 志愿服务是指为公益目的而无偿工作。志愿服务有许多好处，例如结识新朋友、帮助使您的社区变得更好。一些志愿活动将为您提供练习英语或发展职业技能的机会，这将帮助您找到工作或改善您的简历（CV）。许多人进行志愿服务仅仅是因为他们想帮助他人。

heading: 5.8.1 How you can support your community
en: Activities you can do as a volunteer include:
zh: 您可以作为志愿者从事的活动包括：

heading: 5.8.1 How you can support your community
en:
• working with animals – for example, caring for animals at a local rescue shelter
• youth work – for example, volunteering at a youth group
• helping improve the environment – for example, participating in a litter pick-up in the local area
• working with the homeless in, for example, a homelessness shelter
• mentoring – for example, supporting someone who has just come out of prison
• work in health and hospitals – for example, working on an information desk in a hospital
• helping older people at, for example, a residential care home.
zh:
• 与动物一起工作——例如，在当地的救援收容所照顾动物
• 青少年工作——例如，在青年团体进行志愿服务
• 帮助改善环境——例如，参与当地地区的垃圾清理活动
• 帮助无家可归者，例如在无家可归者收容所工作
• 导师指导——例如，支持刚出狱的人
• 在医院和卫生领域工作——例如，在医院的信息台工作
• 帮助老年人，例如在养老院工作。

heading: 5.8.1 How you can support your community
en: There are thousands of active charities and voluntary organisations in the UK. They work to improve the lives of people, animals and the environment in many different ways. They range from the British branches of international organisations, such as the British Red Cross, to small local charities working in particular areas. They include charities working with older people (such as Age UK), with children (for example, the National Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Children (NSPCC)), and with the homeless (for example, Crisis and Shelter). There are also medical research charities (for example, Cancer Research UK), environmental charities (including the National Trust and Friends of the Earth) and charities working with animals (such as the People’s Dispensary for Sick Animals (PDSA)).
zh: 英国有成千上万个活跃的慈善机构和志愿组织。它们以多种方式努力改善人们、动物和环境的生活。这些机构包括国际组织的英国分支机构，如英国红十字会（British Red Cross），也包括在特定领域工作的小型当地慈善机构。其中包括为老年人服务的慈善机构（如 Age UK）、为儿童服务的慈善机构（例如防止虐待儿童全国协会（NSPCC））以及为无家可归者服务的慈善机构（例如 Crisis 和 Shelter）。还有医学研究慈善机构（例如英国癌症研究中心 Cancer Research UK）、环保慈善机构（包括国民信托 National Trust 和地球之友 Friends of the Earth）以及与动物相关的慈善机构（如病人动物人民药房 PDSA）。

heading: 5.8.1 How you can support your community
en: Volunteers are needed to help with their activities and to raise money. The charities often advertise in local newspapers, and most have websites that
zh: 需要志愿者来帮助开展活动并筹集资金。慈善机构通常在当地报纸上刊登广告，并且大多数都有网站提供

heading: 5.8.1 How you can support your community
en: include information about their opportunities. You can also get information about volunteering for different organisations from www.do-it.org.uk.
zh: 关于其机会的信息。您还可以从 www.do-it.org.uk 获取有关不同组织志愿服务的信息。

heading: 5.8.1 How you can support your community
en: There are many opportunities for young people to volunteer and receive accreditation which will help them to develop their skills. These include the National Citizen Service programme, which gives 16- and 17-year-olds the opportunity to enjoy outdoor activities, develop their skills and take part in a community project. You can find out more about these opportunities as follows:
zh: 年轻人有许多志愿服务的机会，并获得认证，这将有助于他们发展技能。其中包括“国家公民服务”（National Citizen Service）计划，该计划为16至17岁的青少年提供参与户外活动、发展技能并参加社区项目的机会。你可以通过以下方式了解更多关于这些机会的信息：

heading: 5.8.1 How you can support your community
en:
• National Citizen Service: at nationalcitizenservice.direct.gov.uk
• England: at www.vinspired.com
• Wales: at www.gwirvol.org
• Scotland: at www.vds.org.uk
• Northern Ireland: at www.volunteernow.co.uk
zh:
• 国家公民服务：访问 nationalcitizenservice.direct.gov.uk
• 英格兰：访问 www.vinspired.com
• 威尔士：访问 www.gwirvol.org
• 苏格兰：访问 www.vds.org.uk
• 北爱尔兰：访问 www.volunteernow.co.uk

heading: 5.8.2 Looking after the environment
en: It is important to recycle as much of your waste as you can. Using recycled materials to make new products uses less energy and means that we do not need to extract more raw materials from the earth. It also means that less rubbish is created, so the amount being put into landfill is reduced.
zh: 尽可能多地回收废弃物非常重要。使用再生材料制造新产品可以节省能源，意味着我们不需要从地球中开采更多的原材料。这也意味着产生的垃圾更少，因此填埋的废物量也会减少。

heading: 5.8.2 Looking after the environment
en: You can learn more about recycling and its benefits at www.recyclenow.com. At this website you can also find out what you can recycle at home and in the local area if you live in England. This
zh: 你可以在 www.recyclenow.com 了解更多关于回收及其益处的信息。如果你居住在英格兰，你还可以在该网站上了解在家中及当地可以回收的物品。这

heading: 5.8.2 Looking after the environment
en: information is available for Wales at www.wasteawarenesswales.org.uk, for Scotland at www.recycleforscotland.com and for Northern Ireland from your local authority.
zh: 些信息在威尔士可通过 www.wasteawarenesswales.org.uk 获取，在苏格兰可通过 www.recycleforscotland.com 获取，在北爱尔兰则可从你的地方政府获取。

heading: 5.8.2 Looking after the environment
en: A good way to support your local community is to shop for products locally where you can. This will help businesses and farmers in your area and in Britain. It will also reduce your carbon footprint, because the products you buy will not have had to travel as far.
zh: 支持当地社区的一个好方法是尽可能在当地购买产品。这将有助于你所在地区以及英国各地的企业和农民。此外，这还将减少你的碳足迹，因为你购买的产品无需长途运输。

heading: 5.8.2 Looking after the environment
en: Walking and using public transport to get around when you can is also a good way to protect the environment. It means that you create less pollution than when you use a car.
zh: 在可能的情况下，步行和使用公共交通工具出行也是保护环境的好方法。这意味着你产生的污染比使用汽车时更少。

heading: 5.8.2 Looking after the environment
en:
• The different ways you can help at your child’s school
• The role of school governors and members of school boards, and how you can become one
• The role of members of political parties
• The different local services people can volunteer to support
• How to donate blood and organs
• The benefits of volunteering for you, other people and the community
• The types of activities that volunteers can do
• How you can look after the environment
zh:
• 你可以在孩子学校提供帮助的不同方式
• 学校理事和学校董事会成员的角色，以及你如何成为其中一员
• 政党成员的角色
• 人们可以志愿支持的不同地方服务
• 如何捐献血液和器官
• 志愿服务对你、他人和社区的好处
• 志愿者可以从事的活动类型
• 你如何保护环境

heading: 6.1 Sources
en: In addition to this PDF version, you can also learn interactively and complete practice materials available online.
zh: 除了这个 PDF 版本之外，您还可以在线进行互动式学习并完成练习材料。

heading: 6.1 Sources
en: • Online study guide
zh: • 在线学习指南

heading: 6.1.2 Physical copy
en:
• Britizen.uk If you prefer to read a physical copy, you can buy a paper copy.
• 45 practice tests
zh:
• Britizen.uk 如果您更喜欢阅读纸质版，可以购买纸质书。
• 45 套练习测试

heading: 6.2 Key Material and Facts
en: A summary of key dates and facts extracted from e-publication Life in the United Kingdom: A guide for new residents, 3rd Edition. We recommend you reading the
zh: 从电子出版物《英国生活：新居民指南》第3版中提取的关键日期和事实摘要。建议您阅读

heading: 6.2 Key Material and Facts
en: source material to get a better context for these facts.
zh: 原始资料，以便更好地了解这些事实的背景。

heading: 6.2.1 Key acts
en:
• Magna Carta (1215): reduced rights of the king and laid out basic rights of the people.
• Act for the Government of Wales (under King Henry VII, 1500s): united England and Wales
• Habeas Corpus Act (1679): forbid unlawful imprisonment
• Bill of Rights (1689): confirmed the rights of Parliament and the limits of the king’s power
• Act of Union (1707): united kingdoms of England and Scotland and created Kingdom of GB
• Reform Act (1832): abolished pocket and rotten boroughs and gave more parliamentary seats to towns and cities. Increased number of (male) voters.
• Emancipation Act (1833): abolished slavery throughout British Empire. William Wilberforce was leading abolitionist and Quakers set up first anti-slavery groups. More than 2 million migrants came from India and China to replace labour force.
• Women’s suffrage – 1918 (vote at 30+ yrs) and 1928 (vote at 21 yrs, same as men)
• 1913: Home Rule proposed in Ireland. Idea was to have a self-governing Ireland with its own parliament that still remained part of the UK. WWI postponed any changes. Irish nationalists didn’t want to wait and the Easter Rising against the British in Dublin took place in 1916. Guerrilla war followed.
• 1921: Peace treaty signed splitting Ireland in two
zh:
• 大宪章（Magna Carta, 1215年）：限制了君主的权利，并规定了人民的基本权利。
• 威尔士政府法案（Act for the Government of Wales，在国王亨利七世时期，1500年代）：合并了英格兰和威尔士
• 人身保护法（Habeas Corpus Act, 1679年）：禁止非法监禁
• 权利法案（Bill of Rights, 1689年）：确认了议会（Parliament）的权利并限制了君主的权力
• 联合法案（Act of Union, 1707年）：合并了英格兰和苏格兰王国，并创建了大不列颠王国
• 改革法案（Reform Act, 1832年）：废除了袖珍选区和衰败选区，并将更多议席分配给城镇和城市。增加了（男性）选民人数。
• 废奴法案（Emancipation Act, 1833年）：在整个大英帝国废除了奴隶制。威廉·威尔伯福斯（William Wilberforce）是主要的废奴主义者，贵格会（Quakers）建立了第一个反奴隶制团体。超过200万来自印度和中国的移民来到这里以替代劳动力。
• 女性参政权（Women’s suffrage）– 1918年（30岁以上女性获得投票权）和1928年（21岁获得投票权，与男性相同）
• 1913年：爱尔兰提出自治（Home Rule）提案。其想法是建立一个拥有自己议会但仍属于联合王国一部分的自治爱尔兰。第一次世界大战推迟了所有变革。爱尔兰民族主义者不想等待，1916年在都柏林爆发了反抗英国人的复活节起义（Easter Rising）。随后爆发了游击战。
• 1921年：签署和平条约，将爱尔兰分裂为两部分
exam_note_zh: 考点：大宪章、人身保护法、权利法案、女性参政权等关键法案

heading: 6.2.2 Eras
en:
• Romans: ruled Britain from 43-410 AD (approximately 400 years). Hadrian’s wall built on orders of Roman Emperor Hadrian to keep out tribes (Picts) who lived in what is now Scotland.
• Middle Ages (1066-1485): period of constant war, including Crusades and Hundred Years’ War.
• Elizabethan period (1500s): known for growing patriotism, expanded trade and rich poetry and drama.
• The Enlightenment (1700s): development of new ideas about politics, philosophy and science. Adam Smith (economics) and David Hume (philosopher) influential Scottish thinkers.
• Industrial Revolution (from mid-1700s to 1800s). Britain produced over half of the world’s supplies of cotton cloth, coal and iron. Machinery and stream power developed.
• Victorian Age (1837-1901): Queen Victoria reigned; Britain increased power and influence abroad. Became largest empire in world history.
zh:
• 罗马人（Romans）：在公元43-410年统治不列颠（大约400年）。哈德良长城（Hadrian’s wall）是根据罗马皇帝哈德良的命令建造的，以抵御居住在现在苏格兰地区的部落（皮克特人）。
• 中世纪（Middle Ages, 1066-1485）：不断战争的时期，包括十字军东征（Crusades）和百年战争（Hundred Years’ War）。
• 伊丽莎白时代（Elizabethan period, 1500年代）：以日益高涨的爱国主义、扩大的贸易以及丰富的诗歌和戏剧而闻名。
• 启启蒙运动（The Enlightenment, 1700年代）：政治、哲学和科学新思想的发展。亚当·斯密（Adam Smith，经济学）和大卫·休谟（David Hume，哲学家）是具有影响力的苏格兰思想家。
• 工业革命（Industrial Revolution, 1700年代中期至1800年代）：英国生产了世界上半数以上的棉布、煤炭和钢铁供应。机器和蒸汽动力得到发展。
• 维多利亚时代（Victorian Age, 1837-1901）：维多利亚女王在位；英国增加了在海外的力量和影响力。成为世界历史上最大的帝国。
exam_note_zh: 考点：罗马统治、中世纪、伊丽莎白时代、启蒙运动、工业革命与维多利亚时代

heading: 6.2.2 Eras
en: Middle classes grew significantly and reformers improved conditions for the poor.
zh: 中产阶级显著增长，改革者改善了穷人的境况。

heading: 6.2.3 Modern era
en:
• 1900s: jet engine and radar invented. TV & World Wide Web (Tim Berners-Lee) invented. Hovercraft invented (Sir Christopher Cockrell) and penicillin discovered (Sir Alexander Flemming). ATM invented. Cloned sheep Dolly. Developed Concorde (supersonic jet) with the French. Co-discovered insulin and co-invented the MRI. Structure of DNA molecule discovered. Radio telescope at Jodrell Bank was for many years the world’s largest. Harrier jump jet (takes off vertically). IVF therapy.
• State retirement pension and free school meals introduced before WWI
• 1929: Great Depression. Aviation and automobile industries developed. High unemployment, especially in “heavy” industries (e.g. shipbuilding).
• 1942: Beveridge Report (William Beveridge) set out ideas which led to foundation of modern welfare state.
• 1944: Education Act (R A Butler). Free secondary education and clear distinction between primary and secondary education.
• 1945-1950: NHS and social security system established.
• 1947: 9 colonies gained independence, including India, Pakistan, and Sri Lanka
• 1950s: Post-war labour shortages led to recruitment of workers from India, Pakistan, West Indies and Bangladesh.
• 1960s: Increased wealth and liberalisation of social laws (e.g. abortion and divorce).
• 1973: UK joined the EEC
• 1998: Good Friday Agreement led to establishment of Northern Irish parliament
• 1999: Scottish parliament & Welsh assembly established
zh:
• 1900年代：发明了喷气式发动机和雷达。发明了电视和万维网（World Wide Web，由蒂姆·伯纳斯-李［Tim Berners-Lee］发明）。发明了气垫船（克里斯托弗·科克雷尔爵士［Sir Christopher Cockrell］），发现了青霉素（亚历山大·弗莱明爵士［Sir Alexander Flemming］）。发明了自动取款机（ATM）。克隆羊多莉（Dolly）。与法国人一起研制了协和式客机（超音速喷气式飞机）。共同发现了胰岛素，共同发明了核磁共振成像（MRI）。发现了DNA分子的结构。乔德雷尔班克（Jodrell Bank）的射电望远镜多年来是世界上最大的射电望远镜。鹞式战斗机（垂直起飞）。试管婴儿（IVF）疗法。
• 第一次世界大战（WWI）前引入了国家退休养老金和免费学校餐
• 1929年：大萧条（Great Depression）。航空和汽车工业得到发展。失业率高企，特别是在“重”工业（例如造船业）中。
• 1942年：贝弗里奇报告（Beveridge Report，由威廉·贝弗里奇［William Beveridge］撰写）提出了导致现代福利国家（welfare state）建立的想法。
• 1944年：教育法案（Education Act，由 R A Butler 推动）。免费的中等教育，以及初等和中等教育的明确区分。
• 1945-1950年：建立了国民医疗服务体系（NHS）和社保体系。
• 1947年：9个殖民地获得独立，包括印度、巴基斯坦和斯里兰卡
• 1950年代：战后劳动力短缺导致从印度、巴基斯坦、西印度群岛和孟加拉国招募工人。
• 1960年代：财富增加以及社会法律的自由化（例如堕胎和离婚）。
• 1973年：英国加入欧洲经济共同体（EEC）
• 1998年：受难日协议（Good Friday Agreement）促成了北爱尔兰议会的建立
• 1999年：苏格兰议会和威尔士议会建立
exam_note_zh: 考点：20世纪重大科技发明、贝弗里奇报告、教育法案、NHS建立与地方议会成立

heading: 6.2.4 Battles and wars
en:
• 1066: William of Normandy conquered England at Battle of Hastings (Bayeux Tapestry)
• 1314: Battle of Bannockburn. Scottish King Robert the Bruce defeated the English.
• 1455: War of the Roses. Civil war between House of Lancaster (red rose) and House of York (white rose) to determine who should be king of England. Ended at Battle of Bosworth Field (1485). Henry Tudor of House of Lancaster became King Henry VII and married Elizabeth of York, uniting the two families as the House of Tudor (red rose with a white rose inside).
• Last of the Welsh rebellions had been defeated by mid-15th century.
zh:
• 1066年：威廉（诺曼底公爵）在黑斯廷斯战役中征服英格兰（贝叶挂毯）
• 1314年：班诺克本战役。苏格兰国王罗伯特·布鲁斯击败了英格兰人。
• 1455年：玫瑰战争。兰开斯特家族（红玫瑰）与约克家族（白玫瑰）之间为争夺英格兰王位而爆发的内战。于1485年的博斯沃思原野战役结束。来自兰开斯特家族的亨利·都铎成为亨利七世国王，并与伊丽莎白·约克结婚，将两个家族联合为都铎王朝（红玫瑰内含白玫瑰）。
• 到15世纪中叶，最后一批威尔士叛乱已被平定。

heading: 6.2.4 Battles and wars
en:
• 1588: Spanish Armada was defeated under Elizabeth I
• 1640: Beginning of English Civil War. Parliament (supporters: Roundheads) vs the King (Cavaliers). Charles I introduced Prayer Book; Parliament, made of Puritans, didn’t back him. King’s army defeated at Battles of Marston Moor and Naseby. King Charles I executed.
• 1776: American colonies declare independence over taxation.
• 1805: Battle of Trafalgar. Lord Horatio Nelson (of Nelson’s Column) defeated French (Napoleon) + Spanish fleet.
• 1815: Battle of Waterloo. Lord Wellington defeated Napoleon.
• 1889-1902: Boer War in South Africa.
• 1916: Battle of the Somme WW1. British forces suffered 60,000 causalities on the first day.
• 1918: WWI ended at 11.00 on 11/11.
• 1939: German invasion of Poland led UK and France to declare war on Germany
• 1940 (WWII): Evacuation of Dunkirk. Rescue of 300,000 men by volunteers and small boats.
• 1940 (WWII): Battle of Britain. German/British aerial battle.
• 1982: Argentina invaded Falkland Islands
zh:
• 1588年：在伊丽莎白一世统治期间，西班牙无敌舰队被击败
• 1640年：英国内战开始。议会（支持者：圆颅党）与国王（骑士党）对抗。查理一世颁布《公祷书》；由清教徒组成的议会不支持他。国王的军队在马尔斯顿荒原战役和内兹比战役中战败。国王查理一世被处决。
• 1776年：美国殖民地因税收问题宣布独立。
• 1805年：特拉法加海战。霍雷肖·纳尔逊勋爵（纳尔逊纪念柱的主人）击败了法国（拿破仑）和西班牙舰队。
• 1815年：滑铁卢战役。威灵顿勋爵击败拿破仑。
• 1889-1902年：南非的布尔战争。
• 1916年：第一次世界大战中的索姆河战役。英军在第一天的伤亡人数达6万人。
• 1918年：第一次世界大战于11月11日上午11点结束。
• 1939年：德国入侵波兰，促使英国和法国对德国宣战
• 1940年（第二次世界大战）：敦刻尔克大撤退。志愿者和小船救出了30万人。
• 1940年（第二次世界大战）：不列颠之战。德英之间的空战。
• 1982年：阿根廷入侵福克兰群岛

heading: 6.2.5 Key events
en:
• 1348: Black Death. Killed over 1/3 of Britain. Fewer people meant less need for cereal crops as well as labour shortages, then increased wages. Movement into cities and towns. Gentry (landowners of large plots) and middle class developed.
• 1400: English became preferred language of courts and official documents.
• 1660: The Restoration (of the monarchy). Charles (King of Scotland) invited to come back as King Charles II after Oliver Cromwell’s death.
• 1665: Great Plague
• Glorious Revolution (1688): English Protestants asked Mary’s husband William of Orange (of the Netherlands) to proclaim himself king, as didn’t want a Catholic king. He faced no resistance.
• During Queen Elizabeth I’s reign, English settlers began to move to North American colonies.
zh:
• 1348年：黑死病（Black Death）。夺去了不列颠三分之一以上的人口。人口减少意味着对谷物作物的需求减少以及劳动力短缺，随后工资提高。人口流向城市和城镇。乡绅（Gentry，大块土地的所有者）和中产阶级开始发展。
• 1400年：英语成为法庭和官方文件的首选语言。
• 1660年：王政复辟（The Restoration）。奥利弗·克伦威尔（Oliver Cromwell）死后，苏格兰国王查理受邀返回成为国王查理二世（King Charles II）。
• 1665年：伦敦大瘟疫（Great Plague）
• 光荣革命（Glorious Revolution, 1688年）：英国新教徒邀请玛丽的丈夫、来自荷兰的奥兰治的威廉（William of Orange）宣布自己为国王，因为他们不想要一个天主教国王。he 面对没有阻力。
• 在女王伊丽莎白一世（Queen Elizabeth I）统治期间，英国定居者开始移居北美殖民地。
exam_note_zh: 考点：黑死病、王政复辟、伦敦大瘟疫与光荣革命等关键历史事件

heading: 6.2.6 People
en: • Sir Robert Walpole: first PM (1721-1742) as King George I (a German) relied heavily on ministers because of his poor English
zh: • 罗伯特·沃波尔爵士：首位首相（1721-1742），由于乔治一世国王（德国人）英语不佳，他严重依赖大臣们

heading: 6.2.6 People
en:
• Oliver Cromwell: titled Lord Protector (circa 1640s-1650s) and led Britain whilst it was without a monarch
• King Alfred the Great united the Anglo-Saxon kingdoms of England and defeated the Vikings.
• Isambard Kingdom Brunel: engineer (bridges, trains, tunnels, ships)
• Dylan Thomas: Welsh poet (“Under Milk Wood” & “Do Not Go Gentle into that Good Night”)
• Robert Burns: Scottish poet (“The Bard”), Auld Lang Syne
• Richard Arkwright: Efficient and profitable factory owner during Industrial Revolution
• Sake Dean Mahomet: set up first curry house in Britain and introduced shampooing
• Florence Nightingale: founder of modern nursing
• Emmeline Pankhurst: Suffrage leader
• Rudyard Kipling: Indian-born author and poet. Work reflected idea that British empire was a force for good.
• George and Robert Stevenson: famous pioneers of railway engines
• St Columba & St Augustine: led missionaries from Rome.
• Sir Francis Drake: Elizabethan sailor who helped defeat Spanish Armada and who later sailed around the world.
• Hugunots: French Protestants feeling prosecution settled in England pre-1720
• Henry VIII: famous for marrying 6 times and breaking away from Church of Rome so he could get a divorce. Wales was united with England under his rule. Wives (in order): (1) Catherine of Aragon (2) Anne Boleyn (3) Jane Seymour (4) Anne of Cleves (5) Catherine Howard (6) Catherine Parr
• Margaret Thatcher was first female PM and the longest serving PM of the 20th century.
• Alexander Fleming: Scottish doctor who discovered penicillin (1928)
• Clement Attlee: Churchill’s Deputy PM. Became PM in 1945. Nationalised major industries and created NHS.
• Mary Peters: Olympic athlete who promoted sport and tourism in Northern Ireland.
• Roald Dahl: Welsh author (“Charlie and the Chocolate Factory” & “George’s Marvellous Medicine”).
• Sir Arthur Conan Doyle: Scottish author (“Sherlock Holmes”)
zh:
• 奥利弗·克伦威尔：头衔为护国公（约17世纪40-50年代），在英国没有君主期间领导英国
• 阿尔弗雷德大帝统一了英格兰的盎格鲁-撒克逊王国并击败了维京人。
• 伊桑巴德·金德姆·布鲁内尔：工程师（桥梁、火车、隧道、船只）
• 迪伦·托马斯：威尔士诗人（《牛奶树下》及《不要温和地走进那个良夜》）
• 罗伯特·彭斯：苏格兰诗人（《诗人的歌》、《友谊地久天长》）
• 理查德·阿克莱特：工业革命期间高效且盈利的工厂主
• 萨基·迪恩·马哈茂德：在英国开设第一家咖喱餐厅并引入洗发服务
• 弗洛伦斯·南丁格尔：现代护理学的创始人
• 埃米琳·潘克赫斯特：妇女参政运动领袖
• 鲁德亚德·吉卜林：出生于印度的作家和诗人。其作品反映了大英帝国是善的力量这一理念。
• 乔治和罗伯特·史蒂文森：著名的铁路机车先驱
• 圣科伦巴与圣奥古斯丁：领导来自罗马的传教士。
• 弗朗西斯·德雷克爵士：伊丽莎白时代的航海家，曾协助击败西班牙无敌舰队，并后来环游世界。
• 胡格诺派：在1720年之前因受迫害而定居英格兰的法国新教徒
• 亨利八世：以结婚六次和脱离罗马教会以便离婚而闻名。在他的统治下，威尔士与英格兰联合。妻子（按顺序）：(1) 阿拉贡的凯瑟琳 (2) 安妮·博林 (3) 简·西摩 (4) 克莱沃斯的安妮 (5) 凯瑟琳·霍华德 (6) 凯瑟琳·帕尔
• 玛格丽特·撒切尔是首位女性首相，也是20世纪任职时间最长的首相。
• 亚历山大·弗莱明：苏格兰医生，发现了青霉素（1928年）
• 克莱门特·艾德礼：丘吉尔的副首相。于1945年成为首相。将主要行业国有化并建立了国民医疗服务体系。
• 玛丽·彼得斯：奥运会运动员，促进了北爱尔兰的运动和旅游业。
• 罗尔德·达尔：威尔士作家（《查理和巧克力工厂》及《乔治的神奇魔药》）。
• 阿瑟·柯南·道尔爵士：苏格兰作家（《福尔摩斯探案集》）
